quotes.quote: NGC5236 (M83) : This galaxy is possibly one of the best cases of the present study. obtained O abundances for 49 regions, with low scattering, which combined with data from 23 regions previously observed shows a clear change of slope associated to a step at 5.5 arcmin (8 kpc). There are very discrepant determinations of the corotation radius for this object.

To make a choice, we must first examine some aspects of the morphology of this galaxy. 1-) The rotation curve is flat to large distances, which indicates that the galaxy is not disturbed by any strong external influence; 2-) A seminal paper of Elmegreen95 hereafter E E Elmegreen95 shows that almost all spiral galaxies have a well defined 2-arms structure up to a distance of 0.5 R25. We see the end of this structure at about 3 arcmin from the centre (also determined by E E for this galaxy), which places R25 and the corotation radius at about 6 arcmin from the center.

sources.summary: The paper presents M 83 as a barred grand-design spiral with an extended H I disk, molecular central region, strong star formation, and measured gas and dynamical masses.

quotes.quote: M 83 is one of the most spectacular nearby galaxies, possessing a face-on, grand-design spiral arm system that is anchored by a large-scale bar. Its beautiful symmetry and prominent spiral arms has earned the moniker, the Southern Pinwheel Galaxy . It is also notable for its extended H i disk, spanning more than a degree along its major axis (Tilanus Allen 1993; Koribalski et al. 2004; Koribalski 2008), and its central nuclear region that is almost exclusively molecular gas (Crosthwaite et al. 2002; Lundgren et al. 2008).

The neutral hydrogen gas reservoir of M 83 extends 1 degree (80 kpc) in length, containing a HIPASS-measured total of 8.3 10 ^9 M _ of H i (Koribalski et al. 2004). Another 4 10 ^9 M _ of molecular hydrogen resides in the central interior and bar of M 83 (Lundgren et al. 2004 and 2008, for a distance of 4.66 Mpc). The total dynamical mass is estimated to be 6 10 ^ 10 M _ (Lundgren et al. 2008) and 8 10 ^ 10 M _ (Crosthwaite et al. 2002).

sources.summary: The paper uses HST imaging of M83 to study cluster age distributions across its differing environments, finding strong position-dependent variation tied to the galactic environment.

quotes.quote: In order to empirically determine the timescale and environmental dependence of stellar cluster disruption, we have undertaken an analysis of the unprecedented multi-pointing (seven), multi-wavelength (U, B, V, H , and I) Hubble Space Telescope imaging survey of the nearby, face-on spiral galaxy M83. The images are used to locate stellar clusters and stellar associations throughout the galaxy. We constructed the largest catalog of stellar clusters and associations in this galaxy to-date, with 1800 sources with masses above 5000 and ages younger than 300 Myr.

We chose the galaxy M83 because it presents such differences in environment, allowing us to test these assumptions see e.g. for differences in gas densities across M83 lundgren04 . In the present work we study the age distributions of clusters in the nearby 4.5 Mpc, thim03 , face-on spiral galaxy M83, based on seven pointings observed with the Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3) onboard the Hubble Space Telescope (HST). Here, we extend the B11 and B12 analysis by including five further WFC3 pointings of M83.

sources.summary: The paper studies M83's molecular gas, dust, and star formation with Herschel SPIRE FTS, emphasizing its nearby grand-design, starburst, gas-rich spiral nature.

quotes.quote: We investigate the physical properties of the molecular and ionized gas, and their relationship to the star formation and dust properties in M83, based on submillimeter imaging spectroscopy from within the central 3.5' ( 4 \ kpc in diameter) around the starburst nucleus.

Motivated by the previous results, we study the properties of molecular gas and dust, and their relationships with star formation, in M83 (NGC5236) with the SPIRE FTS. M83, the so--called Southern Pinwheel galaxy, is the grand-design galaxy closest to the Milky-Way. Its face-on orientation ( 24^ , Comte:1981tw ), starburst nucleus, active star formation along the arms, and prominent dust lanes Elmegreen1998 make it one of the most intensely studied galaxies in the nearby Universe. spatially resolved studies of this galaxy in a wide range of wavelengths, e.g. radio Maddox:2006ey , CO Crosthwaite:2002cr, Lundgren2004a , infrared Vogler2005, Rubin:2007ig , optical Calzetti2004, Dopita:2010fe, Hong:2011ch , ultraviolet Boissier2005 , and X-ray Soria:2002dn , reveal intense star--forming regions in its nucleus and spiral arms. In the past century, at least six supernova events have been detected in M83, making it one of the most active nearby starburst galaxies. Indeed, M83 has been found to be a molecular gas--rich galaxy compared to the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper studies M83's massive stellar content, describing it as a nearby face-on galaxy with high H luminosity, many historical supernovae, and young massive stars.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: centered on the nucleus of M83 (J2000.0: = 13:37:00.9, = - 29:51:56)

quotes.quote: We aim to continue to characterize the massive stellar content of nearby galaxies with high star formation rates starting with M83. M83 lies outside of the Local Group at a distance of 4.8 Mpc 28.4 mag, her08,rad11 . It is a face-on galaxy classified as SAB(s) dev91 with the fifth-highest H luminosity in the local 11 Mpc ^3 volume log L_ H = 41.25, ken08 .

M83 has also been host to six historical SNe, five of which have been identified as massive star or core-collapse: the Type IIP SN 1923A lam23,ros88 , the unclassified SN 1945B lil90 , the Type II SN 1950B har50,wei86 , another Type II SN1957D kow71,wei86 , the Type IIP SN 1968L ben68,bar79 , and the Type Ib SN 1983N tho83,por87 . The high H luminosity coupled with the copious amount of historical SNe and SN remnants bla12,bla14 imply that M83 has a large population of massive young stars. These stars evolve relatively quickly, meaning there may also be a large number of dusty evolved massive stars that are prominent in the high-quality Spitzer archival images.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 83 as the giant spiral center of a Centaurus subgroup at about 4.9 Mpc, around which the survey searched for faint dwarf candidates.

quotes.quote: The Centaurus group has a pronounced bimodal structure, consisting of a larger aggregate centered on the massive peculiar galaxy NGC 5128 = Centaurus A at a mean distance of 3.8 Mpc, and a smaller galaxy concentration around the giant spiral NGC 5236 = M 83 at a mean distance of 4.9 Mpc 2002A&A...385...21K, 2014AJ....147...13K, 2015ApJ...802L..25T, 2015AJ....149..171T . In the following, we refer to the main concentrations as the Cen A and M 83 subgroup, respectively, the whole complex as the Centaurus group, or also Centaurus A group, as it is known in the literature up to the present.

We imaged an area of 60 square degrees (0.3 Mpc ^2 ) around the M 83 subgroup with the wide-field Dark Energy Camera (DECam) at the CTIO 4 m Blanco telescope in g and down to a limiting surface brightness of _r 30 mag arcsec ^ 2 . Various image-filtering techniques were applied to the DECam data to enhance the visibility of extremely low-surface brightness objects. We report the discovery of 16 new dwarf galaxy candidates in the direction of the M 83 subgroup, roughly doubling the number of known dwarfs in that region. The photometric properties of the candidates, when compared to those of the Local Group, suggest membership in the M 83 subgroup.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes M83 as a barred spiral with magnetic and material arms, finding high correlations for total magnetic fields but displaced ordered magnetic arms and larger magnetic pitch angles.

quotes.quote: The nearby barred galaxy M83 provides an outstanding example of a spiral pattern seen in tracers of gas and magnetic field. We analyse the spatial distribution of magnetic fields in M83 and their relation to the material spiral arms. Isotropic and anisotropic wavelet transforms are used to decompose the images of M83 in various tracers to quantify structures in a range of scales from 0.2 to 10 .

The magnetic field configuration in M83 contains pronounced magnetic arms. Some of them are displaced from the corresponding material arms, while others overlap with the material arms. The pitch angles of the magnetic and material spiral structures are generally similar.

sources.summary: The paper presents M83 as a nearby face-on grand-design spiral with a starburst nucleus, active star formation, dust lanes, multiple recent supernovae, high metallicity, and an extended disk.

quotes.quote: M83 (NGC 5236) is a classic grand-design SAB(s) spiral galaxy with a starburst nucleus, active star formation along the arms, and prominent dust lanes elmegreen98 . It has played host to six recorded supernovae (SNe) in the past century, ranking behind only NGC 6946 (with nine) and the far more distant NGC 3690 and NGC 4303 (seven each) barbon99 . At a distance of 4.61 Mpc saha06 , M83 is close enough to be studied effectively with current generations of telescopes (1 = 22 pc), and it is nearly face-on. Hence, M83 arguably provides the most comprehensive view of any galaxy where such active star formation and destruction are taking place.

The integrated effects of this active star formation process are manifest through the generally high metallicity and the chemical abundance gradients measured by spectroscopy of \ regions across the 10 \ diameter bright optical disk bresolin02 , pilyugin06a , pilyugin10 , bresolin16 . A fainter and much more extended disk is seen in \ and in GALEX ultraviolet imaging data huchtmeier81 , thilker05 , bigiel10 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Messier 83 as a resolved nearby spiral, analyzing HST WFC3 point-source photometry across its nucleus, spiral arm, and interarm regions to assess color-based source classification.

quotes.quote: This work analyzes a ten-band photometric catalog of nearly 70 000 point sources in a 7.3 square arcminute region of the nearby spiral galaxy Messier 83, made as part of the Early Release Science program with the Hubble Space Telescope's Wide Field Camera 3. Colour distributions were measured for both broad-band and broad-and-narrow-band colours; colours made from broad bands with large wavelength differences generally had broader distributions although B-V was an exception.

The dataset used for this study is the Wide-Field Camera-3 (WFC3) Early Release Science (ERS) observations of the nearby spiral galaxy Messier 83 (M83). M83 is a grand-design spiral of type SAB, located at a distance of 4.66 Mpc tully13 and the largest member of the M83 subgroup of the nearby Centaurus group of galaxies tully15 . It has a complex nuclear region mast06,thatte00 and has hosted 6 supernovae in the past century stockdale06 .
They cover a 3.6 3.6  kpc ^2 region in the northern part of the galaxy, including the nucleus, a portion of a spiral arm and an interarm region.

sources.summary: The poster describes M83 as a nearby barred spiral and reports Gemini GMOS spectroscopy of planetary nebula candidates to study its chemical enrichment and abundance gradients.

quotes.quote: The M83 barred galaxy is a relatively nearby galaxy, which allows the spectrophotometric study of its photoionized objects. In the literature, HII regions have been extensively explored in this galaxy, though its PNe population has not previously been addressed. In this work we report, for the first time, a spectrophotometric study of the PNe population in the M83 galaxy.

The galaxy M83 (or NGC 5236), known as Southern Pinwheel Galaxy, is a barred spiral (SAB (s)). M83 is located approximately 4.59 Mpc away from us in the direction of the Hydra constellation. In the literature there is a vast content regarding the HII regions in this galaxy on different subjects, including the determination of the radial gradient of oxygen abundance for M83 Bresolin .

sources.summary: The paper studies resolved stellar populations in M83’s extended ultraviolet disk, using HST fields to constrain the outer-disk IMF and finding evidence for high-mass-star deficiency.

quotes.quote: Using Hubble Space Telescope ACS/WFC data we present the photometry and spatial distribution of resolved stellar populations of four fields within the extended ultraviolet disk (XUV disk) of M83. These observations show a clumpy distribution of main-sequence stars and a mostly smooth distribution of red giant branch stars. We constrain the upper-end of the initial mass function (IMF) in the outer disk using the detected population of main-sequence stars and an assumed constant star formation rate (SFR) over the last 300 Myr.

In this work, we study star formation in the extreme outer disk of M83, a nearby grand design spiral with an extended \ disk bigiel+10a,heald+16 . This galaxy is a prototype extended UV (XUV) disk galaxy; its outer disk exhibits high-mass star formation readily detected in the UV by GALEX but faint at most other wavelengths thilker+07,Thilker:2005eh,thilker+05,gildepaz+05 . M83 is also a prototype for galaxies with radially truncated star formation, evidenced by a sharp decline in azimuthally-averaged \ emission mk01,thilker+05 , which traces the most massive ionising stars.

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 as an external barred-galaxy CMZ comparison, noting its similar nuclear gas properties, much higher nuclear star formation rate, and possible maximum in a burst/quench cycle.

quotes.quote: Recently, Callanan et al. (submitted) have obtained ALMA observations in the innermost 500 pc of the barred galaxy M83 at a resolution scale of 10 pc. The nuclear regions of M83 presents gas properties (e.g. gas content, velocity dispersion, metallicity) similar to those of the Milky Way's CMZ see also Israel01, Gazak+14 . However, while our CMZ is currently producing stars at a rate lower than what expected from density-dependent star formation laws (SFR 0.1 ), the centre of M83 is actively star forming SFR 2.5 , Muraoka+07 .

The factor of 25 difference in SFR might be explained by the fact that the CMZs of the Milky Way and M83 are undergoing different phases of their star formation cycles. In this scenario, the CMZ of the Milky Way would be currently near a minimum of a burst/quench cycle, while the CMZ of M83 would be near a maximum. Thus the gas morphology in the centre of M83, and its level of clumping compared to that seen in the Milky Way, represents a useful test of our model.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5236 among the best CTA candidates, notes a reported Fermi LAT detection signature, and includes it in the top-six predicted spectra.

quotes.quote: We identify galaxies of the highest chance of detection by CTA, including NGC 5236, M33, NGC 6946, and IC 342.

Note that M33 has been detected by Fermi LAT as mentioned above, and Ackermann2012 reported a signature of detection ( TS 15 ) for NGC 5236. Other galaxies have not yet been detected by Fermi LAT, which is consistent with our model prediction. Table tab:calculated-flux presents gamma-ray fluxes ( E_ dF_ /dE_ at 1 TeV) predicted by our model for the galaxies considered in this paper, in descending order when _ inj =2.2 . The spectra of the top six galaxies (NGC 5236, M33, NGC 6946, IC 342, NGC 2146, and NGC 7331) are shown in Figure fig:spectrum_examples .
There is a good chance of detection for NGC 5236, M33, NGC 6946, and IC 342.

sources.summary: The paper presents a synoptic nova survey of M83, estimating its nova rate, luminosity-specific nova rate, disk-dominated nova distribution, and faster, brighter nova light curves relative to M31.

quotes.quote: The results of the first synoptic survey of novae in the barred spiral and starburst galaxy, M83 (NGC 5236), are presented. A total of 19 novae and one background supernova were discovered during the course of a nearly seven-year survey comprised of over 200 individual nights of observation between 2012 December 12 and 2019 March 14. After correcting for the limiting magnitude and the spatial and temporal coverage of the survey, the nova rate in M83 was found to be R=19^ +5 _ 3 yr ^ 1 .

This rate, when normalized to the K band luminosity of the galaxy, yields a luminosity-specific nova rate, _K = 3.0^ +0.9 _ 0.6 10^ 10 yr ^ 1 L_ ,K ^ 1 . The spatial distribution of the novae is found to be more extended than the overall galaxy light suggesting that the observed novae are likely dominated by a disk population. This result is consistent with the observed nova light curves which reveal that the M83 novae are on average more luminous at maximum light and fade faster when compared with novae observed in M31.

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 H II-region data as a star-forming comparison sample, finding PAH ionization-ratio similarities to M51a’s nucleus and similar 7.7 PAH carriers.

quotes.quote: We compared the PAH intensity ratios between the spatially resolved M51a regions in this work and the H ii regions in the star-forming galaxies (SFGs) M83 and M33 as examined in . Although M51a hosts an AGN and therefore the integrated emission features compared to SFGs or resolved regions may differ due to intrinsically different environments e.g. Smith07b, Maragkoudakis2018b , examination of the PAH emission characteristics between the regions in M83 and M33 and M51a's ENRs is analogous. In addition, a comparison of the PAH emission in star-forming regions and the central regions of AGN hosts, such as M51a's NR, can offer insights on the impact of AGN radiation on PAH emission.

In all three PAH intensity ratio distributions, M51a ENRs have distinct distributions exceeding to higher ionization fractions compared to M83 and M33 regions and the NR. A breakdown of ENRs into their SF and ISM components (Fig. fig:ratio-histograms112 , bottom row) reveals that although both the SF and ISM ENRs span similar PAH intensity ratio ranges the tail of the ENRs distribution towards higher values has a higher contribution from the SF regions. The 6.2/11.2 intensity ratio distributions indicate that M51a NR has similar ionization fractions to M83 and M33 regions ( p- values of 0.27 and 0.73 as reported by a K-S test; see Table tab:M51KSTest ). On the other hand, the 7.7/11.2 distributions show that M51a NR and M83's regions have similar ionization fractions ( p- value = 0.14), while the 8.6/11.2 ratio suggests that M51a NR and M33's regions have similar distributions ( p- value = 0.31).

At the same time, the similarity of the M51a NR and M83's region 7.7/6.2 distribution, and the M51a NR and M33's region 8.6/6.2 distribution is indicative that the M51a NR has similar 7.7 PAH emission carriers to M83, and similar 8.6 PAH emission carriers to M33, considering that all three region groups have comparable 6.2 distributions.

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 as a nearby spiral with active cluster formation, rich cluster catalogs, IFU spectroscopy, and clusters spanning 1 Myr to 10 Gyr.

quotes.quote: To demonstrate and apply our search strategies for SMS, we focus on massive star clusters in the nearby galaxies NGC 628 and M83, for the following main reasons: 1) Both of them are spiral galaxies with ``sufficient'' star formation rates, such that young star clusters are still forming in these galaxies. 2) They are relatively well-studied and multi-band cluster catalogs are available. 3) Both galaxies host a large number of star clusters to search for SMS. 4) Spectroscopic IFU observations are also available for these galaxies.
Similarly, the clusters of M83 span an age range of 1 Myr to 10 Gyr with a median age of around 45 Myr and a mass range of 246 M _ to 4.5 10^ 6 with a median mass of around 5.5 10^ 3 .
Both class 1 and class 2 type clusters in the LEGUS classification are indicated as class 1 and the other two classes as class 2 in M83 2015MNRAS.452..246A, 2022A&A...666A..29D .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5236 among four barred spirals with inner and outer CO-to-H2 conversion-factor and DGR measurements, separating center/bar regions from spiral arms and finding higher outer conversion factors and lower outer DGR.

quotes.quote: Finally, we obtained \ and DGR in the inner and outer regions of the following four barred spiral galaxies: IC 342, NGC 3627, NGC 5236, and NGC 6946, as listed in table tab:alpha_DGR_radial . The boundary of 0.2 R_ 25 in each four galaxies is shown in figure fig:R25_Vband . The inclination and the position angle were corrected.

In these galaxies, the inner region includes the center and the bar structure, while the outer region includes the spiral arms. The average and standard deviation of (inner) and DGR(inner) are 0.36 0.08 \ and 0.0199 0.0058 , while (outer) and DGR(outer) are 1.49 0.76 \ and 0.0084 0.0037 , respectively. In each galaxy, while (outer) is 2.3 to 5.3 times larger than (inner), DGR(outer) is 0.3 to 0.6 times smaller than that DGR(inner).

sources.summary: The paper compares barred-galaxy simulations with M83 observations, linking central molecular clouds on x1 and x2 orbits to ring instability, inward cloud migration, and AGN fueling.

quotes.quote: Comparison with Observations In the central region of the nearby barred spiral galaxy M83, recent observations of dense molecular gas by the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA are able to identify individual molecular clouds 2019ApJ...884..100H,2021MNRAS.505.4310C . In these observations, the molecular clouds seem to be distributed along the x 1 and x 2 orbits. In addition, a few clouds are also observed in the inside of the x 2 orbit. Our results suggest that formations of the molecular clouds observed along the x 2 orbit would take place because of self gravitational instability of the ring. We also suggest that a few clouds observed inside the x 2 orbit would rotate originally in the x 2 orbit.
These process would contribute to the AGN activity of M83 2016ApJ...824..107Y .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5236 as a grand-design barred spiral with a nuclear starburst ring, regular and circumnuclear rotation, bar-linked ionized gas, field environment, and high star formation.

quotes.quote: Regarding NGC 5236, it has a grand design barred spiral morphology and hosts a nuclear starburst ring Buta1993, Calzetti2004, Knapen2010 leading to a high total star formation rate SFR 4.2 yr ^ 1 , Leroy2021 . Furthermore, observed that both stars and gas kinematics trace regular rotation along the main star-forming disc as well as a fast-rotating circumnuclear disc of 30 ( 700 pc) in diameter, likely originating from secular processes driven by the galactic bar (as already reported by ). They also detected an extended ionised gas region, which could, among other factors, be attributed to a bar-driven inflow of shocked gas.
On the other hand, NGC 5236 has _ 5 = - 0.1 and so it is a ``field'' galaxy. In support of this definition, we mention that NGC 5236 is present in the catalogue of isolated galaxies of .

This is valid for NGC 3351, NGC 3953, NGC 4579 and NGC 4725, while NGC 1566 and NGC 5236 show higher values of _ SFR , as is expected from the presence of highly star-forming spiral arms and nuclear regions.

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 to demonstrate Cepheid discovery by image subtraction, noting its 4.5 Mpc distance, blending problems, and the need for HST follow-up for accurate distances.

quotes.quote: We demonstrated this by re-analyzing the excellent 8.2 meter VLT data of M83, obtained by Thi03 , using the image subtraction method. Blending must be taken into account in deriving the Cepheid distance to nearby galaxies. For example, at the distance of M83 which is 4.5 Mpc Thi03 , the median seeing of 0.76 of the VLT data corresponds to 17 pc.
In M83, a large fraction of the flux of a blended Cepheid could come from its companions and would result in a significant distance bias.
These additional Cepheids are valuable for determining the PL distance to M83 accurately. However, HST observations are necessary to resolve blending effects.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC5236 as a barred giant spiral whose central starburst has organized UV-bright clusters in a half-ringlet, correlated age propagation, and possible gas inflow along the bar.

quotes.quote: Contrary to the dwarfs, the distribution of the stellar clusters in the center of the giant spiral NGC5236 shows a high level of organization, possibly a reflection of the different dynamical environment (presence of a bar) in the massive galaxy. The UV--bright clusters are distributed along a half--ringlet surrounding the optically--bright nucleus. The distribution of the ages also shows a high level of correlation in the star formation, which appears propagating in the S--N direction and inside--out in the ringlet ( , ). There is a clear peak in the number of clusters in the narrow age range 5--7 Myr, with a handful of older clusters (up to 50 Myr). Although the diffuse population is generally consistent with constant star formation over the past 1 Gyr, the sharp cut--off of the cluster ages around 7 Myr could still be explained with either a recent starburst or very rapid cluster dissolution timescales ( ).

Independently of whether the current starburst in the grand design spiral is confirmed to be younger than 10 Myr, a clear mechanism for the triggering and feeding of the starburst, i.e. gas infall along the main bar, is present in NGC5236 ( ).

sources.summary: The paper studies M83's offset nucleus and starburst arc, finding an age gradient along the arc and no stellar-kinematic evidence for hidden central mass concentrations.

quotes.quote: Stellar kinematics show no evidence of hidden mass concentrations at the centre of M83. We show the clearest evidence yet of an age gradient along the starburst arc and interpret the arc to have formed from orbital motion away from a starforming region in the dust lane. galaxies: star clusters, galaxies: individual (M83), galaxies: spiral The nucleus of M83 is offset from the bulge centre and surrounded by a semicircular starburst arc (Fig. 1).

We see a gradient in the H _2 (2-1 S(1)) gas velocity at the same vicinity as D06a report a gradient in Pa \ (Fig. 3). However, the stellar velocity dispersion shows no peak indicative of a mass concentration; we therefore attribute the gradients to a shock on the inner edge of dust lane, as predicted by Athanassoula92 .

sources.summary: The paper uses a resolved stellar stream in M83 to demonstrate CMD fitting, finding a metal-rich stream and noting AGB stars can constrain its age.

quotes.quote: As an example of this method, we present results from our observations of a prominent stellar stream in M83 at a projected distance of 25 kpc from the galaxy center. Using the Starfish code and Padova models with updated AGB tracks , we fit the CMD of this field to a series of models at different ages. The CMD of the stream and the best-fitting model is shown in Figure Sethfig3 . This model has a peak metallicity of [ Z ] =-0.5, suggesting the stream is quite metal-rich. Furthermore, the AGB stars provide us with the possibility of constraining the age of the stream.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution M 83 observations showing dominant disk rotation, kpc-scale gas inflow to the nucleus, a rapidly rotating nuclear disk, and spiral feeding of the central starburst.

quotes.quote: We present observations of the nearby barred starburst galaxy, M 83 (NGC 5236), with the new Fabry-Perot interferometer \ mounted on the 4.2 meter William Herschel Telescope on La Palma. The unprecedented high resolution observations, of 16 pc/FWHM, of the emitting gas cover the central two kpc of the galaxy. The velocity field displays the dominant disk rotation with signatures of gas inflow from kpc scales down to the nuclear regions. At the inner Inner Lindblad Resonance radius of the main bar and centerd at the dynamical center of the main galaxy disk, a nuclear 5.5 ( 0.9) 10^8 M_ rapidly rotating disk with scale length of 60 20 pc has formed. The nuclear starburst is found in the vicinity as well as inside this nuclear disk, and our observations confirm that gas spirals in from the outer parts to feed the nuclear starburst, giving rise to several star formation events at different epochs, within the central 100 pc radius of M 83.

sources.summary: The paper finds that M 83 is a nearby face-on barred spiral with mostly extended PAH-dominated mid-infrared emission and a faint central source.

quotes.quote: M 83 Figure M83 This near, face-on barred spiral galaxy shows mostly extended emission surrounding a faint central object. The extended emission is dominated by PAHs which account for the majority if the MIR luminosity Vogler05 . The 11.9 m flux measured, 232 mJy, is identical with the 11.9 m flux measured by . Our images, however, show a clear, although faint, central source, while report only fuzzy extended emission. The LW3 filter ( 12-18 ) of show the emission clearly tracing the spiral arms of M83, which is not seen in our image.

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 5236 as a nearby spiral with a rich young cluster population, high current star formation, and evidence for a cluster mass-function cutoff.

quotes.quote: Figure  fig:spirals shows the resulting combined mass function for the two spiral galaxies NGC 5236 and NGC 6946, both of which have high current star formation rates and rich populations of young star clusters. The figure includes objects younger than 200 Myr, where the data are still reasonably complete for M > 10^5 M_ .

The combined star formation rate of NGC 6946 and NGC 5236 is about 5 M_ yr ^ 1 and the sample contains 111 clusters with M > 10^5 M_ and ages younger than 200 Myr. Scaling by the SFR, we would thus expect about 20 clusters in the same mass- and age range in the Milky Way.

Using HST imaging of NGC 5236, Chandar2010a find the MF to be consistent with a cut-off above 10^5 M_ , while Chandar2011 find cut-offs of M^ > 2 10^5 M_ to be consistent with observations of NGC 5194.

sources.summary: The paper reports a sub-threshold LAT excess toward M83, notes a possible blazar association, and lists M83 as a promising future Fermi target.

quotes.quote: We additionally obtain relatively large TS values in the directions of NGC 2146 ( 20 ) and M83 ( 15 ). These excesses do not pass the conventional threshold of TS>25 required to claim a source detection. noted an excess in the vicinity of M83, but determined that the best-fit position was inconsistent with the galaxy location and instead proposed an association with the blazar 2E 3100.

Finally, we predict that several more external galaxies might be detected by the LAT during a 10-year Fermi mission. In particular, the galaxies M33, M83, NGC 3690, NGC 2146, and Arp 220 are interesting targets for further study.

sources.summary: The paper studies XMM-Newton observations of M 83, deriving X-ray source populations and luminosity functions for a nearby face-on barred spiral undergoing star formation.

quotes.quote: We present the results obtained from the analysis of three XMM- Newton observations of M 83. The aims of the paper are studying the X-ray source populations in M 83 and calculating the X-ray luminosity functions of X-ray binaries for different regions of the galaxy.

M 83 (NGC 5236) is a grand-design barred spiral galaxy (SAB(s); deVaucouleurs92 ) located at 4.5 0.3 Mpc from the Milky Way Thim03 . M 83 is oriented nearly face-on ( i=24^ ; Rogstad74 ) and shows a galactic disc spanning 12.9^ 11.5^ ( 17 kpc 15.2 kpc; Wofford11 ). M83 is experiencing a starburst activity with a present-day star formation rate (SFR) of 3-4 M _ yr ^ 1 Boissier05 in three regions: the nuclear region (galactocentric distance d 300 pc; Harris01 ), the inner disc ( 300 pc d 7.5 kpc), and the outer disc ( 7.5 kpc d 20 kpc; Dong08 ).

sources.summary: The paper maps M83 with HST/WFC3 to study dust extinction in H II regions, describing it as a nearby face-on grand-design spiral with a starburst nucleus.

quotes.quote: The starburst galaxy M83 has been imaged in multiple narrow bands targeting hydrogen recombination lines (H and H in the optical, and Pa in the near-IR), as part of the Early Release Science (ERS) observations made by the HST/WFC3 Scientific Oversight Committee (SOC), which enables an unprecedented detailed study of the dust extinction and geometry in the nebular gas of a nearby galaxy. M83 (NGC 5236, a.k.a. the ``Southern Pinwheel Galaxy), is classified as an SAB(s) RC3 , is one of the closest grand design-spirals (4.56 Mpc), is virtually face-on, and has a starburst nucleus, a marked bar, and prominent spiral arms. These properties make it an ideal target for detailed investigations of dust extinction in extragalactic H ii regions.

M83 was mapped with HST/WFC3 in 2009 August, with a series of narrow and broad band filters in the UVIS and IR channels, as part of the ERS program (GO-11360, PI: Robert O'Connell; see Chandar10 for further details of the program and the full list of filters used to observe M83). The observations cover two pointings: the southern pointing targets the nuclear region of M83 and about half of its northeastern arm, and the northern pointing targets an adjacent field of the same area where the southwestern arm winds over.

sources.summary: The paper models its simulated barred spiral on M83, using observed gas, stellar potential, rotation, and bar structure, and notes the galaxy's central starburst produced by bar instability.

quotes.quote: The structure of the galactic disc Our galaxy was modelled on the barred spiral galaxy, M83, with the gas distribution and stellar potential taken from observational results (see below). At 4.5 Mpc away, M83 is a nearby type SABc galaxy and has been observed at various wavelengths to measure its atomic HuchtmeierBohnenstengel1981 and molecular gas Lundgren2004a, Lundgren2004b, Sakamoto2004, Muraoka2007 as well as optical emission lines Dopita2010 and X-ray SoriaWu2003 . Stellar potential We used 10^5 fixed-motion star particles to create a stellar potential model in keeping with the observed global characteristics of the stellar distribution in M83. These global galactic structures are consistent with the ^ 12 CO (J=1-0) image of M83 Lundgren2004a , with the bar-end at 2.3 \ kpc . In M83, this bar instability causes a starburst in the central region at radius < 300pc Harris2001 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 as the observed barred-spiral model for its simulation, comparing prior M83 bar and spiral-arm star-formation efficiencies and adopting its gas distribution and stellar pattern speed.

quotes.quote: In two-dimensional models of the barred galaxy M83, Nimori2012 found that the SFE in the bar region was 60 percent of the spiral arm due to the strong internal turbulence of the clouds. Conversely, more recent 3D models of the same galaxy performed by Fujimoto2014 , found that the typical internal cloud velocity dispersion showed little variation between clouds forming in the bar, spiral arm and disc. This implied shear might not be the key to understanding the varying star formation rate.

Our galaxy was modelled on the nearby barred spiral galaxy, M83, with the gas distribution and stellar potential taken from observational results. For the galaxy's radial gas distribution, we assumed an initial exponential density profile based on the observations of Lundgren2004 and used fixed-motion star particles to create a stellar potential with the observed pattern speed for M83.

sources.summary: The survey finds M 83's nuclear molecular gas is rich in shock and grain-mantle species, lacks H recombination and PDR tracers, and appears comparatively shock dominated.

quotes.quote: The molecular gas in the nuclear region of M 83 is rich in some shock tracers and species formed in dust grains, such as HNCO and CH _3 OH (Fig. abundances and Table ratios ), that are easily released through grain mantle evaporation by shock waves. Although the abundances of these two species are high relative to C ^ 18 O ( 10^ 3 and 10^ 2 for HNCO and CH _3 OH respectively), other shock and dense gas tracers are just marginally detected (e.g. SiO, CH _3 CN, and SO). No H recombination lines were detected in M 83, unlike in the more active starbursts (see SFR in Table galaxies ) M 82 and NGC 253, in spite of all three galaxies having similar beam filling factors. This suggests that big and medium-size HII regions and OB star clusters are relatively scarce in M 83. Accordingly, the PDR-tracers -C _3 H _2 and HCO were not detected.

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 as a comparison starburst with nearly simultaneous X-ray constraints; its brightest sources resemble NGC 253 sources but include fewer ULXs, and one variable ULX shapes its spectrum.

quotes.quote: More recently, we have executed joint \ and / \ observations of M83 (Yukita \ in-preparation) and Arp 299 (Ptak \ 2014), galaxies with sSFRs that are comparable to and 20 times higher than that of the MW, respectively. The brightest \ sources in M83 have \ colors similar to those of NGC 253, albeit with fewer ULXs.
For comparison, we also provide best-fit models for M83 (Yukita \ in-preparation) and NGC 253 (Wik \ 2014a), which were also constrained using nearly simultaneous observations with / \ and .

For M83, it appears that the spectral slope in this regime is somewhat steeper than it is for the other three galaxies. As discussed by Yukita \ (in-preparation), the spectral shape of M83 is strongly influenced by a single variable ULX (see also Soria \ 2012 for details on this source).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5236 (M83) as the barred THINGS velocity-map test case, fitting its H I velocity map and comparing bar effects on shear recovery.

quotes.quote: Two objects were fitted from THINGS; NGC 3621 with morphological type SA(s)d, and NGC 5236 (M83) with morphological type SAB(s). The morphology of these two choices was of particular relevance, as it allowed for the effect of bars on the accuracy of the fit to be investigated. The HI emission and velocity map of NGC 3621 are shown in Figures fig:NGC3621_0 and fig:NGC3621_1 , and those of NGC5236 are shown in Figures fig:NGC5236_0 and fig:NGC5236_1 . In both cases the mask radius is 12 pixels and is shown by the black line. Two velocity maps were fitted from THINGS; one barred (NGC 5236) and one not barred (NGC 3621).
This is roughly 1/3 of the radio extent for both NGC 3621 and NGC 5236.

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 to show that young star cluster mass functions favor a Schechter truncation and that cluster formation efficiency declines with galactocentric distance.

quotes.quote: A similar conclusion has been reached studying the YSC population in the disk of the nearby spiral galaxy M83 ( ). The YSC population of M83 is better reproduced by a Schechter mass function with 2 10^5 . A Schechter function is also favoured when analysing the mass function of the M83 YSC population as a function of galactocentric distances.

The left panel of Fig. fig2 reproduces the decline observed in as a function of galactocentric distances in M83. The decline obtained from two different sets of cluster ages and SFR indicators (see inset in the figure) follows quite closely the drop in gas surface density, (H_2) , reinforcing the idea that the gas pressure (density) plays an important role in the efficiency of the cluster formation process.

sources.summary: The paper reuses Spitzer H II-region measurements for the face-on spiral M83, treating its Ne/S values as upper limits because of high metallicity and low ionization.

quotes.quote: Previously we had taken measurements of emission lines in 24 H ii regions in the substantially face-on spiral galaxy M83 (Rubin et al. 2007, hereafter R07) that we observed with Spitzer and of 25 H ii regions in the Local Group spiral galaxy M33 (Rubin et al. 2008, hereafter R08), also observed with Spitzer . Here we combine our measurements and derived quantities from those studies with Spitzer data from 9 H ii regions in the Local Group dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 6822 in order to more fully examine trends that resulted from our previous analysis.

As such, we surmised that our values for Ne/S for M83 were in fact upper limits. Due to its relatively low ionization, M83 would have larger contributions from S ^+ , and less from S ^ ++ and S ^ 3+ . We suspected (R07) this is to blame for the large Ne/S values inferred from the H ii regions in M83.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes M 83 as a nearby grand-design late-type spiral, using CO, gas, and star-formation measurements with NGC 3627 and NGC 5055 to study KS-relation variation.

quotes.quote: All three galaxies are nearby late-type galaxies with grand design (NGC 3627 and M 83) or flocculent (NGC 5055) spiral structures Elmegreen:1987kq . They belong to a similar population of galaxies in terms of stellar mass and SFR plane (Figure fig:MSFR ) but NGC 3627 seems to have lower metallicity than the other two galaxies (Figure fig:MZ ). We use archival data to estimate gas and SFR surface densities. The spatial resolutions of the data are 15 arcsec for NGC 3627 (700 pc at the distance of the galaxy) and NGC 5055 (570 pc), and 25 arcsec for M 83 (550 pc). The inclination corrected spatial resolutions are 892 pc for NGC 3627, 819 pc for NGC 5055, and 575 pc for M 83, which are larger than the boundary spatial resolutions at which the KS relation breaks 80-250 pc, Momose:2010fj,Verley:2010rt,Onodera:2010uq .

sources.summary: The paper confirms two new dwarf satellites within M 83's virial radius and examines the subgroup spatial distribution and possible satellite plane around M 83.

quotes.quote: The nearby Centaurus group, with its two subgroups centered on Cen A and M 83, stands out as an important alternative to the Local Group for scrutinizing cosmological model predictions in a group of galaxies context. We have obtained deep optical images of three recently discovered M 83 satellite galaxy candidates with the FORS2 instrument mounted on the Very Large Telescope. We confirm the two candidates, dw1335-29 and dw1340-30, to be dwarf satellites of the M 83 subgroup, with estimated distances of 5.03 0.24 Mpc and 5.06 0.24 Mpc, respectively.

Both dw1335-29 and dw1340-30 lie inside the virial radius of M 83 (210 kpc; 2015A&A...583A..79M ) with a 3 dimensional separation of 130 kpc and 180 kpc to M 83, respectively, making them satellites of the M 83 subgroup and members of the Centaurus group . As there is strong evidence that the Cen A subgroup hosts a plane of satellites 2015ApJ...802L..25T,2016A&A...595A.119M one may wonder if such an arrangement is found also in the M 83 subgroup.

sources.summary: The paper includes M83 among the six nearby star-forming MALATANG targets mapped in HCN and HCO+ over the central 2 arcmin region.

quotes.quote: Keck Observatory, 65-1120 Mamalahoa Hwy, Kamuela, HI 96743, USA Ying-He Zhao Yunnan Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Kunming 650011, China Ming Zhu National Astronomical Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing 100012, China We present \ and \ maps of six nearby star-forming galaxies, NGC 253, NGC 1068, IC 342, M82, M83, and NGC 6946, obtained with the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope as part of the MALATANG survey. All galaxies were mapped in the central 2 2 region at 14 (FWHM) resolution (corresponding to linear scales of 0.2-1.0 kpc).

In this work we present new mapping observations of six nearby star-forming galaxies, NGC 253, NGC 1068, IC 342, M82, M83, NGC 6946, in the \ lines of HCN and HCO ^+ . These observations were completed in the early stages of the MALATANG (Mapping the dense molecular gas in the strongest star-forming galaxies; Zhang et al. 2018, in prep.) survey with the James Clerk Maxwell Telescope (JCMT). In the MALATANG survey, we select these six targets to be mapped in the central regions because of their broad distribution, strong emission lines of molecular gas, and concentrated star-formation activity in the nuclear regions. Table tab:galaxies outlines some of the basic physical properties of these six galaxies. These are the first \ and \ spatially resolved observations toward the central 2 2 region (i.e., 2-9 kpc) of these nearby galaxies to date.

sources.summary: The paper includes M83 as a central group galaxy lens candidate, adopting group distance, impact parameter, and halo mass for the GW 170817 time-delay calculation.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies are the most plausible for large time delays: NGC 5084, M104, M83, and NGC 5128 (Centaurus A). The later two of these are the central galaxies in groups and all have dynamical halo mass measurements. karachentsev07 compute tip of the red-giant branch distances and the halo masses for the NGC 5128 and M83 groups.

They find a mean distance of 3.76 0.05 Mpc for the NGC 5128 group (for an impact parameter of 1.31 0.02 Mpc) and 4.79 0.1 Mpc for the M83 group (impact parameter of 0.74 0.02 Mpc). For the the M83 group, they find (0.8--0.9) 10^ 12 M_ , which we convert to a log _ 10 (M/M_ ) measurement of 11.93 0.02 .

sources.summary: The paper uses an M83 ALMA GMC catalog and finds its GMC masses are consistent with a power law, with only weak evidence for a physical cutoff.

quotes.quote: M83: A catalog of 873 GMCs was published by Freeman17 , based on ALMA CO(1-0) observations from project 2012.1.00762.S (PI: A. Hirota). They assume a _ CO value of 4.35 M _ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 ) ^ 1 . The catalog covers the nuclear starburst and parts of the northern region.f

For M83 and NGC 3627, the 3 and 2 contours respectively, remain open up to the maximum tested value for M_* . In these cases, the GMC masses are also consistent with being drawn from a pure power law (not excluded at >3 significance), but with some weak evidence for a physical cutoff.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 83 as a face-on grand-design barred spiral with central starburst activity, gas funneled by the bar, and measured stellar, dust, SFR, and H I content.

quotes.quote: M 83 (NGC 5236, Fig. subfig:m83_maps ) is a grand-design spiral galaxy, with a strong bar in the centre and prominent dust lanes connecting the central region to the disc. M 83 is an SAB(s) ( T=5 ) galaxy as classified by 1995yCat.7155....0D , representing a `typical' nearby grand-design Sb-Sc galaxy, and is located at a distance of 7.0 Mpc 2010PASP..122.1397S . It has a nearly face-on orientation, with an estimated inclination of 19 .5 . The nuclear region is a site of strong starburst activity 1980ApJ...235..392T, 1987ApJ...313..644T , with dynamical studies showing that gas is funnelled along the bar producing high rates of star formation at the centre 2010MNRAS.408..797K . According to 2019A&A...624A..80N , M 83 has a stellar mass, dust mass, and integrated SFR of 3 10^ 10 M _ , 2 10^7 M _ , and 6.7 M _ yr ^ 1 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper models young massive clusters in M83, using its tidal field, observed cluster population, and molecular-gas-based two-disk potential to infer initial cluster masses and radii.

quotes.quote: We account for these processes by constructing detailed N body models of young massive clusters in the tidal environment of the nearby spiral galaxy M83. We then present a method to compare those models to the present-day population of clusters in that galaxy to extract the true initial properties of the cluster population, particularly the distribution of masses and radii. We have chosen to use M83 as our test case for this method because of the quality of the data on the young cluster population that exists in the literature, and because we have a good understanding of the galactic environment as well.

The tidal field of M83 is taken from Lundgren04 , who use the kinematics of molecular gas in M83 to estimate that the galaxy's potential is best represent by two exponential disks. The more massive disk has a mass of 5.9 10^ 10 M_ and a scale radius of 2.7 kpc, while the less massive disk has a mass of 3.0 10^8 M_ and a scale radius of 0.05 kpc.

sources.summary: M83 is discussed as a nearby barred spiral whose nuclear region hosts a circumnuclear starburst; its nucleus has the highest sample N/O and agrees with optical estimates.

quotes.quote: M83 is a nearby spiral galaxy with a prominent bar. At present, only the central regions are mapped in the [ O iii ] 52 m line by SOFIA/FIFI-LS, so only the nuclear region of M83 is discussed here. M83 hosts a circumnuclear starburst, which displays a burst age gradient along the star-forming arc spanning from 6 to 8 Myr 2008MNRAS.385.1110H,2010MNRAS.408..797K .
The N/O of different types of galaxies also cluster together: those of dwarf galaxies are systematically lower than LIRGs, and M83 nucleus has the highest value.
For NGC 4214, NGC 2146 and M83 nucleus, the far-IR derived and optical derived N/O abundance ratios agree within the 1 error.

sources.summary: The paper maps atomic carbon in M83, comparing it with CO, H I, and infrared emission, and finds carbon less reliable than CO for tracing cold molecular gas in the disk.

quotes.quote: To evaluate the suitability of C I as the tracer, we performed (^3P_1-^3P_0) (hereinafter ) mapping observations of the northern part of the nearby spiral galaxy M83 with the ASTE telescope and compared the distributions of with CO lines ( CO (1--0), CO (3--2), and CO 13 (1--0)), H I , and infrared (IR) emission (70, 160, and 250 m). The distribution in the central region is similar to that of the CO lines, whereas in the arm region is distributed outside the CO. We examined the dust temperature, T_ dust , and dust mass surface density, _ dust , by fitting the IR continuum-spectrum distribution with a single temperature modified blackbody. The distribution of _ dust shows a much better consistency with the integrated intensity of CO (1--0) than with that of , indicating that CO (1--0) is a good tracer of the cold molecular gas.

M83 is an ideal target to investigate the - CO relation for the galactic structures, because it is one of the nearest (4.5 Mpc, ) spiral galaxies that is nearly face-on (inclination= 24D ; ) and hosts prominent galactic structures (a bar and spiral arms).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5236 as an Sbc spiral in a loose group with excluded SZ-map contaminants and a significant individual correlation in the hot-halo analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 5236 (M 83): This Sbc galaxy is located in a loose group of at least seven significant galaxies and about 1.5 Mpc (in projection) to the north of NGC 5128. It is often assigned as part of the NGC 5128 group. In the SZ maps, there are a few background clusters and foreground residuals that were excluded.

The six objects with the most significant correlations are NGC 253 (chance probability or p-value of 5.9 ), NGC 3031 (p = 9.2 ), NGC 5236 (p = 1.3 ), and three objects with p < 0.2 : NGC 891, NGC 4826, and NGC 5055.
These are followed by NGC 5055 (1.7 or 2.12 ) and two galaxies with S/N 1.5 : NGC 253 (5.9 ); and NGC 5236 (7.6 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 observations as a comparison case, noting region-based ionized and atomic metallicities, standout nuclear data points, gas conversion, and a possible nuclear-starburst explanation.

quotes.quote: For all these galaxies but M83 the values of Z_ atomic and Z_ ionised are the average values over the entire galaxies. For galaxy M83, the reported metallicities of both gas phases are derived in individual regions in the galaxy.

The four observational data points that stand out in the relation correspond to the four regions in the 200 pc nuclear region of M83. Hernandez12-2021ApJ...908..226H discusses that the efficient conversion of atomic to molecular gas and hence the depletion of the \ gas in the nuclear region of M83 is responsible for the relatively high atomic gas metallicity in that region. However, the presence of an ongoing starburst in the nuclear region of M83 Dopita10-2010ApJ...710..964D, Wofford11-2011ApJ...727..100W might be the cause for the significant difference between the metallicities with < .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5236 (M83) as the fiducial TYPHOON galaxy, rebins its datacube from 39 to 940 pc, and tracks how visible spiral-arm and H-emission structure degrades.

quotes.quote: We select NGC 5236 (M83) as our fiducial galaxy in this study, using high resolution spectroscopic data from the TYPHOON survey Poetrodjojo+19 .
For NGC 5236 at a distance of 4.89 Mpc Leroy+19 , an angular size of 1.''65 corresponds to a physical pixel size of 39 pc.

To illustrate what features of the galaxy are visible, in Figure fig:postage_stamps we show H images for NGC 5236 at each resolution explored. At the native resolution, individual regions can be seen as ``bubbles" along the spiral arms of this galaxy, and regions of low H emission in the inter-arm regions are visibly resolvable. As f is increased, structural details of this galaxy are lost.

sources.summary: The paper compares the adopted extinction for NGC 5236 with SWS results and uses CVF-simulated maps to recover saturated LW2 and LW3 nuclear fluxes.

quotes.quote: NGC 5236 - Gen98 report in the SWS 14 20 aperture an extinction of 5 mag, larger than the value adopted here. The ratio [ N ii ] 6583 /(H + [ N ii ] 6583 ) 0.3 in the central 5 Kee84, Ver86 . The nucleus is saturated in the ISOCAM LW2 and LW3 maps, more severely for LW3. We thus used maps simulated from the CVF spectral cube to measure the LW2 and LW3 fluxes. We estimate in this way that the missing flux fractions due to saturation are 11 and 34 respectively, assuming that the effects discussed in Sect. Sub-diagn_PAH_VSG , making photometry from CVFs uncertain by 10--20 , are negligible.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5236 (M83) as a nearby spiral with a rich young-cluster population likely tied to high star formation, including clusters near its nuclear starburst and disk.

quotes.quote: Using new data from the UVES spectrograph on the ESO Very Large Telescope and archive images from the Hubble Space Telescope, we have measured projected velocity dispersions and structural parameters for two bright young star clusters in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 5236 . One cluster is located near the nuclear starburst of NGC 5236, at a projected distance of 440 pc from the centre, while the other is located in the disk of the galaxy at a projected galactocentric distance of 2.3 kpc.

In this paper we analyse two massive young clusters in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 5236 (M83). In our ground-based survey of young massive clusters in nearby spirals (Larsen Richtler ), NGC 5236 was noted as having a very rich population of such clusters, most likely because of its high star formation rate.

We adopt a distance modulus for M83 of (m-M)_0 = 28.25 0.15 ( 4.5 0.3 Mpc) from Thim et al. \ ( ), corresponding to a pixel scale on the WF chips of the Wide Field Planetary Camera 2 on board HST of 2.2 pc pixel ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reports preliminary planetary-nebula velocities in M83, detects the galaxy rotation, removes the rotation component, and finds randomized residual velocities.

quotes.quote: The top frame of Fig. 1 shows the preliminary radial velocities of 203 PNe in M83 after correcting for the systemic and barycentric velocities. PNe with the largest velocities away from us are clustered in the lower left of the frame, while PNe with the largest velocities toward us are in the upper right. Clearly, we are detecting the rotation of the galaxy.

The rotation component is eliminated by subtracting out v_ rot i where v_ rot is taken from studies of H I gas (Tilanus Allen 1993). The resulting residual velocities are random with position in the galaxy, indicating that the rotation has been taken out.

sources.summary: The paper takes NGC 5236 cluster data from L02, describes HST/WFPC2 selection and photometry, and finds its young-cluster luminosity function is well approximated by a power law.

quotes.quote: NGC 5236 and NGC 6946 Data for these two galaxies was taken from L02 and we refer to that paper for details about the data reduction and analysis. Briefly, clusters were identified on V band equivalent (F555W and F606W) HST/WFPC2 images and aperture photometry was carried out using a 3 pixels aperture. Cluster candidates were confirmed by a visual inspection of the images. For NGC 5236 and NGC 6946 there were 1 and 3 HST/WFPC2 pointings available, corresponding to 7.1 kpc ^2 /53 kpc ^2 , respectively.
The LF of the SMC, the LMC and NGC 5236 are characterized by a power-law behavior N L L^ L , with a mean exponent of < > = 2.0 0.2 .

sources.summary: The paper reports silicate absorption and mid-infrared PAH emission in NGC 5236, then models its dust reradiation and attributes a 40 percent infrared contribution to an AGN component.

quotes.quote: A deep feature at 10 \ indicates strong absorption by silicates, particularly for in NGC 253, IC 342, M 82, NGC 5236, and NGC 5253. For NGC 5236 and NGC 7552 there is also evidence of PAH emission in the mid-IR.

Fig. 5 shows that the IR reradiation by dust of NGC 253, IC 342, NGC 3256, NGC 3690, NGC 5236, and NGC 7552 can be also modelled by a unique model corresponding to a AGN with \ = 1000 , 0 \ = 1000 3, and a power-law flux with log \ = 11 (model m7) as was found in Arp 220 (Contini et al. 2004, Fig. A1).
An AGN contributes to the IR by 23 in NGC 253 and IC 342, by 40 in NGC 3256, NGC 3690, and NGC 5236.

sources.summary: The paper studies H-alpha emission-line profiles for 157 H II regions in central M 83, emphasizing instrumental-response correction and a luminosity-velocity dispersion upper envelope.

quotes.quote: We present the integrated \ emission line profile for 157 \ regions in the central 3.4' 3.4' of the galaxy M 83 (NGC 5236). Using the Fabry-Perot interferometer , on the 4.2 m William Herschel on La Palma, we show the importance of a good characterization of the instrumental response function for the study of line profile shapes. The luminosity-velocity dispersion relation is also studied, and in the (L)- ( ) plane we do not find a linear relation, but an upper envelope with equation (L_ H )=0.9 ( )+38.1 . For the adopted distance of 4.5 Mpc, the upper envelope appears at the luminosity L=10^ 38.5 , in full agreement with previous studies of other galaxies, reinforcing the idea of using \ regions as standard candles.

sources.summary: The paper treats M83 as a large grand-design spiral requiring blank-sky background estimation and compares its arm and interarm soft X-ray/K-band ratios, finding differences of no more than 30%.

quotes.quote: For galaxies significantly smaller than the field of view we estimated the background components using a combination of a number of regions away from galaxies. The angular size of four galaxies (M51, M74, M81, M83) exceeds or is comparable with the extent of the combined Chandra image, therefore we used the ACIS ``blank-sky'' files http://cxc.harvard.edu/contrib/maxim/acisbg/ to estimate the background.

Four galaxies in our sample (M51, M74 , M81, and M83) have grand design spiral arms allowing us to measure L_X/L_K ratios of spiral arms and interarm regions separately, obtained values are given in Table tab:lxlk . The arm and interarm regions were separated based on optical and near-infrared images. To our surprise, we did not find large difference between spirals arms and interarm regions, their L_X/L_K ratios being different by no more than 30 .

sources.summary: The review describes M83 outer-disk star formation as occurring in unstable islands and spiral arms, with steep local KS behavior and a very long H I consumption time beyond 1.5R25.

quotes.quote: found unstable islands of star formation in the outer part of M83. In these regions, the star formation followed a steep KS law from point to point with a slope of about 1.4 dong08 . found that outer disk star formation seen by GALEX follows the HI very well in M83, with a uniform consumption time of 100 Gyr per atom beyond 1.5R_ 25 . The form of the star formation is mostly in spiral arms. Outer disk arms could be spiral waves radiating from the inner disk ba10 .

sources.summary: The paper treats M83 as the most distant galaxy in its comparison sample, an SBc system whose nucleus recently formed young UV-bright stars and whose CMZ contains about 5 x 10^7 solar masses of molecular gas.

quotes.quote: M83: The most distant member of our sample at D = 4.5 Mpc, M83 is an SBc system with M _B 21. Its nucleus recently supported a starburst that produced a UV-bright region of young stars. The M83 CMZ has M _ mol 5 10 ^7  M _ (Muraoka et al. 2009).
For this pilot project we adopted estimates of the SFRs of 0.1 M _  yr ^ 1 for IC 342 and Maffei 2, 0.5-1 M _  yr ^ 1 for M83, and 3-10 M _  yr ^ 1 for NGC 253.

sources.summary: The paper studies star clusters in two HST/WFC3 fields of the nearby spiral galaxy M83, adopting its distance and comparing inner and outer cluster populations.

quotes.quote: Star Cluster Mass and Age Distributions of Two Fields in M83 Based on HST /WFC3 Observations We study star clusters in two fields in the nearby spiral galaxy M83 using broad and narrow band optical imaging taken with the WFC3 on-board HST .

The motivation for this work is three-fold: to empirically determine the age and mass distributions of star clusters in the nearby spiral galaxy M83, to understand how the selection of the clusters affects the results, and to test whether or not clusters are disrupted at a rate that depends on their mass. We use multi-band observations of two fields in M83, taken as part of our program GO-11360, for this purpose.
As before, we assume a distance of 4.5 0.2 Mpc to M83 (Thim et al. \ 2003), corresponding to a distance modulus of (m-M)_0 = 28.28 0.1 , and a pixel scale of 0.876 pc pixel ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 as an outer-disk star-formation example where UV profiles lack the H-alpha edge, while knots and diffuse UV light bear on IMF interpretation.

quotes.quote: The case of M83 is particularly troubling: the distinct edge seen in the \ radial profiles of were not at all apparent in far UV profiles ( ).

This is at apparent odds with the results of who find that the statistics of the number of UV bright knots in the outer disk of M83 with and without \ emission as well as the integrated \ to FUV flux ratio from these knots are consistent with a standard IMF, that is like the bright portion of galaxies. However, in that study only the light in these knots are measured; their method is insensitive to diffusely distributed stars. To square this result with the sharp difference between the integrated \ and FUV surface brightness profiles of M83 noted in the introduction requires that there be considerable UV bright star formation in lower mass clusters or in individual stars. This is consistent with HST imaging of the outer disks of NGC 2915 ( ) and M83 ( ) where the obvious young clusters contain only a small portion of the main-sequence stars.

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA astrochemistry results for M83, describing molecular tracers across its nuclear region, circumnuclear ring, and central orbit.

quotes.quote: (in preparation) observed a nuclear region of a face-on starburst galaxy M83 in multiple molecular tracers.

In these observations, methanol emission is enhanced at the entering point of gas into the circumnuclear ring (Cloud A in Fig fig:m83 d) compared with ^ 13 CO tracing overall molecular gas (Cloud A in Fig fig:m83 ). Slightly downstream in the central orbit, N _2 H ^+ emission is enhanced (Cloud A B in Fig fig:m83 e). Further down in the orbit, there is a peak of CN emission (Cloud B C in Fig fig:m83 f), which could be enhanced by PDRs.

sources.summary: The paper describes M83 as a metal-rich galaxy where super star cluster and blue-supergiant spectroscopy test J-band metallicity methods and support a mass-metallicity relation based on massive stars.

quotes.quote: have pioneered this technique with a J-band metallicity study of two SSC, one in the metal rich galaxy M83 and one in the metal poor spiral NGC 6946.

carried out the ultimate test of the SSC method by investigating a large sample of SSC in M83 and by comparing with metallicities obtained from the optical analysis of single BSG. The close agreement found confirmed that SSC through J-band spectroscopy are excellent tracers of metallicity. They also used their new results on M83 to compare mass-metallicity relationships obtained from RSG and SSC with those from BSG and constructed a new MZR based on the spectroscopy of massive stars.

sources.summary: The paper identifies G4Jy 1081 as M83, a nearby starburst spiral with a double nucleus, extensive H I, and a large-scale magnetic field.

quotes.quote: Another star-forming, spiral galaxy in the G4Jy Sample is G4Jy 1081 (GLEAM J133659 295147). This is more commonly known as M83 (NGC 5236), and is nicknamed the Southern Pinwheel Galaxy (Figure varietyoverlays b). Like G4Jy 86, this nearby starburst is well-studied at multiple wavelengths. For example, sub-arcsecond resolution images in the near-infrared Gallais1991 revealed that the double nucleus at the centre of the galaxy consists of an arc of star clusters offset from the old stellar nucleus. At radio wavelengths, Heald2016 present the extent of neutral hydrogen surrounding the galaxy, as well as its large-scale magnetic field.

sources.summary: The paper studies M83's diffuse soft X-ray emission, finding enhancement along and downstream of spiral arms, super-solar lognormal plasma, dust anti-correlation, and likely multiphase outflows.

quotes.quote: We examine how the coupling may be traced by diffuse soft X-ray emission in M83 -- a nearby face-on spiral galaxy undergoing active star formation, based chiefly on 729 ks Chandra observations. Our main findings are 1) the X-ray emission is enhanced not only along the galaxy's grand spiral arms, but also clearly in their downstreams; 2) the spectrum of the emission can be well characterized by a super-solar metallicity plasma with a lognormal temperature distribution, plus an X-ray absorption of a lognormal column density distribution; 3) the intensity of the emission is strongly anti-correlated with the dust obscuration seen in optical images of the galaxy. These findings suggest A) the morphology of the X-ray emission is likely due to the convolution of the feedback heating of the plasma with its thermal and dynamical evolution; B) the X-ray emission, accounting for 10 of the feedback energy input rate, probably traces only the high-energy tail of the radiation from the plasma; C) a good fraction of the recent star forming regions seems sufficiently energetic to produce multi-phased outflows, likely responsible for much of the dust obscuration and X-ray absorption. Direct confrontation of the findings with theories/simulations could help to understand the underlying astrophysics of the coupling and how the hot plasma shapes the interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5236 as a barred spiral with a likely interacting satellite, complex kinematics, and growing peripheral radial velocity or warp-like signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 5236 NGC 5236 is a barred spiral galaxy with a close satellite (NGC 5253) that likely interacts with it. The kinematic structure of this galaxy is complex, so we limit our analysis to radii of 500'' as on larger radii the velocity field becomes too fluctuating. As R_ 25 380'' , thus the analysis stops beyond the optical disc. The TRM detects an inclination angle i(R) with large variations, while the P.A. has a large fluctuation only at small radii and then flattens to a constant value (see Fig.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes M83 with new HI data plus CO and dust maps, deriving annular CO-to-H2 and gas-to-dust quantities that rise by factors of 2-3 across the disk.

quotes.quote: We analyze the CO-to-H _2 conversion factor ( ) in the nearby barred spiral galaxy M83. We present new HI observations from the JVLA and single-dish GBT in the disk of the galaxy, and combine them with maps of CO(1-0) integrated intensity and dust surface density from the literature. and the gas-to-dust ratio ( ) are simultaneously derived in annuli of 2 kpc width from R = 1-7 kpc. We find that and both increase radially, by a factor of 2-3 from the center to the outskirts of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses M83 as a barred-spiral comparison system where observed satellite counts exceed IllustrisTNG-50 analog predictions, motivating the too-many-satellites framing.

quotes.quote: Recently, müller2024toomany studied the abundance of dwarf galaxies around M83, a barred spiral at D=4.9 Mpc Jacobs2009extragalactic .

M83 has 13 confirmed satellites within a projected separation of 330 kpc down to a limiting magnitude of M_V=-10 mag . M83 analogs in the high-resolution IllustrisTNG-50 simulation, however, demonstrate sparse satellite distributions with luminosity functions inconsistent with that of the M83 system at a 3 confidence level -- thus constituting the so-called 'too-many-satellites' problem.
Thus, the too-many-satellites problem is extended from M83 to across 150 fields in the MATLAS catalogue.

sources.summary: The paper reviews M83 as a barred spiral whose bar-region polarized emission was detected, mapped at 2.8 cm, and found to have regular magnetic fields aligned with and strongest along the bar.

quotes.quote: The first detection of polarized radio emission from the bar region was reported by Ondrechen ( ) for M83, with a mean fractional polarization at 6 cm of 25 . Neininger et al. \ ( ) mapped the polarized emission from M83 at 2.8 cm. They showed that the regular magnetic field in the bar region is aligned with the bar's major axis. Observed with higher resolution, the regular field is strongest at the leading edges of the bar of M83 (Beck \ ).

sources.summary: The paper includes M83 among five nearby spirals studied with HST, derives a globular-cluster candidate sample, and estimates its cluster population and structural profile with some uncertainty.

quotes.quote: In this work we attempt to broadly characterize GC systems in five nearby spirals: M81, M83, NGC 6946, M101, and M51.

For M83, we use a single HST WFPC2 field pointing taken in UVI filters. While this does not provide much coverage beyond the central portions in this galaxy, evidence for an ancient cluster system would be interesting and potentially important for followup observations.

In general, we find that M101, M83, and NGC 6946 (the three latest type galaxies) are best fit by a double exponential profile (although the double nucleus in M83 makes the results of our fit uncertain for this galaxy), and the results of our fits are adopted.

sources.summary: The paper newly detects both formaldehyde transitions in M 83, notes stronger 14.5 GHz optical depth, and compares derived densities with other absorption-line galaxies.

quotes.quote: IC 342, M 82, M 83, IR 15107+0724, and Arp 220 (Figures fig:IC342FormSpec , fig:M82FormSpec , fig:M83FormSpec , fig:IR15107FormSpec , and fig:Arp220FormSpec ) were all detected in both H _2 CO transitions.

M 83: This is a new detection of H _2 CO: both lines are detected in absorption, and the 14.5 GHz line shows a significantly larger optical depth than the 4.8 GHz line. imaged the CO J= 2 1 and 3 2 emission from this barred starburst galaxy, deriving a 45^ 15^ structure.

sources.summary: The paper surveys M83 for planetary nebulae, reports 241 candidates in the six-galaxy sequence, and derives a 4.8 Mpc PNLF distance matching earlier estimates.

quotes.quote: As the first step to determine disk mass-to-light ratios for normal spiral galaxies, we present the results of an imaging survey for planetary nebulae (PNe) in six nearby, face-on systems: IC 342, M74 (NGC 628), M83 (NGC 5236), M94 (NGC 4736), NGC 5068, and NGC 6946. Using Blanco/Mosaic II and WIYN/OPTIC, we identify 165, 153, 241, 150, 19, and 71 PN candidates, respectively, and use the Planetary Nebula Luminosity Function (PNLF) to obtain distances. For M74 and NGC 5068, our distances of 8.6 0.3 Mpc and 5.4^ +0.2 _ 0.4 Mpc are the first reliable estimates to these objects; for IC 342 ( 3.5 0.3 Mpc), M83 ( 4.8 0.1 Mpc), M94 ( 4.4^ +0.1 _ 0.2 Mpc), and NGC 6946 ( 6.1 0.6 Mpc) our values agree well with those in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper uses WFC3 observations of M83, describing it as the nearest massive grand-design spiral, slightly barred, and using its clusters to develop age-estimation methods.

quotes.quote: Our primary target for this study is the spiral galaxy M83. At a distance of 4.5 Mpc (corresponding to a distance modulus of m-M = 28.28 ; Thim et al. \ 2003, and a pixel scale of 0.876 pc pixel ^ 1 ), M83, nicknamed the ``Southern Pinwheel,'' is the nearest massive grand-design spiral galaxy. It is a slightly barred galaxy, with a Hubble type SAB(s) (3RC).

sources.summary: The paper reports both H2CO transitions in absorption toward M 83, with stronger 14.5 GHz optical depth, and cites CO imaging of its barred starburst structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 253, NGC 660, Maffei 2, IC 342 (all in Figure fig:NGC253NGC660Maffei2IC342FormSpec ), NGC 2146, M 82, M 82SW, NGC 3079 (all in Figure fig:NGC2146M82M82SWNGC3079FormSpec ), NGC 3628, IC 860, M 83, IR 15107+0724 (all in Figure fig:NGC3628IC860M83IR15107FormSpec ), Arp 220, and NGC 6946 (both in Figure fig:Arp220NGC6946FormSpec ) were all detected in both H _2 CO transitions.

M 83: Both H _2 CO transitions are detected in absorption, with the 14.5 GHz transition showing a significantly larger optical depth than the 4.8 GHz transition (Figure fig:NGC3628IC860M83IR15107FormSpec ). CO J= 2-1 and 3-2 emission imaging of this barred starburst galaxy Sakamoto2004 indicates a 45^ 15^ structure.

sources.summary: The paper says M 83 has a well-constrained NH3 kinetic-temperature fit and molecular emission extended over a compact elongated structure.

quotes.quote: M 83: The quality of the kinetic temperature fit for this galaxy is good, well constrained by the NH _3 (1,1) and (2,2) transitions detected (Figure fig:M83TkFit ). We are not aware of any previous measurements of NH _3 toward this galaxy. High spatial resolution measurements of dense gas tracers in M 83 see summary in Mangum2013 suggest that the molecular emission extends over a 45^ 15^ structure.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5236 has a strong magnetic-field and star-formation correlation, with a steeper power-law index than NGC 6946, possibly reflecting bar-driven amplification.

quotes.quote: The Spearman's rank correlation for NGC 5236 is 0.80 and for NGC 6946 it is 0.74. The power law index, a , is found to be 0.25 0.05 and 0.18 0.03 for the galaxies NGC 5236 and NGC 6946 respectively. NGC 5236 shows comparatively steeper value of a than NGC 6946, indicating efficient magnetic field amplification, perhaps caused due to bar action.

sources.summary: The paper includes M 83 among high-SFR galaxies in its Spitzer survey, contrasts prior limited photometry with a new five-band catalog containing 23,000 sources, and shows its CMD.

quotes.quote: These catalogs include the highly star-forming galaxies M 83 , NGC 6946 , M 101 and M 51 (M 51 a and M 51 b) which enabled the first-ever identification of extragalactic candidate analogs of the Galactic stellar behemoth Carinae ref:Khan_2015c .

ref:Williams_2015 published a mid-IR bright source catalog of M 83 including Spitzer 3.6 and 4.5 band measurements for <4,000 objects, while the M 83 catalog presented here contains Spitzer 3.6 , 4.5 , 5.8 , 8 and 24 band measurements for 23,000 sources.

Figure fig:cmd1 shows the m_ 4.5 vs. m_ 3.6 m_ 4.5 color magnitude diagram (CMD) for M 31 and Figure fig:cmd4 shows the same for the galaxies M 83 , NGC 6946 , M 101 and M 51 which have the highest SFR among all the galaxies surveyed.

sources.summary: The paper compares M83 inner and outer cluster fields, finding the observed environmental change in median cluster age matches the theoretical disruption relation.

quotes.quote: M83 is divided into the inner and outer fields from Bastian12 to probe the environmental dependence in a homogeneous sample, although the inclusion of M31 already pushes the parameter space covered to extremely low gas surface densities. Before deriving the predicted median ages, we require an estimate of . In Section sec:law , we proposed that the power-law slope between the median cluster age and the minimum present-day cluster mass provides a direct measurement of .

Turning to M83, the magnitude of the environmental dependence is the same in the observations and in the theoretical relation, as the median cluster age drops by a factor of 2.5 when moving from the inner to the outer field. This is consistent with the analysis of Bastian12 , who find that for most mass-cuts, the outer field has a median age that is two to three times higher than the inner field.

sources.summary: The paper describes M 83's outer envelope as unusually extended, smooth, oval, off-centered, and likely a flattened disk-like projection rather than a classical halo.

quotes.quote: M 51 presents a smooth but unusually shaped outer envelope, whereas M 74 and M 101 appear to show the more commonly seen spiral structure. In the case of M 83, the outer envelope is unusual in that it extends substantially beyond the disk, shows no spiral structure even in subtraction, and is oval and off-centred. This outermost structural feature suggests that (as is the case with M 51) its envelope is more likely to be a flattened disk-like projection.

sources.summary: The paper notes evidence that M83 has an enormous northwestern loop and disturbed radio outskirts, possibly caused by a retrograde interaction with a neighboring galaxy.

quotes.quote: A deep photographic image of M83 1997PASA...14...52M has revealed ``an enormous loop around NW quadrant of the galaxy''. Also, radio observations of its outskirts show a significant extent of a disturbed nature, suggesting a possible retrograde interaction . This is possibly due to a closest approach 1--2 Gyr ago of a neighbouring galaxy 2014ccds.book.....F .

sources.summary: The paper treats M83 as a barred galaxy without reported velocity dispersion, uses low n and B/T plus bar evidence, and classifies its bulge as a pseudobulge.

quotes.quote: In addition, the galaxies M83 and NGC5792, lacking of reported velocity dispersion data, do not fit into either of the two bulge categories. These galaxies have n<2 and do follow the KR, but to assign a classification, we considered less established properties of pseudobulges, such as B/T ratio and the presence of bars within the bulge region Fisher-Drory_2008 , since M83 (SAB) and NGC5792 (SB) are barred galaxies and have B/T 0.20 . Hence, our final classification is that the bulges in M83 and NGC5792 are pseudobulges.

sources.summary: The paper gives NGC 5236 a partial HII/LINER classification, cites X-ray evidence for an AGN, and attributes the mixed classification to H II contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 157, NGC 253, and NGC 1808 (all transition, in Table tbl4 ) would be classified as H II regions; NGC 936 and NGC 1380 (LINERs, in Table tbl4 ) would be classified as transition; NGC 5236 (H II region/LINER, in Table tbl4 ) would be classified as transition.
