Two galaxies (NGC 4030 and NGC 5236) were classified as HII regions/LINERs, none with broad components of permitted optical emission lines, and only one of them (NGC 5236) shows X-ray emission characteristic of an AGN. This indicates that NGC 5236 indeed hosts an AGN and this partial classification possibly resulted from contaminations of the nuclear LINER spectrum by the emission from H II regions in the area, which were sufficient to result in this partial classification, but not to change the classification to transition.

sources.summary: The simulations find M83 flux is suppressed in the Prim magnetic-field model, but M83 and Cen A mainly populate HS1 for light nuclei.

quotes.quote: The suppression occurs to the same sources affected by the Prim2R, in addition to M83, NGC 6946, and NGC 660.
HS1 is populated mainly by M83 and Cen A.
When light nuclei are injected (p, He, N), M83 and Cen A generate more than 95 of the events populating the HS1 as shown in figure fig:contribution_HS1 .

sources.summary: The paper includes M83 as a starburst-nucleus galaxy, finds it has the sample’s lowest average CN/HCN ratio, and uses its nucleus in the spiral-galaxy relation comparison.

quotes.quote: Our final sample of 9 galaxies (Table table:physical ) is quite heterogeneous, and includes galaxies with a prominent active galactic nucleus (AGN) or Seyfert nucleus (Circinus, NGC1808, NGC3627, NGC 7469), luminous and ultraluminous infrared galaxies (U/LIRGs: IRAS 13120-5453, NGC3256, VV114), galaxies with a starburst nucleus (M83, NGC 1808), and spiral galaxies (NGC3627, NGC3351).

Comparing the individual mean ratios for each galaxy shows a full range of only a factor of 2.5 (0.40 in log space), with M83 having the lowest average CN/HCN line ratio and IRAS 13120-5453 the highest ratio (Table table:ratios ).

Even the nuclei of the more normal spiral galaxies (M83, NGC 3627, NGC 3351) lie along the same relation, which provides some additional support for our choice to use the ULIRG CO-to-H _2 conversion factor for these regions.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5236 among large spirals but flags it as an exception because its low q is surprising for such a massive galaxy.

quotes.quote: We also included two galaxies NGC 4190 and UGC 8833 from the VLA ACS Nearby Galaxy Survey Treasury (VLA ANGST) survey ott.etal.2012 . Table 1 shows the sample of 20 galaxies, of which first 8 are relatively large, massive spirals (NGC 0628, NGC 3184, NGC 4736, NGC 5194, NGC 5236 and NGC 6946), and the remaining 12 are smaller spiral, dwarf or irregular galaxies. The procedure for determining the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks as a function of radius is described in ianjamasimanana12 and ianjamasimanana.etal.2017 as well as in das.etal.2020 . We basically co-added the individual velocity profiles over radial bins to obtain azimuthally averaged, high signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) velocity dispersion values for each radial bin. The stacked profiles were fitted with single Gaussian functions, where the half width of the fitted Gaussian represents the velocity dispersion of the gas. This method gives a better estimate of the velocity dispersion compared to the moment2 maps. Figure 4 in the appendix shows the radial variation of the velocity dispersion across the galaxy disks. Its value is less than 10 kms ^ 1 at the R _ 25 radius, except for a few cases (NGC 5194, NGC 4190, DDO 187 and IC 10), where _ H i lies between 11 and 14 kms ^ 1 .

Figure 2 and Table 2 show that galaxies with more massive stellar masses (M(*) > 10 ^9 M _ ) have q values close to q 1 at R_ 25 and beyond (e.g. NGC 628 and NGC 6946). The exceptions are NGC 4214 and NGC 5236. Of these two galaxies, NGC 4214 is a relatively small galaxy and close to being a dwarf olling.1996 . In fact, based on its rotation velocity, stellar mass, and absolute B-band magnitude values, NGC 4214 has been classified as a dwarf galaxy by 2008AJ....136.2782L . But NGC 5236 is quite massive ( M(*) 6.9 10^ 10 M_ and so its low q value is quite surprising. For the less massive galaxies, a large fraction of which are LSB dwarfs, q is much smaller (Figure 2 and table 2). Six of them have q=0.2-0.3 (Holmberg1, M81dwfA, IC10, DDO75, DDO187 and UGC8833) and 3 of them have q=0.4-0.5 (Holmberg2, F564-V3, DDO46). The exception is IC1613 which has a relatively large q 1 value at R_ 25 .

sources.summary: The paper describes M 83 as a barred spiral where a density wave separated dust, atomic gas, and star-forming tracers, revealing large-scale photodissociation morphology.

quotes.quote: Spiral structure In the Introduction to this paper I pointed out that it was thanks to a strong density wave in the southern barred spiral M 83 that the importance of photodissociation in affecting the morphology of galaxies on the large scale was first unmasked. Other studies have followed on M83 and on other galaxies (M 51, M 100; see for references) and have generally agreed that the initial interpretation in terms of photodissociation remains the most viable option. The separation in the case of M 83 is about 250 pc, and arises because of the difference between the spiral pattern speed and the rotation speed of the gas, coupled with the time for collapse of GMCs and the time that a massive young star lives on the main sequence.

gmw_00000025.name: NGC 4579
gmw_00000025.overview: NGC 4579 is a barred spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2010.04005, arXiv:2404.11656] that hosts an active galactic nucleus classified as a LINER and Seyfert 1.9 [arXiv:2010.04005, arXiv:1503.00280]. The galaxy features a stellar bar driving a nuclear molecular spiral [arXiv:0705.4162], alongside a radio-loud jet that shocks the surrounding gas [arXiv:2312.01936].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4579 as an active barred galaxy with a nuclear molecular spiral, non-circular motions, regular stellar rotation, and complex ionised-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 is an active galaxy classified as S1.9/L1.9 Ho3 . It hosts a NIR stellar bar of 9 kpc of diameter, oriented along PA  =58 . observed this galaxy with the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (CO) as part of the NUGA survey. They found a nuclear molecular spiral from R 1  kpc down to 200  pc, driven by the stellar bar and detected highly non-circular motions over the spiral arms. They interpreted these perturbations as outflow motions.

The stellar continuum isophotes are rather round in the centre and slightly elongated along a PA of 60 corresponding to the orientation of the stellar bar. The stellar velocity field presents a very regular rotation pattern, with a kinematic major-axis oriented at a PA of 95 . The stellar velocity dispersion rises regularly towards the centre.

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 4579 as a nearby LLAGN, finds variability and apparent stability over a sparse short light curve, and treats its timescale constraint cautiously.

quotes.quote: Six LLAGNs, Centaurus A (hereafter Cen A), NGC 4374, NGC 4552, NGC 4579, NGC 4278, and NGC 5077, were selected. These sources are predicted to have variability timescales ranging from a few days to ten days, due to their black hole mass being situated between Sgr A* and M87. This is expected to provide clarity on the sub-millimeter mass scaling relationship.

We accumulate four years of observations using Submillimeter Array for Centaurus A, NGC 4374, NGC 4278, and NGC 5077 and one year of observations for NGC 4552 and NGC 4579. All sources are variable. We measure the characteristic timescale at which the variability is saturated by modeling these sources' light curve as a damped random walk. We detect a timescale for all the sources except NGC 4552. The detected timescales are comparable to the orbital timescale at the event horizon scale for most sources.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4579 as a Milky Way analog with limited HI extent, moderate HI deficiency, LINER activity, and high dark matter halo mass despite acceptable sample-fit properties.

quotes.quote: We note that NGC 4579 and NGC 4698, both well within the selection criteria that resulted in our MW analog sample, have some peculiarities that are worth mentioning. In the case of NGC 4579, the HI extension is quite limited, not reaching R _ 25 . This is most likely due to the moderately HI-deficiency ( def_ HI 1 ), as confirmed by 2009AJ....138.1741C . For MW analog NGC 4698, a short tail signature in the HI (see Figure  fig:plot_Barolo ) may indicate a recent history of a minor merger, also suggested by 2009AJ....138.1741C . We also note that these galaxies have been identified as hosting AGN activity, both of them being classified as LINER galaxies 2000ApJ...532..323P,2003ApJ...594..704G . However, given that these galaxies fit well within our sample selection criteria ( subsec:sample criteria ) and that their RCs, DM halo masses, nor concentration parameters stand as outliers, we do not exclude them from our study.

However, our most HI-poor galaxies (NGC 4579 and NGC 4698) indeed exhibit higher DM halo masses compared to the rest of the sample. The confirmed presence of an AGN in these two galaxies e.g., 2000ApJ...532..323P,2003ApJ...594..704G could explain this finding, with the AGN impacting the HI content e.g., 1998A&A...331L...1S, 2006MNRAS.365...11C . We indeed observe this general trend within our sample (see Table  tab:percentiles ), with the exception of NGC 4579. This is likely associated with the spatially-limited HI extension of this system, associated with it being an HI-deficient galaxy e.g., 2009AJ....138.1741C .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4579 as a LINER/Seyfert galaxy with bar-driven molecular spiral arcs, little gas at the central engine, lopsided inner structure, and kinematics not favoring direct AGN feeding.

quotes.quote: The molecular gas distribution in the central 1 kpc of NGC 4579 suggests that the gas flow responds to the 9 kpc-diameter stellar bar identified in all the NIR images of this galaxy (e.g. Jarret et al. ). The ^ 12 CO(2--1) map of Fig. fig:feeding-4579 a reveals a mass of 3.2 10 ^ 8 M _ of molecular gas piled up in two highly contrasted spiral arcs. The CO lanes lie at the leading edges of the stellar bar, which is oriented along PA=58 ^ . The northern spiral is more continuous and better delineated than its southern counterpart; it is also very well correlated with the red lane seen North in the V--I color HST image of the galaxy, shown in Fig. fig:feeding-4579 a . There is little molecular gas at < 100 pc distance from the central engine of NGC 4579: the closest gas complex of 10 ^ 6 M _ lies East of the nucleus at 150 pc (complex E in Fig. fig:feeding-4579 a, b ).

This indicates that the m=2 point-symmetry of the gas flow driven by the bar of NGC 4579 breaks up at < 200 pc and lets lopsidedness take over. An independent confirmation of this picture comes from the new HST image of the nuclear region of NGC 4579 obtained with the ACS camera at 3300 ; this image resolves the central disk into a winding m=1 spiral instability that mimics a ring (Contini ). The kinematics of molecular gas in the central 1 kpc of NGC 4579 are characterized by the presence of highly non-circular motions detected over the spiral arms and most notably over the central disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4579 as an anemic barred galaxy with low gas and star formation, gas concentrated in bar-end spiral arms, a truncated disk, and high molecular fraction.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 (Fig. fig:plot_n4579 ): is the second anemic galaxy in our sample. As in NGC 4548 it has a prominent bar and a low overall low gas surface density and star formation rate. The highest gas surface densities are found in two spiral arm departing from the end of the bar. In the smoothed image this leads to a quasi ring structure of the gas distribution. The gas distribution is truncated at the optical radius.

Despite the low total gas surface density the molecular fraction is as high as in a ``normal'' galaxy like NGC 4321. The star formation rate is proportional to the molecular gas surface density with a scatter of 0.3 dex.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4579 as a Virgo LINER/Seyfert spiral with hard X-ray and radio AGN signatures, a stellar bar, pseudo-ring atomic gas, molecular spiral arms, and inward gas transport down to 50 pc.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 (Messier 58) is a SAB(rs)b galaxy classified as an intermediate type 1 object (LINER/Seyfert 1.9) by ho97 at a distance of 20 Mpc in the Virgo cluster. It also has an unresolved nuclear hard X-ray (variable) source with a prominent broad Fe K line terashima00,ho01,eracleous02,dewangan04 . A non-thermal radio continuum source is detected at the position of the AGN hummel87,ho01b,ulvestad01,krips07 . The NIR K-band image of NGC 4579 has revealed a large-scale stellar bar and a weak nuclear oval santi09 . The 21 cm \ line observations have shown that the atomic gas in NGC 4579 is currently piling up in a pseudo-ring (radius of 40 , 4 kpc) formed by two winding spiral arms that are morphologically decoupled from the bar structure haan08 .

Molecular gas in the inner 2 kpc disk is distributed in two spiral arms, an outer arc and a central lopsided disk-like structure santi09 . The derived gravity torque budget in NGC 4579 have shown that inward gas flow is occurring on different spatial scales in the disk, with clear smoking gun evidence of inward gas transport down to 50 pc.

sources.summary: NGC 4579 is described as a face-on Virgo barred spiral with a LINER/Seyfert nucleus; its bar region hosts old stellar populations and lacks neutral and molecular gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 ( (J2000) = 12:37:43.5 and (J2000) = + 11:49:05) (also known as Messier 58) is a barred spiral galaxy belonging to the Virgo cluster. The galaxy is seen nearly face-on, as shown in Figure figure:fig1 . This galaxy belongs to the cluster in the identification of barred galaxies and environment by vandenberg_2002 . The galaxy is known to have a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) nucleus with broad wings in the H emission-line profile Stauffer_1982, Keel_1983, Filippenko_1985 , indicating the presence of an AGN (Seyfert 1.9) at the centre Ho_1997a,Panessa_2002 .

Figure figure:fig4 shows the maps and profiles we created to understand the FUV NUV colour and HI and H _ 2 distribution of NGC 4579. This is very similar to the observations made from NGC 3351: the region along the major axis of the bar hosts stellar populations of age 350 Myr and the nuclear or central sub-kpc region shows an embedded bluer and younger clump of star formation ( 150-250 Myr). When we compare the FUV NUV colour map/profile with HI and H _2 maps/profiles, we see that the circular region without the central nuclear region lacks molecular/neutral hydrogen and star formation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4579 as a barred LLAGN/LINER-Seyfert spiral in Virgo; gas transport may feed star formation and AGN, while gas deficiency likely reflects ram pressure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 is classified as an intermediate LINER/Seyfert 1.9 spiral galaxy, according to the classification of . Subsequent works, e.g., , considered this object a representative example of a LLAGN in which several mechanisms can co-operate to transport the gas from the outer disc to the central regions (spiral arms, bar, viscosity), thus feeding star formation and possibly the AGN activity itself. More recently, this galaxy has been studied in terms of variability timescales: found a lack of variability in the X-ray emission and a broad Fe K line. They argued for an altered accretion geometry in LLAGN, compared to luminous AGN.

According to , NGC 4579 is one of the brightest galaxies in the Virgo Cluster. It is an anemic galaxy and has a relatively small amount of neutral hydrogen, located inside its disk and concentrated in clumps Cayatte1990 . This deficiency of gas content is thought to be a consequence of interactions with the Virgo Cluster's intracluster medium, suggesting that ram-pressure stripping may have occurred in the galaxy's outskirts Koopmann2004 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4579 as a dusty Sa barlens galaxy with fine color-map structure, star-forming regions aligned with the disk but associated with the barlens, and a small red central peak.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 (D): this is an Sa galaxy at inc = 41.6 ^ . This is an example of a dusty barlens galaxies. Particularly in the (g-) color map a lot of fine structure appears inside the barlens. The star forming regions inside the barlens are oriented along the disk, but they are much more elongated than the outer disk, meaning that they are clearly associated to the barlens morphology.

In the (i-z) color profile there is a small red central peak. The minor-axis profile further illustrates that the red color is associated to the whole barlens, which color steadily turns bluer from the galaxy center toward the edge of the barlens.

sources.summary: The paper finds that Messier 58 hosts a radio-loud AGN whose jet cocoon shocks molecular and ionized gas in the inner disk, heating gas and likely suppressing star formation.

quotes.quote: Spitzer spectral maps reveal a disk of highly luminous, warm ( >150 K) H _2 in the center of the massive spiral galaxy Messier 58, which hosts a radio-loud AGN. The inner 2.6 kpc of the galaxy appears to be overrun by shocks from the radio jet cocoon. Gemini NIRI imaging of the H _2 1-0 S(1) emission line, ALMA CO 2-1, and HST multiband imagery indicate that much of the molecular gas is shocked in-situ, corresponding to lanes of dusty molecular gas that spiral towards the galaxy nucleus.

Here, we report similarly spectacular radio jet feedback in nearby spiral galaxy M58. M58 (NGC 4579) resides in the Virgo Cluster at z=0.00506. It is a massive, star-forming galaxy, with a mass in stars of 1.5 10^ 11 M_ and a dust-corrected star-formation rate of 0.9 M_ yr ^ 1 2019ApJ...872...16D,2019ApJ...878...74D .

sources.summary: The paper includes M 58 among five galaxies with multiple spiral modes, consistent corotation-resonance distributions, available rotation curves, and short winding times.

quotes.quote: To investigate this aspect, we focus on five objects from our dataset that could have multiple spiral modes: IC 342, M 58, M 74, NGC 3583 and NGC 5371. The R_c distributions of these galaxies have consistent measurements and demonstrate the evident existence of several corotation resonances. In addition, the rotation velocity profiles for these objects are also publicly available.

Subsequently, we obtained rotation curve data v_c(R) for these five objects from the following sources:  Crosthwaite2000 (IC 342),  Lang2020 (M 58),  Walter2008 (M 74),  Noordermeer2005 (NGC 3583),  Sanders1996 (NGC 5371). Analogous phenomena have been observed in other selected galaxies as well. The figure illustrates that, for all the galaxies in our sample, the winding up process of the spiral structure takes approximately two or three galactic years.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4579 has soft diffuse emission matching its star-forming ring, a very bright piled-up nucleus, and stable hard X-ray flux across observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 shows soft diffuse emission with a similar morphology to the circumnuclear star forming ring, in addition to a very bright nucleus (see also Eracleous et al. 2002). The pileup effect for the three objects with the largest count rate per frame (NGC 4203, NGC 4579, and NGC 5033) is serious and we did not attempt detailed spectral fits.

NGC 4579 (L1.9/S1.9) . --- A result on the same data set is presented by Ho et al. (2001). The nucleus is significantly piled up in the observation. The hard band image is dominated by the nucleus and no bright source is seen in the field. Therefore, we used fluxes observed in 1995 and 1998.

sources.summary: The paper fits XMM-Newton spectra of NGC 4579, finding complex iron K emission with a narrow 6.4 keV line and broad ionized component consistent with a truncated disk.

quotes.quote: We report on spectroscopy of the low-luminosity active galaxies (LLAGN) M81 and NGC 4579 both of which have known black hole masses and well-sampled spectral energy distributions (SED). The iron K line profiles from both the LLAGN can be described in terms of two components -- a narrow line at 6.4 and a moderately broad line (FWHM 2 10^ 4 ) arising from highly ionized, He-like or H-like species ( E 6.8 ). We interpret the broad lines arising from an accretion disk the inner edge of which is restricted to large radii ( r_ in 100 r_g ).

NGC 4579 We fit the PN and MOS2 spectrum of NGC 4579 jointly with a power-law model modified by photoelectric absorption. This fit resulted in a ^2 of 296.6 for 253 degrees of freedom (dof). The best-fit absorption column density is consistent with the Galactic column density ( N_ H =3.1 10^ 20  cm^ 2 along the line of sight of NGC 4579.

sources.summary: The paper finds elongated 347-GHz emission in NGC 4579 consistent with CO morphology and reports significant submillimetre variability, implying a substantial direct core contribution.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, in the JCMT measurements at 347 GHz, we found significant elongated features in NGC 4258 (Doi et al. in preparation), NGC 4579, and NGC 5866, and a very diffuse structure in NGC 3147 (Fig. figure2 ). These features are consistent with the morphology of CO emissions that had been imaged with millimetric interferometers (NGC 4258, Helfer_etal.2003 ; NGC 4579, Sofue_etal.2003 ), with dust lane along the major axis of the host galaxy (NGC 5866, e.g., Malhotra_etal.2000 ), or with morphology of a low-frequency radio image (NGC 3147, Hummel_etal.1985 ). It is, therefore, plausible that most of the 347-GHz emission from these sources originates in stellar processes of star-forming regions, probably dust emission, associated with their host galaxies.

NGC 4374, NGC 4472, NGC 4579, and NGC 5866 had been observed at multiple epochs with the JCMT. A significant variability was seen in NGC 4579: 85.7 23.8 mJy beam ^ 1 on 2002 January 16 by jiggle-map mode and 25.8 4.9 mJy beam ^ 1 on 2001 March 15 by photometry mode. The significant fraction of observed submillimetre fluxes may be direct emission of the core.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4579 as a Type I LINER newly observed by Swift, with no significant long-timescale variability and only a few intra-orbit X-ray variations.

quotes.quote: Our sample includes the brightest LINERs in the UV sample, M81, NGC 3998, NGC 4203, and NGC 4579, that are all Type I LINERs (i.e., with detected broad H components) according to . Data for two of these sources, M81 and NCG 4203 were already available in the Swift archive: M81 (net exposure of 33.7 ks) has an X-ray Telescope spectrum of excellent quality and even the suggestion of an Fe K line; NGC 4203 (5.3 ks) has a good XRT spectrum, although slightly under exposed with respect to M81. NGC 3998 and NGC 4579 were observed with Swift for the first time (27.4 and 20.8 ks, respectively).

NGC 4579 presents few significant intra-orbit variations of 20--30 . However, no variations are detected on longer time scales: the suggestion of a coherent increase of 50 in the first part of our monitoring in soft X-rays is not significant (Fig. liners:fig:xlc_all f). After host galaxy subtraction, we do not detect significant optical/UV variability in any of our sources.

sources.summary: The paper assesses M58 as a marginal phase-reference candidate near M87 because VLBI data constrain its radio spectrum but predict millimeter fluxes near EHT sensitivity limits.

quotes.quote: The final candidate we show in Figure fig:M87SED is M58, again with the radio SED obtained from the literature Hees-Wade:64,Sram:75,Dres-Cond:78,Greg-Cond:91,Beck-Whit-Edwa:91,Whit-Beck-Helf-Greg:97,Naga-Wils-Falc:01,Ulve-Ho:01,Cond-Cott-Brod:02,Voll-Thie-Wiel:04,Ande-Ulve-Ho:04,Naga-Falc-Wils:05,Dale_etal:05,Doi_etal:05,Gall_etal:06,Krip_etal:07 . Separated by 1.78^ from M87, M58 is the further than M84 and M89, though still well within the isoplanatic angles at the relevant EHT stations on good nights. Unlike M84 and M89, sufficient VLBI observations exist for M58 to completely characterize its radio spectrum, from 1.7 through 43 Ande-Ulve-Ho:04,Naga-Falc-Wils:05,Krip_etal:07 . These allow a reasonably precise estimate of the mm-VLBI flux densities, giving values comparable to the sensitivity limits of the EHT-C at 230 , and well below them along the Hawaii--CARMA baseline at 345 . As a consequence, this is a marginal phase reference candidate.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4579 as an anemic Virgo spiral with a LINER/Seyfert nucleus, UV and line-emitting filaments, cold molecular spiral arms, and central warm molecular emission.

quotes.quote: 4579 is an SAB(rs)b galaxy with a LINER/Seyfert 1.9 nucleus Ho1997 . As in the case of 4569, this galaxy is considered an `anemic spiral' of the Virgo cluster. HST UV, \ and \ images show a bright nuclear point source surrounded by filamentary structures, with the brightest emission located at distances 2'' towards the NE Maoz1995, Pogge2000 . High-resolution CO intensity maps presented by showed that most of the cold molecular gas in 4579 is located in a two spiral arm structure that can be traced from 10'' down to a distance of 2 \ from the nucleus. The spiral arms lie at the leading edges of the 12 kpc-diameter bar identified in K band images e.g., Knapen2003 and are well correlated with dust lanes revealed by V-I HST images Pogge2000 , especially the arm located towards the north.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4579 as a giant barred Virgo Sa galaxy with a Seyfert 2 nucleus, symmetric high-surface-brightness radio disk, high polarization, and regular magnetic field.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 This giant Virgo Sa galaxy has a prominent bar and was classified as anemic by Cayatte et al. (1994). It has a well-known Seyfert 2 nucleus, with radio jets (Contini 2004). Star formation takes place only in a narrow ( 20'' ) ring structure at the end of the bar. Nevertheless, NGC 4579 has a high surface brightness radio continuum disk which is symmetric. The spectral index in the disk region is quite shallow ( 0.6 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 4579 as a strongly barred Virgo spiral with an active nucleus, possible outer gas stripping, compact radio jets, and asymmetric double-peaked molecular emission.

quotes.quote: 4579 4579 is a strongly barred spiral [ SAB(rs)b, deVaucouleurs1991 ] in the Virgo Cluster; there is some evidence for possible ram-pressure stripping of the outer gas disc, though the H i kinematics is regular e.g. Chung2009 and the effects are nowhere near as extensive as in the case of 4569. It harbours an active nucleus classified as LINER or Seyfert 1.9 by Ho1997 ; HST spectroscopy shows clear broad-line H emission e.g. Barth2001c and there is evidence for compact radio jets emanating from the nucleus see, e.g. Ulvestad2001a . In Fig. f_n4579_im , we show log-spaced isophotes from a Spitzer IRAC1 image from SINGS Kennicutt2003a , along with a close-up of the central regions using the WHT-INGRID K band image of Knapen2003 . From the outer isophotes in the IRAC1 image, we measure a PA for the LON of 95 and an ellipticity of 0.22; the latter implies an inclination of 40 . These estimates agree well with the kinematic analysis of the H velocity field by Chemin2006a , who found PA = 91 3 and i = 44 8 .

sources.summary: NGC 4579 resembles NGC 3031 and NGC 1097 in broad double-peaked Balmer lines; the adopted SED model uses a small transition radius and disfavours jet X-rays.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 NGC 4579 shares many characteristics with NGC 3031 (M81) and NGC 1097: its nucleus features broad double-peaked Balmer emission lines Barth01 and a lack of the iron K line emission Eracleous02 . From the width of the broad H line, Barth01 obtained a rough estimate of the inner radius of the line-emitting portion of the accretion disk r_ tr 160 . Hence, we adopt in our SED models r_ tr = 150 and i=45^ . The resulting thin disk spectrum peaks in the IR and its high-frequency tail extends into the optical-UV; however, the available IR upper limits and HST optical-UV data do not allow us to set strong constraints on the thin disk emission. A jet origin for the X-rays is disfavored by the hardness of the spectrum.

sources.summary: NGC 4579 is described as an Sb galaxy with low spiral amplitude for its rotation speed and gas-stellar arm offsets that help explain discrepant optical pitch angles.

quotes.quote: Figure 4 is particularly suggestive in that 11/13 of the galaxies appear to broadly follow a trend of increasing amplitude of spiral features with increasing rotation velocity; the low amplitude of spiral features for the two objects with the largest rotational velocities (the Sb galaxies NGC 4579 and NGC 2841) are notably discrepant.

The two galaxies that are most discrepant (NGC 3031 (Sab) and NGC 4579 (Sb)) are notably those that were shown in Paper I to exhibit the most convincing offsets between gas shocks and stellar density maxima. The sense of the offset (gas shock precedes stellar density maximum inward of corotation) combined with the fact that one would expect star formation to follow the shock after a fixed time (and therefore at an angular offset that decreases with radius for a differentially rotating galaxy) are both such as to explain smaller pitch angles in the H emission (assuming this traces recent star formation).

In the most discrepant galaxies (NGC 3031 and NGC 4579) this difference in pitch angles can be explained by our previous demonstration (Kendall et al. 2008, 2011) that in these galaxies the spiral pattern traced at 8 m in shocked gas is significantly displaced upstream with respect to the NIR (stellar) arms. In a differentially rotating galaxy it would then be expected that the spiral traced by star formation indicators should be more tightly wound than the stellar arms.

sources.summary: The paper reports three flux-binned spectra for NGC 4579 and fits an absorbed power law plus hot gas with photon index decreasing as luminosity rises.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 Three flux-binned spectra were produced from the available Swift data of NGC 4486. We find an absorbed powerlaw and a hot gas component to be a good fit ( ^2_R = 1.00 ). Previous studies also required an absorbed powerlaw and a hot gas component Gonzalez-Martin2009,Dewangan2004 . We find a range of photon indices very similar to those found by previous studies ( = 1.7 - 2.0 ). The photon index decreases with increasing luminosity in this model.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4579 as a barred Virgo member with peculiar inner CO velocities, central noncircular motions, and radial reversals suggestive of corotation-related gas dynamics.

quotes.quote: Among the barred galaxies, NGC 4579 exhibits notably peculiar CO (1-0) velocities with a large value of in the innermost ring. The high fraction of noncircular motions appears to be related to a blueshifted velocity component in the galactic center, as evident in the moment 1 image in figure fig:mom1 . Similar peculiar motions of gas can also be seen in the velocity field of ionized gas traced by [ O III ] in the central R<20 Dum07 , as well as within R<30 of the interferometric CO (1-0) data in Sof03 .

Figure fig:noncirc (f) indicates that NGC 4303 and NGC 4579 may have two reversals, one just beyond the bar end and the other farther out in the spiral arms. Since both galaxies exhibit v_r<0 between the first and second reversal radii, this region may be inside the corotation of the spiral pattern.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4579's NuSTAR X-ray spectrum with its RIAF model, reproducing a hard power law and attributing longer-wavelength excesses to jets or outer accretion disks.

quotes.quote: Ref. reported a softening feature in the hard X-ray band in NGC 3998, from which they claimed that the electron temperature is 30-40 keV. Our RIAF model can reproduce the softening feature in the NuSTAR band, as well as the Swift BAT data shown in Fig. fig:detail , despite a higher electron temperature. Ref. also provided the X-ray spectrum for NGC 4579, which has a higher m and does not show any softening feature. Our model also produces a hard power-law spectrum consistent with the NuSTAR data (see Fig. fig:detail ). In our RIAF model, the resulting spectra for NGC 3998 and NGC 4579 are relatively hard, and well below the longer wavelength data (radio, infrared/optical/ultraviolet, and soft X-rays). These should be attributed to other emission components, such as compact jets or outer accretion disks . Indeed, radio jets are observed in both objects .

sources.summary: The paper rejects NGC 4579 as BC1’s parent because it is distant in velocity and projection, little disturbed, and too metal-rich for BC1.

quotes.quote: Within the entire 4 deg ^2 region shown around BC1 in Figure fig:BC_NN (top-left), there is only one galaxy that contains \ gas and is sufficiently massive to have formed a BC, NGC 4579. This galaxy is approximately 140 kpc (30 ) to the SW of BC1 and separated from it in velocity by 400 . Thus, BC1 would have needed a large ejection velocity ( > 500 \ in total) for NGC 4579 to be its parent object. Furthermore, other than a slightly deficient disk Chung+2009 define the deficiency of a galaxy's disk as the logarithmic decrement between the observed and expected mean \ surface density within the optical disk, where for the latter they use the average value for isolated galaxies from Haynes+1984 . , NGC 4579 shows little sign of recent disturbance in either its \ or CO morphology and kinematics Chung+2009,Brown+2021 . Finally, NGC 4579 appears to be too metal-rich 12+ ( O/H ) = 8.87 0.05 , DeVis+2019 to match the metallicity of BC1 ( 12+ ( O/H ) = 8.35 0.15 ).

sources.summary: M 58 is described as a barred Seyfert spiral with LOFAR central emission, probable AGN jets, and diffuse disk-scale star-formation emission.

quotes.quote: VCC 1727 (NGC 4579, M 58) The massive barred spiral galaxy M 58 host a Seyfert 2-type low-luminosity AGN Contini2004The4579 . Despite its significant deficiency, it was found to have the radio properties of a normal star-forming galaxy by Vollmer2013 even after subtracting the AGN contribution. In the LOFAR images, a central point source from which swirling features of 5kpc scale extend can be observed. Since their direction is counterclockwise, opposite to the spiral arms, they most likely correspond to the AGN jets. The central structure is embedded in a low-surface brightness emission with a size similar to the optical disk which traces the star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4579 as a barred S1.9/L1.9 galaxy whose inner molecular gas traces bar response, leading lanes, lopsidedness, and complex non-circular motions near the AGN.

quotes.quote: The S1.9/L1.9 barred galaxy NGC 4579 has been mapped at 0.5 ^ resolution (see figure fig:feeding a,b adapted from Garc a-Burillo \ 2004, in prep). The molecular gas distribution in the inner 500 pc mimics the gas response to a bar potential. The ^ 12 CO(2--1) map shows 1.6 10 ^ 8 M _ of molecular gas piled up in 2 leading lanes. However, the m=2 point-symmetry in the gas distribution breaks up in the inner 100 pc disk where lopsidedness seems to take over. As illustrated by figure fig:feeding a , the distribution of neutral gas close to the AGN consists of a 150 pc off-centered ringed disk which is also identified in the V--I color HST image of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4579 as a low-luminosity AGN/LINER with AGN, starburst, shock, jet, and extended HII-region contributions to its line and continuum emission.

quotes.quote: We have modelled the low luminosity AGN NGC 4579 by explaining both the continuum and the line spectra observed with different apertures. It was found that the nuclear emission is dominated by an AGN such that the flux from the active centre (AC) is relatively low compared with that of the NLR of Seyfert galaxies. However, the contribution of a young starburst cannot be neglected, as well as that of shock dominated clouds with velocities of 100, 300, and 500 .

The continuum SED of NGC 4579 is characterized by the strong flux from an old stellar population. Emissions in the radio range show synchrotron radiation from the base of the jet outflowing from the accretion disc within 0.1 pc from the active centre.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4579 as a classical LINER galaxy and finds an old-dominated stellar population, central metal-rich stars, an annulus of metal-poor stars, and a small metal-enhanced ring.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4579 the star formation history is quite simple (Fig. fig1 ). Nearly 100 of the stellar mass has an age of 9-12 Gyr, although some recent star formation, of negligible mass fraction, is also detected. The first generation has two metallicities: metal rich stars are highly concentrated at the center while metal poor stars form an annulus around it. A small fraction of the stellar mass formed an external ring and is metal enhanced.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4579 to M81 and NGC 1097, citing broad double-peaked Balmer lines, prior SED modeling, and adopted disk-radius and inclination constraints.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 NGC 4579 shares many characteristics with NGC 3031 (M81) and NGC 1097: its nucleus features broad double-peaked Balmer emission lines barth01 and a lack of the iron K line emission erac02 . quat99 previously modeled the SED of NGC 4579 compiled by ho99 using the ``old'' ADAF model ny95 . From the width of the broad H line, barth01 obtained a rough estimate of the inner radius of the line-emitting portion of the accretion disk r_ tr 160 . Hence, we adopt in our SED models r_ tr 160 and i=45^ .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4579's zig-zag molecular-gas relation as a consequence of its bar and ring, producing alternating elevated and lowered gas regions.

quotes.quote: Other galaxies show changes in their more central regions, such as the superposition of two slopes in NGC 4535, a flattening and then upturn in NGC 4548, or the zig-zag structure of NGC 4579. These are not solely due to changes in the content but also reflect changes in the central stellar structure of galaxies. NGC 4535 and NGC 4548 are barred with central bulges, causing a steeper relation in their centres and bar regions and a flatter relation in the disc. NGC 4579 has a bar and a ring, causing an alternation between elevated and in the centre, lowered in a higher region between the centre and the ring, and higher in a lower region in the ring and disc. Beyond having interesting effects on the shape of the \ relation, this suggests that we must use caution when comparing properties at fixed , particularly in the ranges where stellar structures could be important.

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra contours for NGC 4579, extended soft X-rays on kiloparsec scales, hard X-rays confined to the AGN-like core, and off-nuclear point sources.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 The Chandra contours of NGC 4579 are shown in Figure 9, overlaid on an HST image . The soft X-ray emission is extended on a scale of 1 kpc on either side of the nucleus. The hard X-rays are confined to the core, presumably from the AGN. There is a single off-nuclear X-ray point source about 7 ^ from the core of the galaxy and possibly a few other faint point sources.

sources.summary: The paper finds mixed bar-age indicators for NGC 4579, ultimately treating its bar as recently formed while noting possible bulge-light contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 has a _ z, bar value closer to that of an evolved bar and a _z value typical of a young one. Our farthest measurements, however, clearly indicate that bar and disk have a similar _z , at least in their outer parts, which makes us consider this bar as still recently formed. Contamination from bulge light may again be the cause for the high _ z, bar values. NGC 2665 is a similar case, and the very low and uncertain _z estimates in the farthest points along the bar minor axis may be occulting a young bar behind a high value for _z .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4579 as a LINER with AGN-like broad emission lines plus reported UV and X-ray variability, using it as a low-luminosity comparison to NGC 4395.

quotes.quote: Maoz et al. \ (1998) have recently studied the UV spectra of seven LINERs. They found that two of the nuclei (M 81 and NGC 4579) show strong broad emission lines. However, at least in other three cases the UV continuum is dominated by stellar features from massive young stars. They suggest that LINERs could be formed by two distinctive populations: a first group where young massive stars are responsible for the photoionization of the line emitting gas, and a second group where the central source is a genuine AGN. The possible UV variability detected in NGC 4579 supports this view (Barth et al. \ 1996).

In this respect, the UV variability reported for NGC 4579 could be a further link between LINER nuclei and more powerful AGN. X-ray long term variability by significant factors has been reported for M 81 (Petre et al. \ 1993; Ishisaki et al. \ 1996) as well as for NGC 4579 (Serlemitsos, Ptak Yaqoob 1996).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4579 as a LINER/Seyfert system whose central AGN dominates H-alpha and 24 micron emission, while its CO J=3-2 emission is very weak.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 (M58) is classified as LINER 1.9/Seyfert 1.9 gb05 .
For NGC 4579, the central AGN dominates the emission at both H and 24 m.
For NGC 4579, the CO J =1-0 mass is a factor of ten larger than the upper limit to the CO J =3-2 mass, again consistent with the very weak CO J =3-2 emission in this galaxy as a whole.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 4579 as a type-1 LINER, reports inconclusive Swift timing, discusses its Fe K line, and compares its optical-to-X-ray behavior with AGN trends.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 also shows a marginal (F-test probability of 3.207 10^ 2 or 2.1- ) Fe K line at 6.4_ 0.4 ^ +0.2 keV, with EW =550_ 310 ^ +5100 eV.
Our XRT timing results on NGC 4203 and NGC 4579 are inconclusive.

A prominent, variable Fe K emission line has been detected by ASCA and XMM-Newton in NGC 4579, and has given rise to various interpretations as to its origin from a standard accretion disk or a truncated disk Dewangan2004:ngc4579xmmfeline,Terashima1998:ngc4579ascafeline,Terashima2000:ngc4579felinevar,Terashima2002:ASCAliners .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4579's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 is a spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster at a distance of D= 16.8 3.4 Mpc tully_nearby_1988 with an active nucleus classified as a broad-line LINER/Sy 1.9 transition nucleus ho_search_1997-1 . A radio jet (PA 135 ) and star formation were detected in the nucleus ulvestad_origin_2001,contini_complex_2004 . The first MIR observations of this object were performed with IRTF in 1980 cizdziel_multiaperture_1985 , in 1982 scoville_10_1983 and in 1983 lawrence_observations_1985 .

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 4579 with galaxies showing radial cold-dust temperature dependence, similarity between heating and temperature maps, and an emissivity distribution varying little with radius.

quotes.quote: When both parameters are fitted simultaneously, the cold dust temperature maps for 10 galaxies (NGC 1097, NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4569, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7331) exhibit a strong radial dependence.

As might be expected, we see a strong similarity between the heating maps and the T_c maps, particularly for NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055, similar to the correlation between resolved 24 m emission and the cold dust temperature found for NGC 891 hughes2014 .

The Old Stellar Population and Submillimeter Slope Variations We find an almost universal decrease in the measured as a function of radius for our sources (the notable exceptions are NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4579, and NGC 4736, where the distribution of does not vary greatly with radius).

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 4579 enigmatic: an anemic galaxy with radio-bright emission likely contaminated by an AGN-driven outflow and atypical radio/SFR-polarization behavior.

quotes.quote: The radio-bright region in NGC 4579 (Fig. fig:rcfir_spixx1c1_nice_4 ) is enigmatic.
Since a CR source (the AGN), which is not connected to star formation, is probably involved in NGC 4579 (Appendix sec:n4579 ), we will discard this galaxy when we calculate sample-average values.

As already stated, NGC 4579 is enigmatic: it is an anemic galaxy as NGC 4548, which is radio-dim, but it is radio-bright probably due to additional radio continuum emission from an AGN-driven outflow (Sect. sec:radiobright ).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4579 as a LINER/low-luminosity AGN with two-zone accretion evidence, lacking a Big Blue Bump and showing a red bump fit by a truncated disk.

quotes.quote: Low-Ionization Nuclear Emission Region (LINER) galaxies, and more generally low luminosity AGN (LLAGN), have unusual spectral properties . It has been proposed that the observations could be understood if accretion proceeds via an ADAF in these galactic nuclei , . A feature of the proposed models is that the accretion flow consists of two zones: an outer thin disk that extends from some large radius down to a transition radius R_ tr and an inner ADAF that extends from R_ tr to the BH horizon. There is some direct evidence for such a geometry of the accretion flow in NGC 4258 , M81 and NGC 4579 . M81 and NGC 4579, in particular, lack the Big Blue Bump that is seen in AGN. Instead, they have a red bump, which can be fitted only with a truncated disk .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4579 in its variability discussion, explains its observations with shock-dominated models, and assigns its nuclear clouds among the highest shock velocities.

quotes.quote: Long-term variability is observed in NGC 3031, NGC 4258 and NGC 4579, while no significant variability was found in NGC 404, NGC 4111, NGC 4438, NGC 4579 and NGC 4639.
The observations of the galaxies NGC 4486, NGC 4579, and NGC 4051 are better explained by SD models (thin solid line joining the results for = 900, 1000, and 1100 and d/g = 10 ^ 13 ).
Clouds in NGC 4486 and NGC 4579 have the highest shock velocities ( 1000 ) and are located in the nuclear region (=8.9 pc).

sources.summary: New NUGA CO data for NGC 4579 resolve asymmetric molecular-gas kinematics, including a one-sided transverse velocity gradient interpreted as evidence for lopsided instability near the AGN.

quotes.quote: New CO(2--1) A-configuration data of NGC 4579 have fully resolved the asymmetric kinematics of molecular gas (Fig. Fig:2 ). The new observations have revealed a transverse velocity gradient on the eastern arm which has surprisingly no counterpart on the western arm; this is further evidence for the existence of a lopsided instability.

However, the new subarcsec NUGA maps have unveiled in some galaxies (NGC 7217 (Combes et al ), NGC 6951 (Schinnerer et al 2004, in prep.) and NGC 4579 (Garc a-Burillo et al 2004, in prep.)) the presence of isolated GMC-like gas units (of high Q) closely linked to the AGN source; these low mass gas concentrations, suspicious of being directly responsible of AGN feeding, seem to coexist with the larger-scale self-gravitating gas instabilities.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4579 as a LINER with an unresolved VLBA radio core, multiple HST UV flux measurements, and short-timescale UV brightening.

quotes.quote: NGC 4579 - VLBA measurements by Anderson et al. (2004) of the radio core, unresolved at 1 mas, are: 14.6 mJy at 0.7 cm; 11.1 mJy at 1.35 cm; 15.3 mJy at 1.9 cm; 16.2 mJy at 3.6 cm; 19.2 mJy at 6 cm. The various UV fluxes that haven been measured with HST, from low to high, are: at 2300 in 1994, 33 10^ 17 (Barth et al. 1996; Maoz et al. 1998); at 2500 in 2003, 61 10^ 17 (M05, mean of two epochs); at 3300 in 2003, 42 10^ 17 (M05, mean of two epochs); and at 2300 in 1993, 110 10^ 17 (Maoz et al. 1995); Between the two epochs of M05, separated by less than a month, the nucleus brightened by 7 per cent in both UV bands.

gmw_00000026.name: NGC 4736
gmw_00000026.overview: NGC 4736, also designated Messier 94, is an early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2303.05586] hosting a low-ionization nuclear emission region [arXiv:2303.05586, arXiv:astro-ph/9811377] and a variable double radio source at its nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0502265]. The galaxy features a star-forming circumnuclear ring [arXiv:1501.07279, arXiv:1109.0369] and a dusty gaseous disk with a multi-armed nuclear spiral structure [arXiv:2303.05586].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 4736 with a newer rotation curve, finding low mass-to-light ratios and concluding that any non-baryonic dark matter is absent or very low.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 Here, we once again consider galaxy NGC 4736, which earlier was studied in bib:jalocha_ApJ . This time, however, we use another, newer rotation curve published by bib:blok . In figure fig:4736 , this new curve is compared with that obtained by bib:sofue we used last time. Both the curves are shown assuming distance 4.7 different from the previous 5.1 . Although both curves do not overlap we come to comparable conclusions concerning the amount of non-baryonic dark matter.

Surface density obtained in the global disk model smoothly approaches that of gas in the outskirts of the galaxy, we obtain also low M/L ratios ( 1.28 for I-band, 1.44 for V-band, and 2.01 for B-band). In addition, the local M/L ratio decreases with radius for the largest radii. Therefore, despite the new rotation curve does not break sphericity condition so strongly unlike that by bib:sofue , we still consider this galaxy as containing no dark matter or that abundance of dark matter is at most very low.

sources.summary: The paper studies heating and cooling in NGC 4736's circumnuclear ring, finding star formation peaks at inflow points but requires an additional distributed component across the ring.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we use new Herschel PACS observations, obtained as part of the KINGFISH Open Time Key Program, along with archival Spitzer and ground-based observations from the SINGS Legacy project, to investigate the heating and cooling of the interstellar medium in the nearby star-forming ring galaxy, NGC 4736. By comparing spatially resolved estimates of the stellar FUV flux available for heating, with the gas and dust cooling derived from the FIR continuum and line emission, we show that while star formation is indeed dominant at the inflow points in NGC 4736, additional star formation is needed to balance the gas heating and cooling throughout the ring. This additional component most likely arises from the general increase in gas density in the ring over its lifetime. Our data provide strong evidence, therefore, for a combination of the two paradigms for star formation in the ring in NGC \ 4736.

To test whether the heating of the neutral ISM in the ring is indeed dominated by stellar radiative heating from stars formed at the inflow points, we investigate the case of the circumnuclear ring in NGC 4736, a nearby (D=4.66 Mpc, i 35 , PA 300 ) SAab spiral galaxy. The ring in NGC 4736 has a radius of 40 (0.9 kpc). Based on a previously observed HI velocity field 2008AJ....136.2563W and assuming that the spiral arms are concave, the north side of the disk is the near side, and the south the far side, with a clockwise rotation. We have combined available data to form a large multiwavelength dataset, tracing the gas and the recent star formation, including Herschel PACS data from the KINGFISH (Key Insights on Nearby Galaxies: A Far-Infrared Survey with Herschel) Open Time Key Program 2011PASP..123.1347K .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4736 as its observational basis, modeling its circumnuclear gas-dust disk, molecular gas, spiral structure, rotation curve, and central black hole.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 (Messier 94) is an early type spiral galaxy of the type (R)SA()ab Korm with a low ionization nuclear emission region. The central part of the galaxy has a massive circumnuclear dusty gaseous disk with a multi-armed spiral structure not related to the large-scale spiral structure of the galaxy. The nuclear spirals are not associated with star formation and they are very irregular with both trailing and leading components that often cross. The number of the arms, traced by the dust, grows from five in the central regions of the mini-disk to approximately twenty at the disk's periphery Elmegreen1 .

Gas and Dust in the Circumnuclear Disk of NGC 4736 Gas in the mini-disk of NGC 4736 is almost entirely in a molecular phase (90--100 ) inside the central kiloparsec of the galaxy Gerin . Shioya et al. , Van der Laan et al. , and Muñoz-Tuñón et al. estimated the gas surface density in central regions of the circumnuclear disk of NGC 4736 to be about 120 M _ pc ^ 2 , decreasing exponentially with a radial scale length of about 0.5 kpc.

The central region of NGC 4736 has a supermassive black hole with mass M_ BH =6.8 10^6 M_ Terrazas which was taken into account in constructing the theoretical rotation curve in our simulations. We use available observational data to build the theoretical rotation curves of the mini-disk of the galaxy NGC 4736 shown in Figure Rot .

sources.summary: The paper maps M94's stellar halo, finding metal-rich outer-disk continuation, a metal-poor halo, and a low-mass dominant merger unlikely to build its pseudobulge.

quotes.quote: The nearby ( D 4.2 Mpc) disk galaxy M94 (NGC 4736) has the largest pseudobulge in the local universe, and offers a unique opportunity for investigating the role of merging in the formation of its pseudobulge. We present a first ever look at the M94's stellar halo, which we expect to contain a fossil record of M94's past mergers. Using Subaru's Hyper Suprime-Cam, we resolve and identify red giant branch (RGB) stars in M94's halo, finding two distinct populations. The metal-rich RGB stars show an unbroken exponential profile to a radius of 30 kpc that is a clear continuation of M94's outer disk. M94's metal poor stellar halo is detectable over a wider area and clearly separates from its metal-rich disk.

By integrating the halo density profile, we infer a total accreted stellar mass of 2.8 10^8 M_ , with a median metallicity of [ M/H ] =- 1.4. This indicates that M94's most-massive past merger was with a galaxy similar to, or less massive than, the Small Magellanic Cloud. Few nearby galaxies have had such a low-mass dominant merger; therefore we suggest that M94's pseudobulge was not significantly impacted by merging.

sources.summary: The paper links non-circular ionized-gas motions in NGC 4736 to a central bar-like structure, molecular-gas radial flows, and a circumnuclear mini-spiral with dust lanes.

quotes.quote: The [ NII ] velocity field reveals non-circular ionized gas motions, as is shown from the fact that turns at =5-30'' . The non-circular motions are most likely connected with bar-like structure at <15-20'' , detected from isophotal parameters variations: a turn of PA and a peak (Fig. 4736m ). This conclusion confirms the result by Wong Blitz ( ) who suggest that molecular gas kinematics is consistent with radial flows in the central stellar bar ( 30'' in diameter).

We must notice that the most highest-contrast part of the circumnuclear mini-spiral is located at these radii and distorts the shape of the isophotes in the HST images. This spiral structure is studied in recent work by Elmegreen et al. ( ). The dust lanes in the spiral appear in the HST image up to =20-30'' .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4736 as a nearby ringed RSab galaxy whose molecular bar, tightly wound spiral arms, gas kinematics, and star-forming ring reflect large-scale dynamics.

quotes.quote: Non-circular Gas Kinematics and Star Formation in the Ringed Galaxy NGC 4736 Tony Wong and Leo Blitz Astronomy Department and Radio Astronomy Laboratory, University of California, Berkeley, CA 94720 twong, blitz@astro.berkeley.edu We analyze the gas kinematics and star formation properties of the nearby RSab galaxy NGC 4736 using interferometric and single-dish CO(1-0) data and previously published \ and \ data. The CO morphology is dominated by a central molecular bar and tightly wound spiral arms associated with a bright ring of star formation. Strong \ emission is also found in the ring, but \ is absent from the central regions. Comparison of the \ and \ distributions suggests that \ in the ring is primarily dissociated H _2 . Modeling of the CO kinematics reveals gas motion in elliptical orbits around the central bar, and we argue that the ring represents both the OLR of the bar and the ILR of a larger oval distortion.

Motivated by these questions, we have undertaken a detailed study of the gas kinematics and star formation in NGC 4736, a nearby ( d = 4.2 Mpc using H_0 = 75 \ Mpc ^ 1 ) Sab galaxy with a bright ring of star formation about 45 \ (1 kpc) from its center. A faint outer ring, about 5 \ (6 kpc) in radius, is also seen in deep optical images San61 . Previous \ synthesis observations Bos77 and optical and near-infrared imaging hereafter MMG95 Mol95 have demonstrated that the disk of the galaxy is non-axisymmetric, and hence the rings may occur at the inner and outer Lindblad resonances (ILR and OLR) of an oval potential Ger91 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a coherent spiral magnetic field in NGC 4736 that crosses its inner pseudoring, relating the field, rings, oval, and minibar to secular evolution.

quotes.quote: Buta 2 1 Astronomical Observatory, Jagiellonian University, 30-244 Krak ow, Poland; chris@oa.uj.edu.pl 2 Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Alabama, Tuscaloosa, AL 35487, USA We report the discovery of a coherent magnetic spiral structure within the nearby ringed Sab galaxy NGC 4736. High sensitivity radio polarimetric data obtained with the VLA at 8.46 GHz and 4.86 GHz show a distinct ring of total radio emission precisely corresponding to the bright inner pseudoring visible in other wavelengths. However, unlike the total radio emission, the polarized radio emission reveals a clear pattern of ordered magnetic field of spiral shape, emerging from the galactic centre. The magnetic vectors do not follow the tightly-wrapped spiral arms that characterize the inner pseudoring, but instead cross the ring with a constant and large pitch angle of about 35^ .

Motivated by these goals we performed radio polarimetric observations of NGC 4736 (M 94), the nearest and largest galaxy in the sky to manifest a strong ringed morphology. Classified as type (R)S A B( s)ab by buta07 , it shows two rings. The outer ring (R) is a weak, diffuse, and smooth feature.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA radio polarimetry of NGC 4736, finding a radio ring but a coherent spiral magnetic field crossing the ring, dust lane, and other gas features.

quotes.quote: We present high sensitivity radio polarimetric (VLA) observations of a galaxy with strong orbital resonances -- NGC 4736. The total radio intensity at 8.4 GHz covers smoothly the whole galaxy bulge and reveals a distinct ring of radio emission closely related to the ring morphology visible in infrared, CO and H emission. However, the magnetic field reveals a very coherent spiral pattern. The magnetic field vectors are crossing the inner starbursting ring, the dust lanes within the ring and other rather circularly shaped features visible in other gas traces.

Using the VLA we investigated this problem and observed NGC 4736 (M 94) -- the nearest and largest galaxy showing a ringed morphology (Buta Combes ). Its inner and outer rings are believed to coincide with Lindblad resonances of a triaxial bulge, the inner disk (oval distortion) and/or the nuclear minibar. The giant inner ring located at a distance of about 47" (1.1 kpc) from the (LINER) nucleus, is a region of intense star formation which dominates the UV, H , infrared and CO images (Wong Blitz ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4736 (M94) as a barred, face-on spiral test case; its inner-ring oxygen abundances vary with azimuth, with the lowest values roughly at the central bar end.

quotes.quote: Case-study: NGC 4736 ( M94) In looking for an azimuthal abundance gradient in an external galaxy, we have chosen this object for several reasons. Firstly, while not having the exact same type as our own Galaxy, it is a spiral with a central bar, and is relatively face-on Block94 . Secondly, it has a ring of \ regions in the inner disk, which are well sampled in azimuth Lynds74 . Thirdly, oxygen abundances of these \ regions are present in the literature: M-B97 studied the \ region ring with narrow-band filters to measure the fluxes of certain diagnostic lines in the optical, and used them to derive abundances.

To the eye, it seems that the abundances in the lower-right quadrant of the figure tend to be lower than average; while at the left edge of the ring at (X,Y) (-1,0) the abundances are highest. Interestingly, the minimum in abundance levels corresponds roughly with the end of the bar _ bar = 110 30 , indicated by the shaded region in Fig. \ fig:ngc4736 - Block94,W-B00 . This is similar to the trend seen in our own Galaxy, where the abundance minimum is found in the RSG clusters located at the end of the Galaxy's Bar.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4736 (M94) as a nearby double-ringed spiral whose 333 MHz emission traces a largely nonthermal circumnuclear star-forming ring and compact nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 (M94) is the nearest large spiral galaxy, which has double ringed morphology. The inner ring is mainly made of recent star forming regions, showing bremsstrahlung spectra duric88a and is distinctly visible in the H . The outer ring however is a low surface brightness feature in the optical. The rings were suggested to be caused by inner and outer Lindblad resonances schom76 .

NGC 4736 (M94) : The 333 MHz map (see Figure figure1 ) of this galaxy has an angular resolution of 13 12 , corresponding to an spatial scale of about 0.3 kpc for a distance of 4.7 Mpc. The other low frequency study of this galaxy exist at 610 MHz bruyn77 , observed with much lower angular resolution of 56 85 . At our angular resolution we clearly detect emission from the inner star forming circumnuclear ring of the galaxy as shown in Figure 8 overlaid on the H map. The ring is located at about 50 (major axis), corresponding to 1.1 kpc, from the center, and is 19 (420 pc) wide (up to the half power) having a flux density of 240 13 mJy. The estimated thermal fraction of the ring at 333 MHz and 1374.5 MHz is 5 1 and 10 4 respectively, and hence the ring at 333 MHz is largely nonthermal in origin.

sources.summary: The paper describes M94 as isolated, with resonant outer star-forming arms but a smooth red outer disk that favors secular radial migration over accretion.

quotes.quote: M94 (NGC 4736) is part of the Canes Venatici I Cloud karachentsev05 , a loose association of galaxies that may be expanding with the Hubble flow karachentsev03 . It is hence fairly isolated: karachentsev14 list its nearest neighbor as IC 3687, a dwarf galaxy located at roughly the same distance as M94 4.5 Mpc; jacobs09, radburn11b , but some 3^ ( 240 kpc, in projection) away on the sky see also: geller83 . The galaxy contains an outer star-forming structure, often referred to as a `ring', at 200 \ (4 kpc), which is offset in position angle from the bright inner disk. This ring is also visible in H 1 e.g. bosma77, mulder93 , where it appears as a set of irregular spiral arms at high column density this is also true of its appearance in the UV, e.g. Trujillo et al. 2009 walter08 .

New star formation in M94 is truncated at 10 kpc, beyond which the disk appears azimuthally smooth and red but for some mild lopsidedness. The stellar disk, which seems to be continuous despite the offset inner and outer isophotes, extends to at least 20kpc, or 9 scale lengths, with no emergence of a stellar halo down to a surface brightness of 30. Given M94's isolation and smooth undisturbed outer disk, our data favors secularly-driven radial migration of disk populations to explain the galaxy's outer structure.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC4736 as a disk-scaling outlier, fixing its dust opacity and noting that its intrinsic dust mass falls away from the fitted relation.

quotes.quote: For three galaxies in our sample, namely NGC3031, NGC4594 and NGC4736, the initial value derived for _ B ^ f is higher than 8.0 the upper limit of the numerical corrections for dust effects given in and . These values are unrealistically large. This could be due to either an overestimate of the stellar mass, or issues with the fit/sky determination (especially for the case of NGC4594) resulting in a larger value for R_ s,d . Therefore, for these 3 galaxies we have chosen to fix the value of the dust opacity to 3.80 (see Table tab:dust ), the average value found by . However, this does not significantly change the overall trends we observe or the mean values for _ B ^ f and dust/stellar ratios, even if we exclude these galaxies from calculations.

It is also seen that for NGC4594 and NGC4736 the intrinsic dust masses are far from the intrinsic relation and their observed values. This could be due to our assumption for the dust opacity _ B ^ f =3.80 .

sources.summary: The paper presents GBT observations of M94-CL9, finding stronger and broader H I emission than earlier FAST text values while leaving its RELHIC status possible but strained.

quotes.quote: We present new observations of the REionization-Limited Cloud (RELHIC) candidate, M94-CL9, detected around M94 by Zhou et al. \ using the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical Telescope (FAST). \ M94-CL9's properties as detected by FAST are consistent with a RELHIC as noted by Benitez-Llambay Navarro. \ Our observations with the Robert C. \ Byrd Green Bank Telescope (GBT) detect greater emission in M94-CL9 and result in properties that are larger (corrected velocity width, W_ 50,,t =35.7 0.6 ; and integrated flux, Sdv =0.28 0.04 Jy ) than those found by Zhou et al. \ but that match those from the FAST All-Sky (FASHI) survey. \ These larger properties do not preclude M94-CL9 from being a RELHIC, but the wider spectral extent and spectral asymmetry reported here may be in tension with predictions of RELHIC properties.

One of these clouds, M94-Cloud 9 (M94-CL9), has no obvious optical counterpart and its properties from FAST are comparable to those of RELHICs in the APOSTLE simulations: it has a narrow velocity width ( 20 ), it is relatively isolated from M94 ( D_ proj 109 kpc 1.3 degrees), and its distribution is fairly circular ( _ CL9 6' , axial ratio b/a >0.8 at a column density of 1 10^ 18 cm^ 2 ; _ FAST 2.9' ). \ More recently, 2023BenitezLlambay presented a comparison of the FAST distribution of M94-CL9 measured by 2023Zhou with RELHIC models from 2017BenitezLlambay and find consistency between observations and simulations. \ Here, we present a new profile of M94-CL9 using the Robert C. \ Byrd Green Bank Telescope (GBT).

sources.summary: The paper argues that M94 may shape the Local Sheet flow field: receding dwarfs cluster in its direction, an ascending-node mass estimate matches satellite-orbit constraints, and nearby dwarfs may approach it.

quotes.quote: Receding dwarfs on the near side of the Council are predominantly located in the direction of M94, although this may be a manifestation of the limitations of sampling. If M94 were entirely responsible for the placement of the ascending node, then the galaxy's total mass relative to the Local Group would have to be 0.8^ +0.4 _ 0.3 , the same as indicated by the orbits of satellite galaxies. Rather, if the placement of the ascending node were set by matter distributed evenly in azimuth at the Council's radius, then the required total mass relative to the Local Group would have to be 4^ +3 _ 2 , which is 30 to 40 lower than implied by satellite motions but still consistent within errors.

Given how dwarfs with positive peculiar velocities are distributed in longitude, a possible explanation for the ascending node is a localized tug of war between the Local Group and M94. If correct, the ascending node would be located at the distance r_a from the centroid of the Local Group at which gravitational forces balance, in which case the ratio of the total mass M _ M94 of M94 relative to the total mass M _ LG of the Local Group would be given by M _ M94 / M _ LG = (r_ M94 / r_a) -1 ^2 where r_ M94 is the distance of M94 from the centroid of the Local Group. From mcc14a , the heliocentric distances of M94 and the centroid of the Local Group are 4.51 0.11 Mpc and 0.33 0.03 Mpc , respectively. Thus, r_ M94 is 4.57 0.11 Mpc . In the direction of M94, r_a is 2.41 0.23 Mpc . Consequently, M _ M94 / M _ LG = 0.80^ +0.42 _ 0.27 .

sources.summary: The paper uses planetary nebulae in NGC 4736/M94 to trace outer disk kinematics, finding flat rotation and velocity dispersions consistent with a simple isothermal sheet model.

quotes.quote: For this pilot project NGC 4736 (M94) was chosen. It has a large angular size and is at a distance of 6 Mpc (Bosma et al 1977). The galaxy shows some peculiar morphological features, most notably an inner and an outer optical ring with radii of 1 and 5.5 arcmin respectively. The stellar light is too faint for direct optical spectroscopy outside 1 arcmin, and our goal was to measure PN kinematics to three times this radius.

Flat rotation is seen out to the edge of the field, although the scatter is large. This rotation velocity agrees with the extended HI rotation curve also shown, implying that the PNe are distributed in a disk rather than a halo. The velocity dispersion along the major axis is observed to decrease from 45 km/s at one scalelength to 20 km/s at four scalelengths.

sources.summary: The paper reports a variable double radio source in NGC 4736, tying the stronger component to the low-luminosity AGN nucleus and considering the second component as a jet feature, hotspot, or possible second nucleus.

quotes.quote: During all epochs we detect a double radio source near ULX NGC 4736-1 as shown in Fig. fiN4736Map . Their separation is about 1", which corresponds to 20 pc at the galaxy for a distance of 4.3 Mpc. The position of the stronger radio source coincides with the position of the LLAGN reported by NagarFalckeWilson2002 and is in agreement with the Chandra position of ULX NGC 4736-1 (see table tablePositions and Fig. fiN4736Map ). No radio emission is detected at the position of the other ULX NGC 4736-2 (see Fig. fiN4736Map ).

To check whether these sources are compact we have observed NGC 4736 with the European VLBI Network. While source b was detected with a correlated flux of 0.86 mJy source a was not detected. No other low-luminosity AGN (LLAGN) in 2 cm survey of NagarFalckeWilson2002 shows such a double structure.

sources.summary: The paper reports an H survey of M94, deriving a nova rate, comparing its K-band luminosity-specific rate, and noting its star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: A multi-epoch H survey of the early-type spiral galaxy M94 (NGC 4736) has been completed as part of a program to establish the galaxy's nova rate. A total of four nova candidates were discovered in seven epochs of observation during the period from 2005 to 2007. After making corrections for temporal coverage and spatial completeness, a global nova rate of 5.0 ^ +1.8 _ 1.4 yr ^ 1 was determined. This rate corresponds to a specific-luminosity nova rate of 1.4 0.5 novae per year per 10 ^ 10 L_ ,K when the K luminosity is determined from the B-K color, or 1.5 0.4 novae per year per 10 ^ 10 L_ ,K when the K luminosity is derived from the Two Micron All Sky Survey.

M94 is noted for a ring of active star formation with a diameter of 70'' surrounding the galaxy's bright nucleus. Given the models of Yungelson et al. (1997), which predict a higher LSNR in galaxies with a recent history of star formation, it is perhaps surprising that the LSNR for M94 is not higher than those of galaxies with similar Hubble types, but with less active star formation.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4736 in its six-galaxy LBT outer-disk sample, finding marginally resolved star clusters beyond R_25 that trace H I gas and imply low-level cluster formation.

quotes.quote: Expanding on the pilot project of , we present deep ( 27.5 mag V band point-source limiting magnitude) U \ and V band imaging of six galaxies: IC 4182, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, NGC 4826, NGC 5474, and NGC 6503. We find that the outer disk of each galaxy is populated with marginally-resolved star clusters with masses 10^3 M_ and ages up to 1 Gyr (masses and ages are limited by the depth of our imaging and uncertainties are large given how photometry can be strongly affected by the presence or absence of a few stars in such low mass systems), and that they are typically found out to at least 2 R_ 25 but sometimes as far as 3 to 4 R_ 25 - even beyond the apparent H 1 disk. The mean rate of cluster formation for 1 R_ 25 R 1.5R_ 25 is at least one every 2.5 Myr and the clusters are spatially correlated with the H 1 , most strongly with higher density gas near the periphery of the optical disk and with lower density neutral gas at the H 1 disk periphery.

We present new observations of six galaxies (IC 4182, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, NGC 4826, NGC 5474, NGC 6503) with the LBC-Blue imaging camera on the LBT on the dates listed in Table tab:gals . These systems comprise a representative sample of nearby disk galaxies by spanning a range of physical disk sizes. All are nearby ( D 15 Mpc) and only two, NGC 4826 and NGC 6503, are seen at high inclination ( > 60^ ).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4736 with an NFW halo only, finding central HI deficiency, centrally peaked molecular gas, differing molecular rotation curves, and interpretation complicated by non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 In Figure fig:N4736-NFW we plot the results from the mass modelling for NGC 4736. Fits using the ISO halos did not converge and resulted in unrealistic fits with R_c 1 and _0 1000 , which is most likely due to a sharp decline in the outer part of the rotation curve. We therefore only include results for the NFW halo.

For NGC 4736 we use a 2-component stellar decomposition to calculate the stellar predicted rotation curves. There is a deficiency of H i in the centre of NGC 4736. Correspondingly, the molecular gas distribution peaks in the centre of NGC 4736, with the maximum molecular gas mass surface density reaching 100 2008AJ....136.2782L using the Milky Way conversion factor.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4736 as an exception with a steep H I profile tied to ring-like morphology and a transition between its main disk and faint outer rings.

quotes.quote: The radial profiles of NGC 4736 and NGC 3621 decline steeply around 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 and for NGC 4826 at 5 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 . These three galaxies, however, have some structural peculiarities. NGC 4736 has a ring-like H i morphology which manifests itself as bumps in the H i column density profiles shown in Figure fig:radprof .

Earlier lower resolution H i observations by bosmaetal77 also revealed this pattern. The authors classified the galaxies as consisting of 5 different zones in which a faint outer ring surrounds the main body of the galaxy. The H i edge around 7 kpc corresponds to the transition from the main disk of the galaxy and the faint outer rings identified by bosmaetal77 .

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 4736 as a nearby Sab spiral with a LINER nucleus, finding disk X-ray binaries plus a nuclear source that may indicate a near-quiescent AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 is a nearby Sab spiral galaxy, hosting one of the closest examples of a LINER nucleus. We have utilized recent observations by and to characterise the X-ray properties of this galaxy. Twelve discrete X-ray sources are detected within the region subtended by its optical disk, the majority of which are likely to be X-ray binaries associated with the galaxy. By far the brightest source in the X-ray band is positionally coincident with the nucleus of the galaxy and is spatially resolved into a component with a radial extent of 3 kpc plus a point-like core.

A plausible model is that the hard continuum originates in a near-quiescent active galactic nucleus (with L_X 6 10^ 39 , 0.5--10 keV) embedded in the LINER at the centre of NGC 4736. However, an alternative explanation, namely that the LINER is the site of a dense population of X-ray binary sources, cannot be completely excluded. galaxies:individual:NGC 4736 -- galaxies:nuclei -- X-rays:galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4736 as a normal spiral with near-solar metallicity, no starburst or AGN energy source, and physical parameters matching the authors' Model 0 hot-core chemistry case.

quotes.quote: We identify Model 0 as a potentially good representative normal spiral. The metallicity of IC 342 is estimated to be 1.07 z _ (using 12+log(O/H) = 9.30 from Vila92, Garn98b ) while for NGC 4736 we have 1.04 z _ (using 12+log(O/H) = 9.01 from Zari94 ). In addition, these two galaxies do not show any bursts of star formation, suggesting that a radiation field comparable to that of the Milky Way as well as a hot core temperature of 300 K may be appropriate for them. They do not contain any additional source of energy such as, for instance, an AGN which could be responsible to an enhancement of the cosmic ray ionization rate value. All these physical parameter values suggest that Model 0 may be a good representative for these two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 4736, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 NGC 4736 (Fig. fig:ngc-4736 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] The 3.6 m IRAC image of NGC 4736 is dominated by a ring of star formation which is also prominently visible in the radio continuum and the total \ map. The center is well-defined both in the 3.6 m image and in the radio continuum. The former shows a small central minimum in the flux which coincides within 1 3 with the central radio-continuum maximum . We use the position of the radio continuum source ( =0 2 , =-0 4 ) for a \ run with all parameters left free.

sources.summary: The paper attributes NGC 4736's rotating torque pattern to two offset oval stellar distributions and finds the outer oval drives net outflow in the atomic gas disk.

quotes.quote: For NGC 4736 we found that the direction of the torque rotates by about 60 in the region of 2 - 0 kpc. This is likely due to the presence of two oval stellar distributions whose major axes have an offset of 60 suggesting a decoupling between these two dynamical modes. These ovals are clearly visible in the NIR images (see Fig. fig_torq_dpj ). The gravitational torque caused by the outer oval is still dominant in the region of the large gas spiral at 6.2 kpc producing a net outflow at this location (5.5 - 7 kpc). Averaged over the entire atomic gas disk, gas is transported outwards.
