sources.summary: The paper finds polarized emission from NGC 4736's inner star-forming disk and minor-axis lobe, with increasing large-radius polarized fraction and a central-disk minimum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 (M94) The polarized emission in this galaxy is seen from both the inner star-forming disk and also concentrated along the minor axis, particularly in the form of a possible polarized lobe directed toward the southwest. Within the central disk, the polarized intensity declines to a minimum in the direction of the receding major axis (PA = 296 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). The central source is polarized at about the 2 level. The inner ring is polarized on the south side, at about the same fraction. The polarized fraction increases at large radii up to about 40 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4736 as a classical LINER and finds old, 2.5-Gyr, and younger stellar components, with metal-rich nuclear stars and metal-poor stars arranged in a bar.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4736 we found that most of the stars were formed at two epochs: nearly 1/3 of the light comes from old stars (T > 10 Gyr) (Fig fig1 ). The metal rich ones (about solar) are highly concentrated in the nucleus while metal poor stars are scattered around it. Nearly 2/3 of the light is emitted by stars formed about 2.5 Gyr ago. These stars come in two metallicities: metal enriched (2.5 solar) form an oval structure around the nucleus while metal poor (0.21 solar) form a bar nearly aligned with the axis of rotation (Fig. fig2 ). A smaller fraction of the stars were formed more recently.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4736 as ringed and lacking prominent spiral structure, with ordered spiral-shaped magnetic fields and central/ring field strengths of 18 G and 15-25 G.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 is a ringed galaxy with no prominent spiral structure from radio through infrared to optical. Polarized radio emission revealed ordered magnetic field in spiral shape possibly amplified by large-scale MHD dynamo chyzy08 . They report mean total magnetic field of 17 G, slightly higher than our estimate of 14 G within a region of 3.5 arcmin (corresponds to inner 2.3 kpc radius). From our map (see Fig. bmaps ), the total magnetic field in the center is found to be 18 G while in the ring, the field strength is 15--25 G with an average total field strength of 16.5 G close to chyzy08 . Beyond the ring the magnetic field falls off to about 8--10 G at a distance of 3 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper finds that MOND overpredicts NGC 4736 outer velocities, while the EFE lowers the outer rotation curve and improves chi-squared from 5.96 to 1.57.

quotes.quote: (8) NGC 4736 : The Milgromian dynamics curve without the EFE agrees well with the observed curve in the inner parts, but it predicts significantly higher-than-observed velocities in the outer parts. The EFE leads to a velocity decrease in the external part of the rotation curve and significantly improves the quality of fit such that the chi-squared reduces from ^2=5.96 to 1.57 . A best fitted external filed of a_e=1.450^ +1.350 _ 0.505 a_0 is required for this agreement between MOND and the observation. This galaxy is located at a distance of about 5.02^ +1.4 _ 0.8 Mpc from us. The galaxy NGC 4244 ic-md, n4244-dm is the most effective source near to NGC 4736 n3034-dm, n4736-p, n3034-lu, n4736-dm with a distance of 4.12 Mpc from us.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4736 as the earliest-type sample galaxy, with low k_i values and a prominent star-forming ring in its SFR map.

quotes.quote: We have therefore selected eight galaxies in our sample: NGC 628, NGC 925, NGC 1097, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7793. Properties As we can see in Table tab:sample , the sample is made of fairly regular star--forming galaxies in the nearby universe, spanning the Hubble sequence from Sab to Sd. This ensures different levels of star-forming activity (from sSFR=-10.76 yr ^ 1 for NGC 4736 to sSFR=-9.59 yr ^ 1 for NGC 7793) and different levels of diffuse dust emission sauvage1992a .
Of particular interest, NGC 4736 has a strong star--forming ring that shows prominently on the map.
It is therefore not surprising that NGC 4736, the earliest type galaxy in the sample, shows some of the lowest k_i .

sources.summary: The paper discusses M94 as a sparsely satellited galaxy excluded from the main correlation because of limited survey radius, but important for future wider satellite searches.

quotes.quote: M94: using deep Hyper Suprime-Cam (HSC) observations, Smercina2018 performed a survey of the field of M94 down to M_ V =-9.1 mag finding only two satellites for this galaxy. Regrettably, their survey covers only out to 150 kpc projected distance and while we discuss this galaxy in our study, we do not include it in our correlation analysis.

Assuming that future surveys do not change the number of satellites of M94 significantly, adding it to the analysis would increase the significant of the correlation to well above the 3 confidence level. Actually, even if future surveys increase the number of confirmed satellites of M94 by a factor of 3 or 4, the significance of the N_ sat vs. B/T correlation would increase even further. It is therefore very important to conduct deeper and wider surveys in the field of this particular galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC 4736 as producing high-temperature, low CO(7-6) ratios, attributing them to a post-starburst nuclear population and softer intense stellar radiation rather than current star formation.

quotes.quote: We note that all of the high-temperature low CO(7-6)/ ratio points are contributed by the same galaxy, NGC 4736. This galaxy's central near-IR spectrum lacks Br emission walker88 and optical spectroscopy diagnoses its nuclear region as a 1 Gyr post-starburst with very little current star formation taniguchi96 . NGC 4736's dust is likely heated by this high density poststarburst population instead of ongoing star formation. Furthermore, NGC 4736 has the strongest central suppression smith17 which is similarly interpreted to be due to a high-intensity, but softer (than ongoing star formation) stellar radiation field. Thus NGC 4736's very low CO(7-6)/ ratios at relatively high dust temperature may be explained by intense starlight with a relative lack of recent star formation.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HST photometry for M 94, describing archive imaging in four filters, previous identification of three probable LBV stars, and a Hertzsprung-Russell diagram for its stellar content.

quotes.quote: Figure  Fig4 shows an HST image of a part of the galaxy M 94. The stellar photometry of this galaxy was performed with DOLPHOT 2.0 software package http: .

The archive HST telescope contains images of the M 94 galaxy (ID10402) obtained in the F435W, F555W, F658N and F814W filters with exposures of 1200, 1000, 1400 and 900 seconds. In this galaxy, a visual search for massive stars has already been carried out and three probable LBV stars have been found SVF2019 . The Hertzsprung-Russell diagram obtained after photometry of stars the M 94 is shown in Figure  Fig6 .

sources.summary: The paper fits only the inner part of NGC 4736 because its outskirts appear tidally disturbed, concluding outer spiral and stream features are tidal debris.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 As the outer parts of this galaxy appear to be tidally disturbed, we have fitted only the region inside R = 180 . The resulting rotation curve is shown in Figure  fig.N4736 , with the fitted radial and azimuthal bar flow velocities SS07 indicated, respectively, by the green and blue points with error bars. dB08 reported steep gradients in the apparent projection geometry within 100 , where we prefer a bar fit, and slightly more mild variations out to R=400 . Our model has generally higher orbit speeds than theirs over the radius range we fit. Our large uncertainties stem from the over 5 ^ uncertainty in the rather low inclination of the inner part of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4736 (M 94) as a spiral target, estimating its globular-cluster count, distance, SMBH mass, luminosity, and globular-cluster specific frequency.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present new measurements and analyses of the globular cluster systems of the Sab galaxies NGC 3368 (M 96), NGC 4736 (M 94) and NGC 4826 (M 64), and the Sm galaxy NGC 4395. Their surface brightness fluctuations (SBF) distances are 10.4 ^ +1.1 _ 1.0 Mpc for NGC 3368, 5.0 0.4 Mpc for NGC 4736, and 7.3 0.7 Mpc for NGC 4826 tonr01,blak10 . NGC 4395 is completely devoid of a bulge, and hence lacks an SBF measurement; we adopt a Cepheid distance of 4.3 0.4 Mpc thim04 . The masses of their central supermassive black holes are (3.6 1.1) 10^5 \ M_ for NGC 4395 pete05 ; (7.5 1.5) 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 3368; 6.8 ^ +1.5 _ 1.6 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 4736, and (1.6 0.4) 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 4826 korm13a .
Hence, we get N_ GC = 35^ +20 _ 16 for NGC 3368, N_ GC = 15 7 for NGC 4395, N_ GC = 46 18 for NGC 4736, and N_ GC = 69^ +31 _ 28 for NGC 4826.

For NGC 4736, the values are B_ T,0 = 8.75 0.13 mag and ( B \ V ) _ T,0 = 0.72 0.01 mag, from which M_V = -20.46 0.21 mag, and S_N = 0.30 0.10 , with random errors only, and S_N = 0.30 0.13 , if we include systematics.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4736 as an early-type spiral with central atomic depletion, enhanced star formation near a resonance ring, and high Q_g despite ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: We find that four of the galaxies (NGC 4321, 4501, 4736, 5457) show both an \ deficit and CO enhancement in their inner regions, suggesting a large-scale conversion of atomic to molecular gas. This phenomenon has long been noted e.g., Morris:78 , and its occurrence appears to be unrelated to the Hubble type of the galaxy, since 4736 is classified as early-type (Sab) whereas 5457 is classified as late-type (Scd).

The two exceptions are NGC 4321, where the SFR is suppressed in the vicinity of a bar ( R =15 -55 , where _ mol 1.4 ), and NGC 4736, where the SFR is enhanced near a resonance ring ( R =30 -55 , where _ mol 1.6 ). Even in NGC 4321 and 4736, however, there are regions where a power-law relationship is roughly obeyed.

At first glance, it is impressive how well the relation _ gas _ crit holds for some of the galaxies (NGC 4501, 5033, and 5055). On the other hand, there are two galaxies (NGC 4321 and 4414) where _ gas > _ crit over a significant range of radius, while in NGC 4736 and 5457, _ gas < _ crit across most of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper resolves NGC 4736's halo into RGB stars, measures a TRGB distance, and notes its isolation despite being the brightest CVn I galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 is the brightest galaxy of type Sa in CVn I. We resolve it into stars for the first time. The WFPC2 was pointed at the galaxy periphery to avoid stellar crowding. In the galaxy halo the CM diagram (Fig. 2) shows numerous of RGB stars with I (TRGB) = 24.33 0.28 mag, which yields distance of 4.66 0.59 Mpc. Karachentseva Karachentsev (1998) carried out a proper search for dwarf companions to NGC 4736 based on the POSS-II plates.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4736 as a ringed galaxy with nuclear stellar and CO bars whose kinematic terms are attributed mainly to bar or oval-distortion streaming.

quotes.quote: This prototypical ringed galaxy contains a nuclear stellar bar and a corresponding CO bar Wong:00 . We attribute the large s_1 and s_3 terms in the CO data to the effects of the bar, although the scatter in the s_1 s_3 plot is large [ Fig. fig:s13:4736 () ] , partly due to the complicating effects of a strong spiral arc at R 25 whose streaming motions are distinct from the bar's. The \ kinematics between R =50 \ and 150 \ show a strong s_1 term with |ds_3/ds_1| 0.2 .

The more sophisticated analysis presented in sec:ellstr suggests that instead we may be viewing elliptical streaming in the vicinity of an ILR ; in fact, the prominent H ring in this galaxy at R=45 has been modeled as the ILR of a large-scale oval distortion Gerin:91 . A pronounced decrease in _ morph occurs between R =50 -100 , also consistent with such a distortion [ Fig. fig:s13:4736 (b) ] .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4736 with a thin-disk mass reconstruction, finding its rotation curve and luminosity can be matched without a massive dark halo.

quotes.quote: As an example, the method is applied to galaxy NGC 4736; its rotation curve does not allow one to employ a model with a massive spherical halo. We find a global mass distribution in this galaxy (without non-baryonic dark matter) that agrees perfectly with the high resolution rotation curve of the galaxy. This mass distribution is consistent with the I band luminosity profile with the mean mass-to-light ratio M/L_I=1.2 , and also agrees with the amount of hydrogen observed in the outermost regions of the galaxy.

Rotation of the spiral galaxy NGC 4736 can be fully understood in the framework of Newtonian physics. We have found a mass distribution in the galaxy that agrees perfectly with its high-resolution rotation curve, agrees with the I band luminosity distribution giving low mass-to-light ratio of 1.2 in this band at total mass of 3.43 10^ 10 M_ , and is consistent with the amount of observed in the remote parts of the galaxy, leaving not much room (if any) for dark matter.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4736 to show that a thin disk mass model can fit the rotation curve and outer hydrogen without requiring significant dark matter.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 is an example of a spiral galaxy with a rotation curve of which outer parts cannot be satisfactorily reconstructed when a spherical halo is assumed. By a simple examination one finds that in the circular orbit approximation, the rotation curve of this galaxy cannot be created by a spherical matter distribution. By applying the thin disk model, with no constraining assumptions about the mass-to-light ratio profile, one can find a mass profile in this galaxy that perfectly conforms with its rotation curve, and agrees with the amount of hydrogen observed at large radii, beyond the cutoff radius bib:bratek . Only insignificant (if any) amount of dark matter is required, whereas the customary models predict the galaxy to be dark matter dominated.

sources.summary: The paper applies PCA Tomography to NGC 4736, describing its aging-starburst LINER nucleus and finding a bona fide type 1 AGN displaced from the stellar bulge.

quotes.quote: With the aim of illustrating the PCA Tomography method, we have applied it to a Gemini GMOS--IFU datacube of the nuclear region of the nearby LINER galaxy NGC 4736 (M94). The LINER characteristics of NGC 4736 are considered to be related to an atypical population of stars, as it is an aging starburst galaxy eracleous, cid . Applying the PCA methodology, we show that it has a bona fide type 1 Active Galactic Nucleus -- AGN -- displaced from the centre of its stellar bulge.

With traditional spectroscopic techniques it would be difficult to show the existence of the BLR/AGN in NGC 4736; even more difficult would be to determine the position of the BLR with the accuracy we obtained here.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 4736 lacks outer CO velocity-dispersion coverage, is excluded from disk averages, and may be the only sample galaxy where cloud-cloud shielding matters.

quotes.quote: However, the maps of NGC 4736 and NGC 4826 contain no data at larger radii, and so for these two galaxies we quote disk velocity dispersions excluding only a central 9 pixel diameter region. The disk velocity dispersions of these two galaxies are likely biased to be somewhat higher than those of the other galaxies in our sample and thus we do not include them in calculating the average outer disk velocity dispersion.

Only in NGC 4736, where the surface density may be as high as 3 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 assuming a CO J=3-2/J=1-0 line ratio of 0.3 and a CO to H _2 conversion factor of 2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (K km s ^ 1 ) ^ 1 s88 , is it possible the clouds may shield one another to some degree. However, the actual surface density depends sensitively on the value that we assume for the CO J=3-2/J=1-0 line ratio; if we adopt a value of 0.6 which is more appropriate for the dense gas, rather than the value of 0.3 found in a direct comparison of CO J=3-2 and J=1-0 maps of NGLS galaxies w09,w10 , then the projected disk-averaged column density is similar to that of individual GMCs.

sources.summary: NGC 4736 is discussed as having a radio ring matching its bright inner pseudoring, tied to intense star formation, Faraday rotation, and ringed-galaxy magnetic-field studies.

quotes.quote: Star Formation Rings Chyzy08 obtained 8.46 and 4.86 GHz data on NGC 4736 that showed a distinct ring of total radio emission precisely corresponding to the the bright inner pseudoring visible in other wavelengths. They described the approximately 47" radius ring as a well-defined zone of intense active star formation visible in optical, H_ , UV emission Waller01 and infrared and CO maps Wong00 and found the observed distribution of Faraday rotation suggested the possible action of large-scale MHD dynamo. They suggest galaxies of ringed morphology and resonant dynamics can be excellent targets to investigate questions of galactic magnetism. NGC 4736 is shown as FCG J125053.0+410713 (Group 376229) in Figure typical_ring .

sources.summary: The paper places this THINGS low-surface-brightness galaxy in Group A, finding fitted BDM cores with r_s exceeding r_c, typical transition energy near 0.11 eV, and BDM fits preferred over other cored profiles.

quotes.quote: We obtain seven galaxies (DDO 154 (A1), NGC 2841 (A2), NGC 3031 (A3), NGC 3621 (A4), NGC 4736 (A5), NGC 6946 (A6), and NGC 7793 (A7)) (we call Group A) with fitted values r_s > r_c 0 that are shown in Table tab:onlydm_bdm . For these galaxies we obtain average values for: the core r_c 40 pc , r_s 5 kpc , and the energy E_c 0.11 \ eV for the min.disk analysis, that are typical values for a galaxy, except for DDO 154 which has a larger core, r_c = 1.35 kpc . We notice that for all these galaxies the ^2 in BDM is smaller than NFW's but the extra parameter ( r_c ) in BDM makes the reduced ^2_ red equivalent for both profiles. When comparing the BDM profile with the other two cored profiles, Burkert and ISO (Ref. ) we conclude that BDM is still better.

sources.summary: The paper reports no XMM-Newton spectral variations for NGC 4736, avoids cross-instrument comparison due to annular contamination, and finds weak light-curve and U-band variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 : Variations are not found from XMM -Newton data, i.e., SMF0 is used in a period of four years. Chandra and XMM -Newton data are not compared since the emission from the annular region contributes with 84 in Chandra data. From the analysis of the light curves, variations in the soft, hard, and total bands are obtained below 2 confidence level in all cases. At UV frequencies, a variation of 66 is obtained from the U filter.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 4736 with an absorbed power law plus hot gas and finds a photon index close to previous studies.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 Only a single summed spectrum of NGC 315 could be produced from the available Swift data. We find an absorbed power law plus a hot gas component to be the best-fitting to the Swift data. Previous studies successfully also model the spectrum of NGC 4736 with an absorbed power law and a hot gas component e.g. Roberts2001, Terashima2002 . We find a very similar value for the photon index to previous studies ( = 1.45 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4736 requires a broad H component near the BLR threshold and has narrow-component line ratios consistent with AGN ionization.

quotes.quote: For H a broad component of _ B 740 km s ^ 1 is needed (see Sect. C15 and Fig. Panel_NGC4736 ). The FWHM of this component is in the limit of being associated with the BLR of the galaxy, which is also the case for NGC 4676B (see below).
The broad component for NGC 4736 contributes significantly to the total flux of both H and the whole H [ N II ] complex, as can be seen in Fig. Panel_C15 .

This is true for all objects except for NGC 4736, which is the only galaxy for which both [ N II ] /H and [ S II ] /H line ratios of the narrow component are consistent with being produced by the AGN, as mentioned above.

sources.summary: The paper treats M94 as a satellite-deficient Milky-Way analog, but finds its low satellite abundance can fit model scatter once host mass is considered.

quotes.quote: In a focused study, smercina2018 found only two satellites with M_V<-9 in the inner projected 150 kpc volume around the MW-analog M94 (compared to seven in this range found around the MW). They argue that common AM relations applied to the DMO results from the EAGLE Project schaye2015 have far too little scatter to explain M94's anemic satellite population. smercina2018 suggest that significantly increasing the scatter in the stellar-halo mass relation (SHMR) could explain M94's satellite system.
While M94 is clearly a deficient satellite system smercina2018 , it is still within the 2 spread of the models.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4736 as an unbarred star-forming ring galaxy, with marginal outer-ring detection and a bright inner pseudo-ring clearly visible in SCUBA-2 data.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 NGC 4736 (Messier 94), shown in Figure fig:ngc4736 , is an actively star-forming unbarred ring galaxy. The galaxy has a weak and diffuse outer ring, which is at best marginally detected in our SCUBA-2 observations, and a bright and actively star-forming inner pseudo-ring wong2000, waller2001 . This inner pseudo-ring, with a radius of 47^ chyzy2008 , is clearly visible in our SCUBA-2 data. HARP ^ 12 CO J=3 2 observations of NGC 4736 were presented by wilson2012 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4736 as a spiral with an inner bar, uncertain inner geometry, low radial velocity, and coherent anisotropies preserving circular symmetry.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 NGC 4736 is a spiral galaxy with a bar within the inner disk. The inclination angle measured by the TRM is i=44^ for R>50'' ; at smaller radii, both the inclination angle and the P.A. show large variations, likely due to the TRM's difficulty in handling galaxies with small inclination angles (see Fig. NGC4736 ). These fluctuations are well inside the optical radius R_ 25 230'' , cause the transversal velocity from the VRM to differ from the circular velocity from the TRM in the inner disk, while they are closer at larger radii.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4736 as having molecular gas in a central bar and spiral arms, with enhanced CO-to-radio ratios linked to the northwestern arm and bar ends.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 . The molecular gas in this nearby source is distributed in a central molecular bar and in two tightly wound spiral arms which are associated with a bright ring of star formation (Wong Blitz 2000). A background radio continuum source, apparent in the upper left panel of Figure 1( d ), has been omitted from the analysis in this paper. As discussed in 4.3, there appears to be an enhancement in \ (Figure figuraq ) associated with the northwestern spiral arm; the enhancement may be a result of the starburst there. It also appears that \ is larger at the bar ends, at roughly 15 \ from the galaxy center, and along the ``arclike'' extensions from the bar that probably are associated with the beginnings of the spiral structure (Wong Blitz 2000).

sources.summary: The paper surveys M94 for planetary nebulae, reports 150 candidates in the six-galaxy sequence, and derives a 4.4 Mpc PNLF distance consistent with literature values.

quotes.quote: As the first step to determine disk mass-to-light ratios for normal spiral galaxies, we present the results of an imaging survey for planetary nebulae (PNe) in six nearby, face-on systems: IC 342, M74 (NGC 628), M83 (NGC 5236), M94 (NGC 4736), NGC 5068, and NGC 6946. Using Blanco/Mosaic II and WIYN/OPTIC, we identify 165, 153, 241, 150, 19, and 71 PN candidates, respectively, and use the Planetary Nebula Luminosity Function (PNLF) to obtain distances. For M74 and NGC 5068, our distances of 8.6 0.3 Mpc and 5.4^ +0.2 _ 0.4 Mpc are the first reliable estimates to these objects; for IC 342 ( 3.5 0.3 Mpc), M83 ( 4.8 0.1 Mpc), M94 ( 4.4^ +0.1 _ 0.2 Mpc), and NGC 6946 ( 6.1 0.6 Mpc) our values agree well with those in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4736 as a spiral galaxy whose resonance regions align with rings and gaps, including H II-region rings, corotation gaps, and stable inner and outer rings at ILR/OLR.

quotes.quote: As an example, Schommer_Sullivan1976 argued for the relation between the resonance regions and the ring-like structures observed in the spiral galaxy NGC 4736. The authors showed that the position of the 2:1 ILR is coincident with a ring-like zone of H II regions, the corotation is associated with a gap in the optical brightness and in H I , and also the 2:1 OLR coincides with the position of a faint outer ring of stars and H I , and also possibly a ring of H II regions. Later on, Gu1996 conducted gas cloud-particle simulations and showed that both inner and outer rings in NGC 4736 are stable structures located respectively at the ILR and OLR.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4736's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 is a spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 4.9 0.8 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus classified as a LINER ho_search_1997-1 . There are indications of a past powerful starburst in the nucleus walker_2_1988,taniguchi_poststarburst_1996 and a nuclear star cluster gonzalez_delgado_hst/wfpc2_2008 , which could also explain the observed optical emission lines. However, the existence of a compact source in radio, UV and X-ray together with the H properties favour the presence of a low-luminosity AGN in NGC 4736 nagar_radio_2005,kording_radio_2005,gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2009,masegosa_nature_2011 .

sources.summary: NGC 4736 requires a relatively small transition radius for its UV data, may have old stellar optical emission, and has X-rays too hard for a jet model.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 As was the case of NGC 1097, NGC 4143 and NGC 4278, this LINER requires a relatively small transition radius in order to explain its UV data. The 3300 \ observation can also be reproduced by a 1.2 10^8 M_ stellar population. The X-ray spectrum is too hard to be explained by a jet model (Figure fig:n4736 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4736 as one of two THINGS galaxies with steeply declining H i rotation curves; its central H i absence leaves the inner slope poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: We therefore fit the polyex model to the two most steeply declining H i rotation curves in the THINGS sample deblok08 , namely NGC 4736 and NGC 2903. Unfortunately, for both these galaxies the inner slope is not tightly constrained (for NGC 4736 due to absence of H i in the center; for NGC 2903 due to streaming motions induced by the central bar), leading to some degeneracy between the r_ PE and fit parameters. For NGC 4736 we find values of between 0.001 and 0.002 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4736 as having steeply declining star formation, radial cold-dust temperature dependence, and strong similarity between heating maps and temperature maps.

quotes.quote: The most noticeable offsets occur in NGC 1097, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055; in our sample, these galaxies exhibit some of the steepest declines in star formation from the center to the outskirts.

When both parameters are fitted simultaneously, the cold dust temperature maps for 10 galaxies (NGC 1097, NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4569, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7331) exhibit a strong radial dependence.

As might be expected, we see a strong similarity between the heating maps and the T_c maps, particularly for NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4321, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055, similar to the correlation between resolved 24 m emission and the cold dust temperature found for NGC 891 hughes2014 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4736 as a bulge-contributing disk, notes discrepant inner H2 surface densities, and compares its H2 radial profile with literature results.

quotes.quote: For example, the inner H _2 surface densities in NGC 4736 and NGC 5055 that were calculated by differ from results by one order of magnitude.

We want our model of the exponential sphere to have the same circular velocity as the (bulge) model of exponential disc of for each galaxy with significant contribution from the bulge (NGC 2841, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, NGC 6946, and NGC 7331).
For completeness, we also compared our _ H _2 radial profile for NGC 2403, NGC 4736, and NGC 5055 with those reported by .

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4736 as requiring a non-unity warm-gas fraction and says a two-phase model reproduces its observed energy profile.

quotes.quote: NGC 2403 and NGC 4736, which we discuss below, fall into the former case, while the rest of the galaxies in the sample is compatible with having f_ w 1 , hence the resulting efficiencies are equivalent to those in Table tab:all_eta for the warm atomic gas.
fig:2phase_panels show the result of this analysis for NGC 2403 and NGC 4736: the profiles of the observed energy are remarkably well reproduced by the two-phase best model, for both galaxies.
We obtain that the best model is given by _ atom,2ph 0.021 and f_ w 0.55 (or _ th 6.1 ) for NGC 2403, and _ atom,2ph 0.029 and f_ w 0.35 (or _ th 4.9 ) for NGC 4736.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4736 among the lowest-valued massive galaxies, gives it lower mass accretion rates than prior modeling, and reports a CO conversion factor five times another estimate.

quotes.quote: The of NGC 2841, NGC 3198 and NGC 4736 are the lowest of all galaxies, with values below 30.

The models of 6 out of 17 galaxies have more than three times lower mass accretion rates than those of the 2011AJ....141...24V model for the following reasons: in NGC 5055, NGC 4736, and NGC 7331 2011AJ....141...24V used the observed H i velocity dispersions of ( 2009AJ....137.4424T ), which are more than 5 km s ^ 1 higher than those obtained by our model.
There is a big discrepancy for NGC 4736 where our CO weighted conversion factor is five times higher than that of 2013ApJ...777....5S .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4736 as a strong example where high central mid-infrared brightness coexists with low F15/F7, consistent with an older central stellar population.

quotes.quote: A strinking example of this is NGC 4736. Its central mid-infrared brightness is in the high range, but its central F_ 15 /F_7 ratio is low, in accordance with its large mean stellar age confirmed by Taniguchi . From optical population synthesis, they find that a central starburst occurred about 1 Gyr ago in this galaxy, and that subsequent nuclear star formation has proceeded at a low rate.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4736 as a LINER with compact radio detections and HST UV flux measurements for SED analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 4736 - VLA measurements with a resolution of 0 15 reveal an unresolved nuclear source with a flux of 1.7 mJy at 2 cm (Nagar et al. 2005). At 3.5 cm, with a resolution of 0 24 , K ording et al. (2005) also find a flux of 1.7 mJy. Maoz et al. (1996) used the HST/FOC in 1993 to measure a 2300 AA flux of at 19 10^ 17 .

gmw_00000027.name: IRAS 23365+3604
gmw_00000027.overview: IRAS 23365+3604 is an advanced, late-stage galaxy merger [arXiv:1012.5899, arXiv:1612.08181] classified as a LINER-type ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1612.08181]. Its 200-parsec nuclear region hosts a starburst [arXiv:1012.5899, arXiv:1201.5021], active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1201.5021, arXiv:1612.08181], and massive molecular outflows [arXiv:1710.02356, arXiv:2009.07293].

sources.summary: The paper presents EVN observations of the advanced-merger ULIRG IRAS 23365+3604, mapping its nuclear starburst, extended radio emission, and candidate core-collapse supernovae.

quotes.quote: IRAS 23365+3604 ( = 23 ^h 39 ^m 01 7, = 36 21^ 14^ ), hereafter IRAS 2336, is an advanced merger at a distance of 252 Mpc (1 mas 0.8 pc) with a very high luminosity (log(L _ FIR / L _ ) = 12.13) which corresponds to a CCSN rate of 5 SN/year . We present multi-epoch, -frequency EVN observations of the ULIRG IRAS 2336 (see Figure fig:epochs ). Putative young radio SNe with typical peak luminosities of L 10 ^ 27-28 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 should have a flux of 0.01 to 0.1 mJy at the distance of IRAS 2336.

Our images reveal the presence of a nuclear starburst with an approximate extension of 200 pc. We note that at 18 cm, the size of the nuclear region is larger than at 6 cm, for all the epochs. This can be explained by the longer lifetime of electrons at lower frequencies .

We have also detected some sources at 18 cm with emission > 5 (L 10 ^ 27-28 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 ) with no counterpart at 6 cm. These are candidates of CCSNe exploding in the circumnuclear region (see sources labelled as probable SNe in Figure fig:epochs ).

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 23365 + 3604 as a ULIRG with compact and extended radio emission, a 200 pc two-component nucleus, AGN/SN activity, radiative losses, and strong magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution, high-sensitivity radio images of the ultra-luminous infrared galaxy (ULIRG) IRAS 23365 + 3604. We performed contemporaneous observations at 1.7 and 5.0 GHz, in three epochs separated by one year from each other, with the European very long baseline interferometry Network (EVN). We also present complementary Multi-Element Radio Linked Interferometry Network (MERLIN) at 1.6 and 5.0 GHz, and archival Very Large Array (VLA) data, taken at 1.4 and 4.9 GHz. We find that the emission at 5.0 GHz remains quite compact as seen at different resolutions, whereas at 1.7 GHz, high resolution imaging reveals some extended structure.

The nuclear region has an approximate linear size of 200 pc and shows the presence of two main emission components: i) one with a composite spectrum due to ongoing non-thermal activity (probably due to recently exploded supernovae and AGN activity), ii) another one with a steep spectrum, likely dominated by an old population of radio emitters, such as supernova remnants (SNRs). Radiative losses are important, so re-acceleration or replenishment of new electrons is necessary. We estimate a magnetic field strength of 18 G at galactic, and 175 G at nuclear scales, which are typical for galaxies in advanced mergers.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 23365+3604 as a LINER-type late-stage merger with tidal tails, about half AGN luminosity, OH P-Cygni absorption, and multiple outflow components.

quotes.quote: IRAS 23365+3604 : This LINER-type ULIRG shows no evidence of a double nucleus dow98 , and the tidal tails observed in the optical mur96 suggest a post-coalescence, late-stage merger martin16 . About half of its luminosity is associated with an AGN vei13 . Besides OH119 and OH79, OH84 also shows a P-Cygni profile in this source, and the OH65 peak absorption is blueshifted by 100 \ relative to [ C ii ] 157 m. OH119 shows blue absorption up to 1000 \ and possibly more, but the other doublets only show absorption up to 500 . Our best fit model involves three outflowing components, with the CORE component outflowing at 100 \ ( M 340 M _ yr ^ 1 ).

They combine to give a total P _ out 7.5 10^ 35 dyn, 4 the momentum rate supplied by the AGN. If a compact starburst is considered, the outflow in IRAS 23365+3604 requires little momentum boost for both components ( 3 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses IRAS 23365+3604 as a moderate local ULIRG outflow template, predicting OH P-Cygni and excited-gas signatures detectable by SPICA to at least z about 1.3.

quotes.quote: We adopt 3 local template ULIRGs to make predictions on the detectability of molecular outflows at high- z , by scaling the far-IR spectroscopic observations obtained with Herschel /PACS: IRAS 03158+4227, Mrk 231, and IRAS 23365+3604 (GA17). The IR luminosities ( 8-1000 m, L_ IR ) are listed in Table tab1 . All three sources, as well as many other local ULIRGs, show clear evidence for massive molecular outflows from P-Cygni line profiles in the ground-state OH119 and OH79 doublets e.g. fis10 .

Strong outflow sources as IRAS 03158+4227 and Mrk 231 would be detected up to the maximum observable redshift (set by the instrumental limit), and more moderate outflow sources like IRAS 23365+3604 would be detectable up to at least z 1.3 . The upper-right panel of Fig. oh79_lr shows that the LR mode predicts a clear P-Cygni line shape for the OH79 doublet in outflowing sources, thus unambiguously revealing massive molecular outflows In sources like IRAS 23365+3604, signal will be obtained in two consecutive channels in absorption and emission, and outflow detection at 4 level will rely on 3 detection in both channels. .

sources.summary: The paper lists IRAS 23365+3604 with OH79 and OH119 P-Cygni outflow signatures, but rejects a central-spaxel OH84 P-Cygni classification used by another study.

quotes.quote: Out of these 19 galaxies, five (IRAS 01003-2238, IRAS 17208-0014, IRAS 23365+3604, Mrk 273, and NGC 6240) have two OH transitions that show a P-Cygni feature and one (Mrk 231) contains three transitions that display it.

We note that gon17a identifies the PACS observation for OH84 in IRAS 23365+3604 as a P-Cygni feature, but we do not. The key reason for this difference is that gon17a uses a 3 3 spaxel area for their spectra, while we only use the central spaxel. This means that if the P-Cygni feature is tracing a molecular outflow in the galaxy, it has left the central region of the galaxy.

gmw_00000028.name: 4C 35.06
gmw_00000028.overview: 4C 35.06 is an extended, radio-loud active galactic nucleus located at the center of the Abell 407 galaxy cluster [arXiv:1411.7261, arXiv:1607.05080]. It features a helically twisted, precessing jet system spanning up to 400 kpc [arXiv:1411.7261, arXiv:1607.05080] and contains steep-spectrum relic radio lobes from multiple episodes of activity [arXiv:1504.06642, arXiv:1607.05080].

sources.summary: The paper reports GMRT observations of 4C 35.06, describing a helically twisted, precessing kpc-scale radio jet from a faint AGN host in Abell 407's merging central region.

quotes.quote: We report GMRT observation of the strong radio source 4C 35.06 , an extended ( z=0.047 ) radio-loud AGN at the center of galaxy cluster Abell 407 . The radio map at 610 MHz reveal a striking, helically twisted jet system emanating from an optically faint AGN host. The radio morhology closely resembles the precessing jets of the galactic microquasar SS 433 .

In this study we focus mainly on the morphology of the jet from the radio source 4C 35.06 hosted by the cluster Abell 407 . The low frequency maps from GMRT data show extended faint diffuse emission from the jet structure of 4C 35.06 . The 610 MHz radio map shows a jet with helically twisted morphology and with a size estimate of approximately 250 kpc , closely resembling that of the well known microquasar SS 433 (Blundell and Bowler 2004).

Results and conclusion :The radio source 4C 35.06 hosts a precessing kpc scale jet similar to that of the microquasar SS 433 , and is seen to be emanating from the faintest galactic member. 4C 35.06 underscores the paradigm that despite the vast difference in scales, jet phenomena in radio loud AGNs keep a striking similarity with that in microquasars.

sources.summary: The paper studies 4C 35.06 as a complex radio galaxy in Abell 407, tracing relic plasma, recurrent AGN activity, helical emission, and broad H I absorption against the source center.

quotes.quote: Using observations obtained with the LOw Fequency ARray (LOFAR), the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT) and archival Very Large Array (VLA) data, we have traced the radio emission to large scales in the complex source 4C 35.06 located in the core of the galaxy cluster Abell 407. At higher spatial resolution ( 4 ), the source was known to have two inner radio lobes spanning 31 kpc and a diffuse, low-brightness extension running parallel to them, offset by about 11 kpc (in projection). At 62 MHz, we detect the radio emission of this structure extending out to 210 kpc. At 1.4 GHz and intermediate spatial resolution ( 30 ), the structure appears to have a helical morphology.

We show that the source morphology is most likely the result of at least two episodes of AGN activity separated by a dormant period of around 35 Myr. The outermost regions of radio emission have a steep spectral index ( < -1 ), indicative of old plasma. We connect the spectral index properties of the resolved source structure with the integrated flux density spectral index of 4C 35.06 and suggest an explanation for its unusual integrated flux density spectral shape (a moderately steep power law with no discernible spectral break), possibly providing a proxy for future studies of more distant radio sources through inferring their detailed spectral index properties and activity history from their integrated spectral indices.
Using the the WSRT, we detect \ in absorption against the center of the radio source. The absorption profile is relatively broad (FWHM of 288 ), similar to what is found in other clusters.

sources.summary: The paper studies 4C 35.06 as a radio-loud AGN with twisted 400 kpc jets, steep relic lobes, and jet precession tied to merger-driven black hole activity.

quotes.quote: We report the results of our radio, optical and infra-red studies of a peculiar radio source 4C 35.06, an extended radio-loud AGN at the center of galaxy cluster Abell 407 ( z=0.047 ).

Multifrequency radio observations of 4C 35.06 with the Giant Meterwave Radio Telescope (GMRT) at 610, 235 and 150 MHz reveal a system of 400 kpc scale helically twisted and kinked radio jets and outer diffuse lobes. The outer extremities of jets contain extremely steep spectrum (spectral index -1.7 to -2.5) relic/fossil radio plasma with a spectral age of a few (10^7 - 10^8) yr. Such ultra-steep spectrum relic radio lobes without definitive hot-spots are rare, and they provide an opportunity to understand the life-cycle of relativistic jets and physics of black hole mergers in dense environments. We interpret our observations of this radio source in the context of the growth of its central black hole, triggering of its AGN activity and jet precession, all possibly caused by galaxy mergers in this dense galactic system. A slow conical precession of the jet axis due to gravitational perturbation between interacting black holes is invoked to explain the unusual jet morphology.

gmw_00000029.name: 3C 273
gmw_00000029.overview: 3C 273 is a nearby flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1012.2980, arXiv:2212.07061] hosted by an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0808.0918, arXiv:1803.01855]. It features a one-sided relativistic jet [arXiv:1602.01654, arXiv:2212.07061] and exhibits multiwavelength variability [arXiv:0912.2266, arXiv:2105.07030].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 273 as a well-studied quasar with a massive SMBH and variable jet-dominated X-ray emission, then models its SED to predict possible VHE inverse-Compton or synchrotron behavior.

quotes.quote: 3C 273 3C 273 z=0.158, 1992ApJS...83...29S is the first quasar that was identified as a high-redshift object 1963Natur.197.1040S and the best studied. It hosts a SMBH whose mass is at least 2.0 10^9 M_ as inferred from studies of Balmer lines 2005A&A...435..811P and its maximum acceptable viewing angle is about 15 1985ApJ...289..109U . Blazars display featureless X-ray contribution but radiogalaxies can have a much more complex environment at low energies e.g. 2006ApJ...642..113G . However since the purpose here is to model the non-thermal contributions of these objects, we decided to consider the X-ray contribution as dominated by the jet emission, keeping only a feature in soft X-ray as a signature of the accretion disk 2007ApJ...659..235G . Since this source is highly variable, one needs to be careful to select simultaneous data.

The nature of the X-ray emission of 3C 273 is still an unresolved issue. The high frequency bump, thought to be probably due to IC emission, would present a peak at a rather low frequency compared to other AGNs. This implies that either 3C 273 does not emit at detectable VHE levels, or that the nature of this bump is synchrotron, implying then the presence of a hypothetical IC bump at ultra high energies. Assuming that the hard X-ray emission is indeed due to inverse Compton emission, the solid blue line in Fig. fig:3C273_SED shows the SED of 3C 273 with the predicted VHE flux (see column 5 of Table tab:param for the corresponding parameters).

sources.summary: 3C 273 is described as a bright blazar whose PSD resembles soft-state Seyferts, with a 10-day bend timescale and scaling behavior requiring no relativistic time-dilation correction.

quotes.quote: 3C 273 3C 273 is one of the brightest AGN in the sky and, as such, has been observed by all major observatories since the 1970s. We have collected these data, including extensive observations with , and the long term light curve is shown in Fig fig:273 a. Using PSRESP , in our normal manner, I have made the PSD which is shown, unfolded from the distortions introduced by the sampling pattern, in Fig fig:273 b. It can be seen that this PSD is exactly like that of a soft state Seyfert galaxy PSD. The bend timescale is 10 days.

It can be seen that 3C 273 fits the scaling relationship between , and as well as all the Seyfert galaxies. We note that there is no necessity to alter the observed bend timescale by any relativistic time dilation factor to make the observed bend timescale fit with the relationship derived for Seyfert galaxies and BHBs. For a clock moving with the jet of 3C 273, that factor would be about 10.

sources.summary: The paper presents Fermi LAT observations of 3C 273's September 2009 gamma-ray outbursts, describing it as a nearby flat-spectrum radio quasar with record fluxes, hardened spectra, and asymmetric six-hour variability.

quotes.quote: We present the light curves and spectral data of two exceptionally luminous gamma-ray outburts observed by the Large Area Telescope (LAT) experiment on board Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope from 3C 273 in September 2009. During these flares, having a duration of a few days, the source reached its highest ray flux ever measured. This allowed us to study in some details their spectral and temporal structures. The rise and decay are asymmetric on timescales of 6 hours, and the spectral index was significantly harder during the flares than during the preceding 11 months. We also found that short, very intense flares put out the same time-integrated energy as long, less intense flares like that observed in August 2009.

We have now observed such events in 3C273, the nearest quasar. , the first quasar discovered by Schimdt (1963) and the first extragalactic source detected by COS B in the gamma-ray band (Swanenburg et al. 1978), is one of the most extensively studied AGN across the entire electromagnetic spectrum. It is classified as a flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) and has a redshift z=0.158 (see e.g., Strauss et al., 1992).

sources.summary: The paper applies a lag-based jet-emission method to quasar 3C 273, using its light curves, jet properties, BLR size, and radio-line lags to infer pc-scale radio/ray emitting regions.

quotes.quote: 3C 273 was first identified as a quasar at redshift z=0.158 by b74 . It is one of the best studied AGNs in all bands see e.g. b53,b90,b85,b80 . The database site http://isdc.unige.ch/3c273/ of 3C 273 provides a series of about 70 light curves as well as some spectra. The jet of 3C 273 is one-sided, with no signs of emission from the counterjet side b88 .

The blue-bump of 3C 273 is thermal continuum emission from the inner accretion disc b75 . Fe K lines observed in 3C 273 were shown to be from an accretion disc around a supermassive black hole b95,b84 .
The method is applied to FSRQ 3C 273. Because the ray light curves are very sparsely sampled for 3C 273, it should be unreliable to employ them to estimate the time lags.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 273 as a well-studied extragalactic quasar whose saturated core, host-galaxy structure, spiral features, and possible merger origin could be tested by NIRISS AMI.

quotes.quote: 3C 273: The original quasar 3C 273 was the first quasar recognized to be extra-galactic sch63 , lying at z=0.158 (749 Mpc luminosity distance; 1 =2.7 kpc linear scale) and is well studied at all wavelengths e.g. sold08 . In most bands, the quasar core is saturated. So, modelling the underlying quasar environment and host galaxy requires PSF subtraction e.g. bahcall95,hutch04 . bahcall97 find agreement with an underlying E4 host galaxy from fitting the residual intensity in regions >1 after subtraction of a best-fit stellar PSF. However, any structure inside 1 will complicate both the subtraction of a stellar PSF and the model-dependent interpretation of the quasar host galaxy. The highest angular resolution study of 3C 273 to date was with the HST-ACS coronagraph martel03 , down to an IWA of 1300 mas.

NIRISS AMI can probe a factor of 20 closer (75-600 mas) than the HST-ACS coronagraph (and a factor of 3 closer than coronagraphs on JWST). NIRISS AMI can test whether spiral structure at 1 which wraps around the quasar martel03 continues inwards, possibly fuelling the quasar. NIRISS AMI can also test a merger origin for the quasar in departures from a smooth light profile at <1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C273 as the baseline FSRQ for a time-dependent leptonic blazar model, fitting its SED and observational constraints before simulating multiwavelength flares.

quotes.quote: The quasar 3C273 is used as an illustrative example. Our code takes into account Fermi-II acceleration and all relevant electron cooling terms, including the external radiation fields generally found to be important in the modeling of the SEDs of FSRQs, as well as synchrotron self absorption and pair-production. General parameters are constrained through a fit to the average spectral energy distribution (SED) of the blazar by numerically solving the time-dependent Fokker-Planck equation for the electron evolution in a steady-state situation. We then apply perturbations to several input parameters (magnetic field, particle injection luminosity, acceleration time scale) to simulate flaring events and compute time-dependent SEDs and light curves in representative energy bands (radio, optical, X-rays, rays).

Specifically, for 3C273, we have the following observables see for references to the observational data Boettcher13 : z = 0.158 , _ , app = 13 (the apparent transverse velocity of individual jet components, normalized to the speed of light), t_ var 1 d, L_ disk = 1.3 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , and L_ BLR = 9.1 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 . The observed apparent superluminal speed implies a limit to the bulk Lorentz factor of > 13 . We choose the observing angle as _ obs = 1/ so that = , and the size of the emission region is then constrained by Equ. ( Rconstraint ). In the SED of 3C273, the accretion disk component is directly visible as a prominent Big Blue Bump, which facilitates reliable estimates of the black hole mass and Eddington ratio, l_ Edd = L_ disk / L_ Edd .

sources.summary: The paper reconstructs 3C273's discovery as the first quasar, emphasizing Parkes/Palomar radio position and optical identification plus occultation evidence for a double, core-jet radio source.

quotes.quote: We are undertaking a detailed investigation, based on the available evidence, of the sequence of events that led to the historical discovery of the first quasar, 3C273. History and philosophy of astronomy, quasars: individual (3C273), occultations The pages of Nature for March 16, 1963 carried two short papers which announced the precise position, structure, and the optical identification and redshift measurement of the radio source 3C273, the first quasar.

3C273 is clearly a double source at 410 MHz, aligned so that both components reappear together along P.A. \ 45 degrees. However, the immersion record at 136 MHz shows no evidence for a second component as is shown at 410 MHz.

For both the August and October 1962 occultations, Hazard's 3C273 analysis had revealed a ``core-jet'' structure with ``normal'' steep-spectrum component A and an inverted-spectrum, very compact component B. The final image and positional information was determined by Hazard in collaboration with W.Nicholson of the H.M. Nautical Almanac. The detailed arcsecond resolution image is given here in Figure 4, taken from Hazard et al. \ (1963).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 3C 273 from radio to gamma rays, finding two high-energy components: Seyfert-like X-rays and blazar-like jet gamma rays with variability tied to electron changes.

quotes.quote: The high energy spectrum of 3C 273 is usually understood in terms of inverse-Compton emission in a relativistic leptonic jet. This model predicts variability patterns and delays that could be tested with simultaneous observations from the radio to the GeV range. The instruments IBIS, SPI, JEM-X on board INTEGRAL, PCA on board RXTE, and LAT on board Fermi have enough sensitivity to follow the spectral variability of 3C 273 from the keV to the GeV. We looked for correlations between the different energy bands, including radio data at 37 GHz collected at the \ Radio Observatory and built quasi-simultaneous multiwavelength spectra in the high energy domain when the source is flaring either in the X-rays or in the rays.

Both temporal and spectral analysis suggest a two-component model to explain the complete high energy spectrum. X-ray emission is likely dominated by a Seyfert-like component while the ray emission is dominated by a blazar-like component produced by the relativistic jet. The variability of the blazar-like component is discussed, comparing the spectral parameters in the two different spectral states. Changes of the electron Lorentz factor are found to be the most likely source of the observed variability.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 273 as a nearby blazar with stacked VLBI evidence for a sheath and a modeled March 2010 orphan gamma-ray flare tied to blob ejection.

quotes.quote: For an initial test of our stacking procedure's ability to detect jet sheaths, we selected the blazar 3C 273. At a redshift of Z = 0.158 ( lavaux11 ), 3C 273 is one of the closest examples of a blazar, and as such is an ideal candidate to search for a jet sheath. As outlined in 3, we selected twenty relatively quiescent epochs of jet activity to create the stacked radio map shown in Figure fig3 . As predicted by the spine-sheath model, the linear polarization increases toward the edges of the jet downstream of the radio core. The EVPAs downstream are predominantly inclined to the jet spine (shown as a red line delineating the historical trajectories taken by blobs ejected from the radio core). A series of slices was taken through our stacked map to highlight the profiles of the Stokes parameters transverse to the jet axis. We detect an orphan ray flare in the Fermi LAT light curve during March 2010.

The VLBA images also exhibit the ejection of a blob from the radio core of 3C 273 during the onset of this orphan flare on March 6th 2010 25 days ( jorstad17 ), with a bulk Lorentz factor of 6 . The time of the blob ejection is demarcated by a vertical arrow in the lower panel of Figure fig4 . Table tab2 lists the Ring of Fire model parameters used to obtain a fit to this orphan ray flare.

sources.summary: RadioAstron observations of 3C 273 in a low state constrain its inner-jet brightness temperature and show the prior extreme value was a short-lived departure from equipartition.

quotes.quote: We present observations at 22 GHz of 3C 273, performed in 2014, designed to reach a maximum baseline of approximately nine Earth diameters. Reaching an angular resolution of 0.3 mas, we study a particularly low-activity state of the source, and estimate the nuclear region brightness temperature, comparing with the extreme one detected one year before during the RadioAstron early science period. We also make use of the VLBA-BU-BLAZAR survey data, at 43 GHz, to study the kinematics of the jet in a 1.5-year time window. We find that the nuclear brightness temperature is two orders of magnitude lower than the exceptionally high value detected in 2013 with RadioAstron at the same frequency (1.4 10^ 13 K, source-frame), and even one order of magnitude lower than the equipartition value.

The kinematics analysis at 43 GHz shows that a new component was ejected 2 months after the 2013 epoch, visible also in our 22 GHz map presented here. Consequently this was located upstream of the core during the brightness temperature peak. Fermi LAT observations for the period 2010-2014 do not show any ray flare in conjunction with the passage of the new component by the core at 43 GHz. These observations confirm that the previously detected extreme brightness temperature in 3C 273, exceeding the inverse Compton limit, is a short-lived phenomenon caused by a temporary departure from equipartition.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 3C 273 light curves across radio through X-ray bands, using multifractal methods to characterize its well-established flux variability and non-linear temporal correlations.

quotes.quote: The quasar 3C 273 is a non-extreme, but a very bright type of AGN 2007MNRAS.375.1521M . The quasar 3C 273 has been extensively studied on diverse time scales across the entire EM spectrum, with measurements made in single bands and often as well as in multi-bands 2014ApJS..213...26F,2009AJ....138.1428F,2010ApJ...714L..73A,2009A&A...494...49P,2009MNRAS.392.1181D,2008A&A...486..411S,2007MNRAS.375.1521M,2007A&A...465..147C,2006ApJ...648..900J,2006A&A...446...71S,2005ApJ...633L..85A,2002MNRAS.336..932K,2002A&A...390L..19G,2001ApJ...549L.161S,2000A&A...354..513C,2000A&A...354..497M . Blazars show rapid flux variability across the complete EM spectrum 2015MNRAS.451.1356K . Fluctuations ranging from a few tens of minutes (and sometimes even more rapid) to less than a day is often known as intra-day variability (IDV) 1995ARA&A..33..163W . It has been reported by 1990A&A...234...73C that the flux variation across the spectra of 3C 273 shows no simple correlation. It was also noticed that, at higher frequencies, the strength of the flux variations in 3C 273 are higher than in the lower frequencies e.g. 2009AJ....138.1428F .

For the present study, we have chosen some of the 3C 273 light-curves provided by Integral Science Data Centre (ISDC) http://www.isdc.unige.ch/3c273/ linked to the Astronomical Observatory of the University of Geneva 2008A&A...486..411S,1999A&AS..134...89T . Specifically, we have selected the time series covering the following wavebands: radio at 8 GHz, millimetre (mm) at 1.3 mm, infrared (IR) at 2.2 m, optical at 5479 , UV at 1525 , and X-ray at 5 keV. The referred data are displayed in Fig. fig1 , where the behaviour of Flux (in Jansky) versus Time (in days) is given for all the wavebands in consideration. The reason why we chose this candidate is that it is suitable for multifractal analysis due to the well-established flux fluctuation in its light-curves, in all observed spectrum regions.

sources.summary: The paper applies a stratified jet model to 3C 273, using its SED, external photon fields, superluminal motion, and broadband radio-to-gamma-ray emission as constraints.

quotes.quote: 3C 273 is the first quasar to have been identified thanks to its relatively close distance (redshift z = 0.158 , Schmidt:1963kd ) and has been extensively observed and studied. The broadband SED data used here come from ; they averaged over 30 years of observations and are more likely to represent the average state of the AGN. This quasar represents a good test for the model as this average state can be associated to a quiescent state that we can model.

Emission attributed to a hot corona is observed in the SED of 3C 273 in the X-ray band. Following who used a model of thermal comptonization to reproduce the hot corona emission, we add an emission following a power law of photon index =1.65 between 5 10^ 15 Hz and 5 10^ 19 Hz (0.02-200keV) as an extension of the accretion disc represented in Fig. fig:3C273_SED . These photons contribute as well to the inverse-Compton emission and Compton rocket process. Superluminal motion has been observed in 3C 273 by , proving the existence of relativistic motion in this jet. The deduced apparent velocity is v_ app 7.5 The authors compute an apparent velocity of 9.5c assuming a value for the Hubble constant H_0 = 55 km.s^ 1 .Mpc^ 1 which is now known to be closer to 70 km.s^ 1 .Mpc^ 1 . This imposes constraints on the observation angle and one can determine that i_ obs < 15 (or i_ obs > 0.96). Here we fixed i_ obs = 13 in agreement with these constraints. A best-fit of the model on the average data from is performed. Such a fit is a difficult task in this case as the model is computationally expensive and requires many parameters. We developed a method using a combination of genetic algorithms and gradient methods. As the source parameters have been fixed by observations, the free parameters of the model used for the fit are R_0 , n_0 , B_0 , Q_0 , , and . Results and discussion With the seven free parameters, the fit gives a reduced ^2 = 4.92 . The model accurately reproduces the entire SED of 3C273 from radio to gamma-rays except for the far radio below 10^8 Hz.

sources.summary: The paper uses a decade of reverberation mapping to characterize 3C 273’s BLR, deriving stratified lags, black-hole mass, host stellar mass, and agreement with the Magorrian relation.

quotes.quote: After its discovery Schmidt1963 , 3C 273 has been intensively studied over the whole range of the electromagnetic spectrum (from radio to ray bands) and has become one of the most representative quasars Cour1998 , as it shows most of the intriguing features of active galactic nuclei (AGNs), e.g., the jet and the large flux variations at all wavelengths. Although 3C 273 is classified as a blazar for its high-energy radiation above 100 MeV, it shows a prominent big blue bump and strong emission lines in the UV and optical bands. These coexistent characteristics make 3C 273 an interesting object Turler1999 .

Along with the UV lines, we find that 3C 273 has a stratified structure in its BLR, with higher-ionization lines arising from inner regions but lower-ionization lines from the outer part. The UV lines deviate significantly from the FWHM ^ 0.5 relation. Adopting a virial factor of f_ BLR = 1.3 , 3C 273 has a BH mass of = 4.1_ 0.4 ^ +0.3 10^8 M_ and is accreting with a rate of 9.3 L_ Edd ^ 2 , indicating that the black hole is undergoing intermediate super-Eddington accretion. From decomposition of its \ images, we obtain a host stellar mass of M_* = 10^ 11.3 0.7 M_ . We find that a ratio of BH mass and host spheroid ( 2 10^ 3 ) follows the Magorrian relation.

sources.summary: The paper maps 3C 273 with MUSE and ALMA, finding complex host-galaxy ionized gas, substantial molecular gas, a large ENLR, and a jet-driven hot gas cocoon affecting an inclined disk.

quotes.quote: In this article, we present a combined study of deep optical integral-field unit (IFU) observations of 3C 273 with the Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer MUSE, Bacon:2010 and CO(1-0) mapping with archival ALMA observations. The MUSE data reveal highly complex ionized gas motions in the host galaxy of 3C 273 and the ALMA observations highlight substantial amounts of molecular gas. We discuss our observations in the light of an expanding hot gas cocoon inflated by the radio jet which creates a fast outflow and shock front in the ISM.

In this article we presented the morpho-kinematic structure of the ionized and molecular gas around the low-redshift hyper-luminous quasar 3C 273 with VLT-MUSE and ALMA in unprecedented detail. We recover a large ENLR with 12.1 0.2 kpc in size which implies that either the ENLR size-luminosity relation has no break or the break radius is much larger than previously reported. It is possible that CCA plays a relevant role in the formation and properties of 3C 273 ENLR. Within the ENLR, a kinetically disturbed region (KDR) is discovered within the central 6 kpc.
We interpret this as a signature for an expanding jet-driven hot gas cocoon where the transverse shock is pushing the gas of an inclined gas disk back on the approaching side and up on receding side.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 273 as a long-studied quasar with optical variability, finding IDV dips, a 0.40-hour flare, and a Kerr black-hole mass upper bound.

quotes.quote: The variability of 3C 273, the first quasar discovered in 1963 SH63 , was extensively investigated from radio to rays e.g., Xi99,Fa09,Ab10a,Ab10c,Ka15,Xi17,Yu17 . 3C 273 was observed for more than 100 yr e.g., Vo13 , and optical variability on various timescales was reported. Fa09 reported the IDV timescales from 13 to 245 minutes for 3C 273. The variation timescales from 23.9 to 744 days were also found Fa14 .

A flare on 2011 May 10 shows R 0.05 0.01 mag in a duration of 0.40 hr for 3C 273. Sharp dips appear on 2011 May 9 for 3C 273, and show R 0.05 0.01 mag. Under the assumptions that the IDV is tightly connected to black hole mass, M_ , and that the flare durations are representative of the minimum characteristic timescales, we can estimate upper bounds to M_ . In the case of the Kerr black holes, M_ 10^ 8.91 M_ and M_ 10^ 9.02 M_ are given for S5 0716+714 and 3C 273, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper combines GRAVITY spectroastrometry and reverberation mapping of 3C 273 to infer its BLR geometry, angular-distance measurement, Hubble-constant estimate, and black-hole mass potential.

quotes.quote: 3C 273, the first quasar discovered , has a redshift of z=0.158 and a K band magnitude K 10.0 (both from the NASA Extragalactic Database). GRAVITY successfully measured the differential phase curves ( _ * ) of 3C 273 in July 2017, January, March, and May 2018 . The spectral resolution of GRAVITY is / 500 , which is good enough to constrain some parameters of the BLR from the observed Paschen (hereafter Pa ) line.

The present joint analysis generates an angular distance of D_ A =551.5_ 78.7 ^ +97.3 Mpc with a relative statistical error of | D_ A |/D_ A 0.16 on average. This is a very encouraging accuracy for the joint analysis of the first SARM data, demonstrating the power of the present analysis as a feasible tool for measuring extragalactic distances. The SARM measurements as a geometrical method avoid various calibrations and corrections used in the popular measurements through Cepheid variable stars and type Ia supernovae (SNIa) , such as extinction corrections in both tools, necessary calibrations through the standardization and cosmic ladders in the latter.

We can measure the Hubble constant H_ 0 through the SARM-based distance of 3C 273. Employing the z-D_ A relation , we have H_ 0 =71.5_ 10.6 ^ +11.9 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 for a cosmology of _ M =0.315 and _ =0.685 determined by the Planck CMB measurements (but H_ 0 very weakly depends on _ M and _ for the current case).

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 273 as a blazar with renewed 2015-2019 high-energy activity, connecting outbursts and millimeter flares with photon-index changes, radio lag, VLBA jet features, and a parsec-scale jet origin.

quotes.quote: Due to its powerful radiation over the entire electromagnetic spectrum and its radio jet activity, the blazar 3C 273 offers the opportunity to study the physics of ray emission from active galactic nuclei. Since a historically strong outburst in 2009, 3C 273 showed relatively weak emission in the ray band over multiple years.

We find two prominent ray outbursts in 2016 (MJD 57382) and 2017 (MJD 57883) accompanied by mm-wavelength flaring activity. The ray photon index time series show a weak hump-like feature around the ray outbursts. The monthly ray flux--index plot indicates a transition from softer-when-brighter to harder-when-brighter at 1.03 10^ 7 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . A significant correlation between the ray and mm-wavelength emission is found, with the radio lagging the rays by about 105--112 days. The 43 GHz jet images reveal the known stationary features (i.e., the core, S1 , and S2 ) in a region upstream of the jet.
Our results support a parsec-scale origin for the observed ray elevated activity, suggesting association with standing shocks in the jet.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 273's orphan gamma-ray flare, measuring a roughly one-day variability timescale and fitting broadband emission with a two-region leptonic scenario.

quotes.quote: We study the temporal and spectral profile of these Orphan ray flares in detail from three ray bright blazars, 3C 273, PKS 1510-089 and 3C 279 and also their simultaneous broadband emissions. We find that the variability timescales of the Orphan ray flares were ( 0.96 0.28 ) days, ( 3.12 2.40 ) hr and ( 2.16 0.72 ) hr, for 3C 273, PKS 1510-089 and 3C 279, respectively. The broadband spectral energy distributions (SEDs) during these flares have been modelled with a leptonic model from two emission regions. This model suggests that Orphan ray flares might have originated from inverse Compton scattering of relativistic electrons by the seed photons from the broad-line region or dusty torus, which is the first region.

The multi-waveband light curves (MWLC) of 3C 273, PKS 1510-089 and 3C 279 help in the clear identification of Orphan ray flares. We examine the temporal evolutions of these Orphan ray flares by using shorter time bin light curves of these sources and modelling their flare profiles. The details of the MWLC and results of ray flare modelling are discussed in this section. Multi-waveband light curves 3C 273 The source exhibited the Orphan ray flare with a daily average peak flux of (4.32 0.45) 10 ^ 6 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 on MJD 55275 0.5.

sources.summary: The paper compares simulations to 3C 273, describing it as a well-monitored FSRQ whose jet shows fast moving and quasi-stationary radio knots during low-activity states.

quotes.quote: In order to compare the results of our simulations with an astrophysical case, we focus on the radio observations of one of the brightest and best monitored FSRQs over decades, 3C 273 (B1226+023). Its redshift of z = 0.1583 Strauss_1992 translates into a scaling factor of 2.73 pc/mas considering H_0 = 70 km.s ^ 1 .Mpc ^ 1 . 3C 273 displays a peculiar mix of fast moving and quasi-stationary radio knots. This hybrid radio kinematic behavior is most often observed in intermediate blazars (Low or Intermediate frequency-peaked BL Lacs) Hervet_2016 . However 3C 273 significantly differs from these sources in the way that quasi-stationary knots are visible only during low-activity periods of the jet, not continuously.

In Figure fig: 3C273 , we see that quasi-stationary knots appear during a specifically long quiescent state of the source of 2 years. This low activity period is marked by the long decrease of the radio flux seen by OVRO starting from 2011 up to 2013, and also corresponds to an absence of radio ejecta. This observation suggests that the radio-jet of 3C 273 presents at least two quasi-stationary knots (k32, k35) in its nominal (quiescent, non-perturbed) state.

sources.summary: The paper compares 3C 273's X-ray flare intervals with VLBA superluminal-knot ejections, uses radio and SED constraints on Doppler boosting, and notes roughly 16-year jet precession.

quotes.quote: We therefore compared the time intervals between X-ray flares for 3C 273, with radio observations of superluminal knots ejection times, observed on a parsec scale by VLBA at 43 GHz . Keeping in mind that the knots ejection rate varies in time , we considered only the knots ejected in the time range of the analyzed Swift XRT light curves. The distribution of t for 3C 273 is shown in Figure fig.dt_obs and one can note a double peak structure, similar to that present in the t distribution for BL Lacs, where the distribution for radio knots is consistent with Swift XRT.

3C 273 3C 273 featuring an extended jet showing superluminal motion, and thus for this source, can be estimated from apparent speed _ app of radio blobs. Polarimetric measurements of the radio knots with VLBA lead to = 9 1.4 , which is also consistent with estimated from variability timescale of the superluminal jet components observed in radio . Later observations of the radio knots on a parsec scale from the center showed _ app in a wider range from 5 to 15 . Consistently with that, averaged multiwavelength SED was successfully modeled by one-zone leptonic SSC scenario, resulting in 14 . Note that the precession of the 3C 273 jet was observed with period of about 16 yrs, but the accompanying variations seem to fall in the range of _ app observed in radio .

sources.summary: The paper detects extended millimeter emission in 3C273's host galaxy, rejects star formation as its source, and interprets it as thermal bremsstrahlung from gas ionized by the central quasar.

quotes.quote: We detect spatially extended millimeter emission associated with the host galaxy, cospatial with the Extended Emission Line Region (EELR) observed in the optical. The millimeter spectral energy distribution and comparison with centimeter data show that the extended emission cannot be explained by dust thermal emission, synchrotron or thermal bremsstrahlung arising from massive star formation. We interpret the extended millimeter emission as thermal bremsstrahlung from gas directly ionized by the central source. The extended flux indicates that at least 7 of the bolometric flux of the nuclear source was used to ionize atomic hydrogen in the host galaxy.

The stellar mass of the host elliptical galaxy of 3C273 is estimated to be M_* 10^ 11.1 \ M_ with an average stellar population of 2.5 Gyr zhang19 . For nearby star forming galaxies on the main sequence, the expected SFR at this stellar mass is only a few 10 \ M_ yr^ 1 peng10 . Thus, if the observed millimeter flux is due to synchrotron emission from massive star formation, the host of 3C273 must be an extreme starburst, but such a bursty phase contradicts with the relatively old stellar population. In addition, the molecular gas mass in 3C273 estimated from ^ 12 CO (J=1-0) emission is only 10^9 \ M_ husemann19b , which is comparable to local spirals which have SFR of only few M_ yr^ 1 . We therefore conclude that synchrotron originating from massive star formation in the host galaxy is not the source of extended millimeter emission in 3C273.

sources.summary: The paper reports negative optical and gamma-ray periodicity results for 3C 273, with apparent 6-8.2 year peaks attributed to red noise and radio hints not significant after simulation.

quotes.quote: 3C 273 The analysis reports a negative result for the optical R- and V-band LCs, as well as the Fermi LAT ray data set. A peak in both the LS periodogram and the WWZ appears in all three data sets with values between 6.0 years and 8.2 years, however not statistically significant and most likely caused by the influence of the red noise, as derived from the analysis (see Figure 3c273_results ). Moreover, the almost unpolarized emission presented by this blazar also leads to no significant period detected in its polarization degree. report a period of 13.65 years in the optical B-band emission of this blazar in a 110 year LC. However, due to the much shorter coverage of our data sample, we cannot aim to prove such a long period.

On the other hand, the radio LC presents a peak above 2 in both of the methods used in the first analysis step (PDF and REDFIT) at a period of 3 years. However, this period does not appear significant after a careful treatment of the red noise and the variability through the LC simulation used for the analysis. In addition, report a period in the radio emission of 3C 273 of 8.55 years.

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 273's far-infrared emission varied between IRAS and ISO epochs, with synchrotron dominating high states but a thermal dust bump appearing during low synchrotron activity.

quotes.quote: In 1983, at the epoch of the IRAS observations, the mid- to far-infrared SED of 3C 273 was entirely dominated by synchrotron emission. However, at the time of the ISO measurments in July 1996 the 60 to 100 m fluxes of 3C 273 were about 50 lower than measured by IRAS. This low synchrotron activity is consistent with the results of monitoring the source at other wavelengths (see Fig.1 in Stevens et al. 1998 and 1.3 and 3.3 mm monitoring with IRAM by Hans Ungerechts, private communication). The variability alone indicates that the FIR flux of 3C 273 contains a considerable synchrotron contribution, at least during the phases of high flux, like in 1983. Using the mm fluxes in 1996 and a synchrotron spectrum ^ 1.1 as reference (see Fig. 5), we find that only at the longest wavelengths (170, 180 m) the ISO measurements are consistent with a pure synchrotron power-law, while the flux values between 60 and 120 m straddle significantly above that power-law.

Fortunately, the low synchrotron activity of 3C 273 at the time of the ISO observations enabled the detection of a thermal contribution to the FIR spectrum. In fact, the ISO FIR spectrum between 60 and 100 m with values around 1100 and 1300 mJy lies well above the extrapolation of the mm-submm spectrum towards the IR: F _ = 354 mJy ( / _ 60 )^ 1.1 (dashed line in Fig. 5). We take this excess as an indication of the presence of a thermal bump in 3C 273 which contributes roughly 500 mJy at both 60 and 100 m.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 273 as a blazar with strong emission lines, a MeV bump, high inferred metallicity, superluminal motion, and a predicted Doppler-shifted K line detectable by future instruments.

quotes.quote: 3C 273 is a typical blazar with strong emission lines, powerful continuum emission from radio to rays (see an extensive review of Courvoisier 1998). A very strong MeV bump has been discovered in 3C 273 by the Compton Gamma-ray Observatory (Lichti et al. 1995). This MeV feature could be explained by several different models, for example, a break in the electron injection function (Ghisellini et al. 1996), incomplete cooling of relativistic electrons (Sikora et al. 1994); and pair cascade process (Blandford Levinson 1995). However, in such models, one encounters other difficulties to reconcile the broad band multi-wavelength continuum emission [ see a brief review of Sikora et al. (1997) ] .

The observed flux ratio of N v / C iv is 0.46 in 3C 273 (Baldwin et al. 1989), which gives a metal abundance A =Z/Z_ 10 from Fig. 6 of Hamann Ferland (1999). The superluminal motion has been extensively studied, the latest observation shows that the apparent velocities for different components are from 9 to 22 (Jorstad et al. 2001). Here, we take 10 . For the parameters of 3C 273, we estimate a K line luminosity L_ K ^ obs 3.0 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 and the flux F_ K =3.9 10^ 12 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 .
